#### SA-B4 — Crimson Reach Black Market auctions

**Status**: done
**Phase**: Phase III | **Size**: L | **Effort**: 1.5 weeks
**Depends on**: SA-B2, SA-1 | **Blocks**: SA-B6

**Goal.** Second Bidding venue. Layer Reach-specific content and integrations on top of SA-B2's venue-agnostic engine and SA-B3's content-and-integration patterns. Author 12-16 candidate lots across the 4 design-doc-locked Reach categories (`contraband`, `restricted_weapon`, `salvage_lot`, `faction_commodity`) with Wreckers' Guild tier coverage and 1-2 headliners; register a NEW Reach Floor Manager NPC with a net-new voice sheet whose register is impatient, declarative, and "no questions asked" (the antithesis of Velo's ceremonial cadence); ship demand-driven session cadence (lots accumulate game-day-by-game-day until the venue fires); gate venue entry on Wreckers' Guild membership tier (per SA-1's `WRECKERS_GUILD_CONFIG`); apply faction-rep legality consequences when the player wins `contraband` or `restricted_weapon` lots; render the post-session social phase with Salko's quiet read + ambient Reach buyers + Floor Manager close; and tint the auction view with ReachDarkLayout's red-on-dim accent palette when the venue is `crimson_reach`. Inherit SA-B3's voice-data-as-JSON pattern, NPC pattern, and hub-navigation pattern; do NOT modify SA-B3's Stellaris artifacts.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/sa_bidding_design.md` (sections 1.4 Wreckers' Guild tier gating, 2 lifecycle, 3 lot schema + worked example 3 Reach contraband, 4.5 Reach Flavor + Salko persona specs, 5 player input model, 6 Captain Memory at Reach, 7 crew/skill bonuses (venue-agnostic), 9.4 + 9.5 banter flags + achievement stubs, 11 locked decisions; §10.6 Reach floor manager voice deferred to SA-B4; §12 hand-off table)
- `requirements/agent_principles.md`
- `requirements/aurelia_voice_examples.md` (16-item diagnostic checklist; Reach-rhythm cadence per agent_principles.md line 73; required reading before authoring Floor Manager voice + Reach Buyer ambient lines)
- `requirements/character_voices.md` (Captain Fenn Salko lines 1368-1410; Sable Trent lines 1457-1503; Malia Torres lines 372-411 for Reach register reference; SA-PREP-1 NPC inventory + speaker_id registry lines 575-641 — note `reach_floor_manager` is net-new and SA-B4 owns its sheet authoring)
- `requirements/cultural_guide.md` (Crimson Reach culture; Wreckers' Guild origin; "no laws, no mercy, no refunds" tagline that ReachDarkLayout already surfaces)
- `requirements/station_anchors.md` (Phase III SA-B4 paragraph at line 150; Bidding integration commitments at 154-160)
- `requirements/onboarding_design.md` (PT-M `FirstTimeTipOverlay` pattern; auction tip is shared with Stellaris via `seen_auction_first_session_tip`)
- `requirements/si3_flag_registry_cookbook.md` (cross-module flag helper conventions for new Reach flags)
- `data/galaxy/locations.json` (lines 436-480 — Crimson Reach system; `crimson_market` is the Wrecker's Bazaar marketplace and stays as-is; SA-B4 ADDS a new `unique` location `crimson_black_market`)
- `data/galaxy/systems.json` (lines 231-262 `crimson_reach` system; faction id is `crimson_reach`)
- `data/auctions/stellaris_lots.json` + `data/auctions/stellaris_voices.json` (read-only — SA-B3's authored content is the schema model SA-B4 mirrors with the Reach venue files; do NOT modify)
- `spacegame/models/bidding.py` (`AuctionState.enter_preview` / `is_session_due` / `schedule_session`; `_close_session` Stellaris-cadence reschedule note at line 949-956 which SA-B4 extends with Reach demand-driven path; `generate_lot_pool` line 447; `_tier_distance` ladder line 534-550 — Stellaris-specific; SA-B4 must extend per locked decision §B4.3; `REACH_SESSION_SIZE = 4` line 78; `RECENTLY_SEEN_EXCLUSION = 5`)
- `spacegame/models/bidding_lot.py` (`LOT_CATEGORY_*` constants — categories `contraband`, `restricted_weapon`, `salvage_lot`, `faction_commodity` are the 4 Reach-locked categories per design §1.3; `VENUE_CRIMSON_REACH = "crimson_reach"`)
- `spacegame/models/bidding_persona.py` (`make_reach_flavor(instance_index)` line 359; `make_salko()` line 280-326; `PERSONA_REACH_FLAVOR = "reach_buyer"`; `NAMED_RIVAL_IDS` does NOT change — SA-B4 ships zero new named rivals per locked decision §B4.7)
- `spacegame/models/wreckers_guild.py` (`WRECKERS_GUILD_CONFIG` line 54-65; tiers `unjoined` / `apprentice` / `journeyman` / `master`; SA-B4 reuses the canonical tier ids verbatim per locked decision §B4.2)
- `spacegame/models/player.py` (`sub_reputation` dict; `get_sub_reputation_tier(org_id)`; `is_at_least_tier(org_id, tier_id)`; `modify_reputation("stellaris_commerce_guild", delta)` for the legality-consequence path)
- `spacegame/views/auction_view.py` (lines 220-232 `set_active_personas` / `set_voice_templates`; lines 677-721 PREVIEW empty-state with `empty_state` template; lines 773-789 `_velo_running_commentary` reads `velo_lines` key — SA-B4 adds `auctioneer_lines` alias per locked decision §B4.6; lines 852-924 POST_SESSION social UI; lines 204-208 `AnimatedBackground("trade_routes")` — SA-B4 wraps with venue-specific dim tint when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`)
- `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py` (lines 97-103 `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS`; lines 1127-1175 unique-card detail panel rendering; SA-1 `WreckersGuildView`'s tier-gate dialogue pattern is the navigation reference for tier gating)
- `spacegame/views/station_layouts.py` (lines 968-1047 `ReachDarkLayout`; `accent_color = (180, 50, 40)`; `bg_tint = (20, 5, 5, 15)`; `faction_tagline = "No laws. No mercy. No refunds."`)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` (lines 2317-2416 `_ensure_auction_view`; lines 2418-2536 `_prepare_stellaris_session` — SA-B4 adds a sibling `_prepare_reach_session`; line 1942 hub-entry dispatch where SA-B4 routes `crimson_black_market` to the Reach session prepare)
- `spacegame/data_loader.py` (lines 1195-1264 `load_auction_lots` + `load_auction_voices` are ALREADY wired to read `crimson_reach_lots.json` and `crimson_reach_voices.json` — SA-B4 just authors the data files and the loader picks them up)
- `spacegame/constants/flags.py` (`auction_first_session_complete`, `auction_first_win`, `auction_rival_encountered`, `auction_sable_ceiling_correct`; SA-B4 ADDS `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter`, `auction_first_reach_session`, `auction_first_contraband_win`)
- `data/journal/entries.json` (existing 4 auction entries SA-B2 + SA-B3 ship; SA-B4 adds 3 entries)
- `data/characters/npcs.json` (NPC schema; pattern from `cassian_velo` SA-B3 entry is the model)

**Touch zones.**
- `data/auctions/crimson_reach_lots.json` (NEW — Reach candidate lot catalog, 12-16 lots)
- `data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json` (NEW — Floor Manager auctioneer line templates, Salko flat-bid template (already authored by SA-B3 in `stellaris_voices.json`; SA-B4 mirrors the entry verbatim so Reach reads from its own file), Reach Buyer ambient line templates, post-session social lines, empty-state Floor Manager flavor, retired-rival aside)
- `data/galaxy/locations.json` (ADD a NEW `unique` location `crimson_black_market` to the `crimson_reach` system list at lines 436-480; `crimson_market` (Wrecker's Bazaar) stays unchanged)
- `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` (NEW Floor Manager off-floor dialogue tree; first-encounter dialogue; tier-locked-entry dialogue surfaced from station hub when player is `unjoined`)
- `data/characters/npcs.json` (NEW `reach_floor_manager` NPC entry — proposed canonical name `Vex Tarn`, title "Reach Floor Manager", `home_system_id="crimson_reach"`, `dialogue_id="reach_floor_manager_main"`)
- `data/journal/entries.json` (3 NEW auto-entries: `auto_auction_first_reach_session` keyed on `auction_first_reach_session`; `auto_auction_first_floor_manager_encounter` keyed on `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter`; `auto_auction_first_contraband_lesson` keyed on `auction_first_contraband_win`)
- `requirements/character_voices.md` (NEW section "Vex Tarn — Reach Floor Manager" authoring the net-new voice sheet per SA-PREP-1 NPC inventory line 641's gap; one-line update to the speaker_id registry table marking `reach_floor_manager` as registered for SA-B4)
- `spacegame/models/bidding.py` (extend `_tier_distance` to a venue-aware helper or wrap with `tier_ladder_for_venue(venue_id) -> tuple[str, ...]`; new module-level helpers `reach_session_due(state, current_day) -> bool`, `reach_advance_demand(state, current_day) -> int` (returns new pending count), `wreckers_tier_for_membership(player) -> str` (delegates to `Player.get_sub_reputation_tier("wreckers_guild")` so Reach pool generation reads the right ladder); module-level Reach demand constants `REACH_DEMAND_PROBABILITY = 0.35`, `REACH_DEMAND_MAX_GAP_DAYS = 8`)
- `spacegame/models/bidding.py` (extend `_close_session` so `active_auction_id == "crimson_reach"` reschedules via demand-reset rather than 5-7 day calendar; existing Stellaris path unchanged)
- `spacegame/views/auction_view.py` (read `auctioneer_lines` voice key first, fall back to `velo_lines` for SA-B3 compatibility — minimal alias change in `_velo_running_commentary` and `_format_rival_bid_line` is unaffected; apply Reach dim tint when `self.venue_id == "crimson_reach"` (`bg_dim` alpha goes to 180; accent color drives PREVIEW header underline + post-session list bullet))
- `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py` (add `"crimson_black_market": GameState.AUCTION` to `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS`; in the unique-detail panel, suppress the "Enter" button when the location is `crimson_black_market` AND the player's Wreckers' Guild tier is `unjoined`; instead render a tier-locked message line "The floor is for members. Talk to the Guild first." pulling from `crimson_reach_voices.json`'s `tier_locked` template — wire the suppression check via `Player.get_sub_reputation_tier("wreckers_guild")` so SA-1 stays the source of truth)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` (NEW `_prepare_reach_session` mirroring `_prepare_stellaris_session`; on hub entry to `crimson_black_market` it advances Reach demand for any elapsed game-days, checks `reach_session_due`, generates the lot pool with the Wreckers' Guild tier, hands the personas (Salko + 3 reach_buyer_N) and voice templates to AuctionView; legality-consequence handler `_apply_reach_legality_penalty(lot)` registered as a venue-specific extension of `on_lot_won` — fires `Player.modify_reputation("stellaris_commerce_guild", -2)` for `contraband` lots, `-1` for `restricted_weapon`, `0` otherwise)
- `spacegame/constants/flags.py` (ADD `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter()`, `auction_first_reach_session()`, `auction_first_contraband_win()` per SI-3 cookbook conventions)
- `tests/test_models/test_bidding_reach.py` (NEW — Wreckers' tier ladder, demand-driven cadence determinism, Reach lot catalog data integrity, lot-pool determinism with locked seed, legality-consequence math, headliner cap at Reach)
- `tests/test_models/test_data_loader_auctions.py` (EXTEND with a `TestReachAuctionFiles` test class; do NOT modify SA-B3's tests — purely additive)
- `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py` (NEW — Reach dim tint applied when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`; PREVIEW empty-state Floor Manager line; POST_SESSION rendering with Salko-only attendance; auctioneer_lines key alias resolves to Floor Manager content)
- `tests/test_views/test_station_hub_view.py` (EXTEND if it exists; otherwise add minimal targeted test in the auction-view test file — covers the tier-locked Enter-button suppression for `unjoined` players AND the Enter-button render for `apprentice`/`journeyman`/`master` players; do NOT modify SA-1's wreckers-guild station-hub tests)
- `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_reach_blackmarket.py` (NEW — full Reach session end-to-end: hub-entry as journeyman → demand-driven preview → 4-lot session with Salko + 3 reach buyers → 1 contraband win → legality penalty fires → post-session social → close → next-session demand reset)
- `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py` (NEW — Writing Bible scan over `crimson_reach_lots.json` + `crimson_reach_voices.json` + Floor Manager dialogue tree + 3 new journal entries; voice-distinct test asserting Floor Manager templates do NOT match Velo's ceremonial cadence (sentence-length distribution + ceremonial-keyword scan))

**Deliverables.**
- **Reach lot catalog** — 12-16 candidate lots in `data/auctions/crimson_reach_lots.json`; exclusively `venue="crimson_reach"`; exclusively from the 4 Reach-locked categories (`contraband`, `restricted_weapon`, `salvage_lot`, `faction_commodity`); covers the 3 active Wreckers' Guild tiers (`apprentice` / `journeyman` / `master`); 1-2 lots flagged `is_headliner=True`; zero `season_tag` entries (Reach ships unconditional weighting per locked decision §B4.11); each lot's headline + description voice-checked against the Reach-rhythm register (per `agent_principles.md` line 73) and the `aurelia_voice_examples.md` 16-item diagnostic.
- **Reach voice file** — `data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json` containing: 6 Floor Manager auctioneer line templates under the `auctioneer_lines` key (lot-open, "we are at X", lot-closed-sold, lot-closed-withdrawn, session-open, session-close); 1 rival flat-bid template (Salko, identical to the SA-B3 Stellaris entry — Reach reads its own file so the Stellaris file stays untouched); 8 post-session social lines (4 outcome buckets for Salko: rival-won-vs-player, player-won-vs-rival, rival-bid-no-overlap, rival-absent; 4 ambient Reach Buyer comment options post-session); 4 Sable post-session read templates (mirrors the SA-B3 schema; Sable's read at the Reach is sharper, less procedural — register reflects "she sees the room differently here"); 1 PREVIEW empty-state Floor Manager flavor line; 1 retired-rival aside template; 1 `tier_locked` template surfaced at the station hub for `unjoined` players ("The floor is for members. Talk to the Guild first.").
- **Vex Tarn (Reach Floor Manager) NPC + voice sheet + dialogue tree** — net-new section in `requirements/character_voices.md` authoring the voice sheet (tonal register: impatient, declarative, "no questions asked"; verbal habits: terse, no honorifics, accepts the lot for what it is, ends conversations rather than wrapping them; what he never says: ceremonial cadence, lengthy preamble, anything that sounds like Velo's "we are at X" register taken straight); NPC entry in `data/characters/npcs.json` (id `reach_floor_manager`, name "Vex Tarn", title "Reach Floor Manager", `home_system_id="crimson_reach"`, `dialogue_id="reach_floor_manager_main"`); off-floor dialogue tree in `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` with at least 4 branches (first-encounter — sets `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter` and triggers the journal entry; demand-cadence info "next session when there's enough on the table"; tier-locked variant for `unjoined` callers; generic exit). One-line update to the speaker_id registry table at `character_voices.md:641` marking `reach_floor_manager` as registered for SA-B4.
- **Reach venue location** — NEW entry in `data/galaxy/locations.json` under the `crimson_reach` system: `id: "crimson_black_market"`, `name: "The Reach Floor"`, `location_type: "unique"`, `description` written in Reach register (terse, specific, no "couldn't help but" or "a testament to"), `flavor_text` short and declarative. The existing `crimson_market` (Wrecker's Bazaar marketplace) is NOT modified — separate concern.
- **Wreckers' Guild tier gating** — `wreckers_tier_for_membership(player)` reads `Player.get_sub_reputation_tier("wreckers_guild")` (canonical SA-1 source). `unjoined` players never see the Enter button at the station hub; `apprentice` players see only `apprentice`-tier-required lots; `journeyman` adds `journeyman`-tier lots; `master` adds `master`-tier lots. Pool generation reads the tier and applies the existing `generate_lot_pool` filter — tier ladder dispatch via `tier_ladder_for_venue("crimson_reach") -> ("none", "apprentice", "journeyman", "master")` extends `_tier_distance` so the rep-tier weight multiplier works correctly at Reach.
- **Demand-driven cadence** — `reach_session_due(state, current_day)` returns True when (a) Reach pending lots accumulated to `REACH_SESSION_SIZE=4` OR (b) `REACH_DEMAND_MAX_GAP_DAYS=8` game-days have elapsed since `last_auction_day["crimson_reach"]`. `reach_advance_demand(state, current_day)` is called on hub entry and at game-day-advance hooks; for each elapsed game-day it draws a deterministic `_seeded_unit_from_token(f"{day}_reach_arrivals") < REACH_DEMAND_PROBABILITY=0.35` and increments a counter when true. Session counter resets to 0 on `_close_session` for the Reach venue. `last_auction_day["crimson_reach"]` and `next_auction_day` semantics: SA-B4 stops setting `next_auction_day` for Reach (demand-driven, not calendar) and instead writes a transient `_reach_pending_arrivals` value into `state.next_auction_day["crimson_reach_pending"]` to keep the existing AuctionState schema unchanged. Save/load: existing `next_auction_day` dict round-trips the new key cleanly; no schema bump.
- **Persona pool at Reach** — Salko attends every Reach session the player attends (per design doc §4.5 player_target_escalation; mirrors his Stellaris attendance rule). 3 Reach Buyer ambient personas drawn per session via `make_reach_flavor(instance_index=N)` for N in (1, 2, 3). NO Prentiss, NO Kade, NO new named rival — locked decision §B4.7. Salko's existing `make_salko()` factory is venue-agnostic; Reach's `desire_multipliers` for `contraband`/`restricted_weapon`/`salvage_lot` pick up automatically because Salko's `desire_multipliers` table at lines 280-322 covers all 8 categories.
- **Faction-rep legality consequences** — Winning a `contraband` lot fires `Player.modify_reputation("stellaris_commerce_guild", -2)`. Winning a `restricted_weapon` lot fires `-1`. Other categories: 0 (no change). Wired in `engine/game.py` via a venue-specific extension to `on_lot_won` — when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"` AND `lot.category in {contraband, restricted_weapon}`, apply the penalty before the existing `auction_first_win` flag set. The penalty surface uses the existing `_pending_faction_deltas` notification queue (per CLAUDE.md "Common Pitfalls" pattern) so the player sees the rep change feedback in the next frame's hub UI.
- **Reach-themed dim UI** — When `AuctionView.venue_id == "crimson_reach"`, `_bg_dim.set_alpha(180)` (vs. 150 for Stellaris); PREVIEW header underline color + post-session bullet color = ReachDarkLayout's `accent_color = (180, 50, 40)`. The change reads ReachDarkLayout's color constants directly so future palette refinements at the layout layer flow through.
- **Auctioneer-lines voice key alias** — In `_velo_running_commentary`, read `self._voice_templates.get("auctioneer_lines")` first; fall back to `velo_lines` if the auctioneer_lines key is absent. SA-B3's existing `velo_lines` key in `stellaris_voices.json` continues to work via the fallback. SA-B4's `crimson_reach_voices.json` uses the venue-neutral `auctioneer_lines` key. Minimal engine touch; backward-compatible.
- **Hub navigation + tier gating** — `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS["crimson_black_market"] = GameState.AUCTION`. The unique-detail panel for `crimson_black_market` shows the Enter button when player tier is at least `apprentice`; suppresses the button and shows a tier-locked message ("The floor is for members. Talk to the Guild first.") when player is `unjoined`. The talk-to-Floor-Manager affordance surfaces alongside Enter when the player is at least apprentice; for `unjoined` players the Floor Manager dialogue still surfaces with the tier-locked branch (so `unjoined` players hear Vex turn them away in his voice, not a generic UI string).
- **Polish fold-in #1 — 3 flag-gated journal entries.** `auto_auction_first_reach_session` (first Reach session entered: terse, in-fiction, no narrator gloss); `auto_auction_first_floor_manager_encounter` (first Floor Manager dialogue: registers Vex as a recurring presence); `auto_auction_first_contraband_lesson` (first contraband win + rep loss: short journal entry that names the rep change concretely without moralizing). All three voice-checked against the Writing Bible scanner AND the 16-item diagnostic. Trigger flags wired through `spacegame/constants/flags.py` per SI-3 cookbook.
- **Polish fold-in #2 — Banter trigger flags.** `auction_first_reach_session` and `auction_first_contraband_win` registered in `spacegame/constants/flags.py`. SA-X6 (crew banter) consumes these flags later; SA-B4 just sets them at the right moments.
- **Polish fold-in #3 — Achievement stub.** `achievement_auction_reach_debut` registered (per design doc §9.5; SA-B4 owns this stub per the SA-B2 hand-off note). Unlock condition: win first lot at the Reach venue. SA-X7 authors the achievement metadata + display content.
- **News-ticker integration** — Already wired by SA-B3's `_ensure_auction_view` callbacks; the existing `on_headliner_sold` and `on_headliner_withdrawn` handlers use `venue_display` and produce correct Reach text automatically when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`. No SA-B4 changes needed for the news ticker.
- **First-time tip overlay** — Shared with Stellaris via the existing `seen_auction_first_session_tip` flag. SA-B4 does NOT add a new tip overlay. Reach-specific affordances (legality risk, demand cadence, tier-locked entry) are taught via Floor Manager dialogue + the 3 new journal entries. Rationale: avoids tip-overlay-soup; SA-X3 may refine.
- **Tests** — 4 NEW test files + 1 extension (`test_data_loader_auctions.py` adds a `TestReachAuctionFiles` class; SA-B3's classes untouched), ~25-35 new test functions covering tier ladder dispatch, demand-cadence determinism, lot catalog integrity, legality-consequence math, dim-tint application, voice-bible compliance, full scenario end-to-end, tier-locked hub-entry suppression.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. Player docks at Crimson Reach and the station hub renders the new `crimson_black_market` (`The Reach Floor`) `unique` card. `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS["crimson_black_market"] = GameState.AUCTION`. Verified by `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py`.
2. Tier-gated entry: when `Player.get_sub_reputation_tier("wreckers_guild") == "unjoined"`, the unique-detail panel does NOT render the Enter button; instead it surfaces the tier-locked message line read from `crimson_reach_voices.json`'s `tier_locked` template. The "Talk to Floor Manager" affordance still surfaces for `unjoined` players and routes to the tier-locked dialogue branch. For `apprentice` / `journeyman` / `master` players, the Enter button renders and routes to `GameState.AUCTION` with `venue_id="crimson_reach"`.
3. `tier_ladder_for_venue("crimson_reach") == ("none", "apprentice", "journeyman", "master")`; `tier_ladder_for_venue("stellaris") == ("none", "apprentice", "regular", "certified", "patron")`. `_tier_distance` dispatches per venue. Pool generation respects the resulting tier — a `journeyman` player draws no `master`-required lots; weight multiplier `+0.3` per tier above min applies as documented.
4. Reach lot catalog (`data/auctions/crimson_reach_lots.json`) contains 12 to 16 lots, exclusively `venue="crimson_reach"`, exclusively from the 4 design-doc-locked Reach categories (`contraband`, `restricted_weapon`, `salvage_lot`, `faction_commodity`). At least 1 lot per category. At least 1 and at most 2 lots have `is_headliner=True`. Zero lots have a non-null `season_tag`. All `source_module_id` references (where category is `module` — should be zero for Reach since `module` is not a Reach category) resolve in `data/ships/modules.json`; SA-B4 expects no module-category lots.
5. Reach voice file (`data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json`) loads via the existing data-loader path; structure validates against the locked schema (6 Floor Manager templates under `auctioneer_lines`, 1 Salko template under `rival_bids`, 4×2 post-session lines under `post_session.salko` + 4 ambient Reach Buyer post-session options under `post_session.reach_buyer`, 4 Sable templates under `sable_reads`, 1 `empty_state` line, 1 `retired_rival` template, 1 `tier_locked` template). Missing file → loader logs warning and returns empty dict; auction view falls back to design-doc-default strings without crashing.
6. Vex Tarn (`reach_floor_manager`) registered in `data/characters/npcs.json` and `requirements/character_voices.md` (NEW voice sheet section); off-floor dialogue tree in `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` contains at least 4 branches (first-encounter, demand-cadence info, tier-locked variant, exit); first-encounter sets `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter` flag and triggers `auto_auction_first_floor_manager_encounter` journal entry; the speaker_id registry at `character_voices.md:641` is updated to mark `reach_floor_manager` registered for SA-B4.
7. Voice-register distinctness: a static text scan across `crimson_reach_voices.json`'s `auctioneer_lines` (Floor Manager) AND the Floor Manager dialogue tree shows ZERO ceremonial cadence keywords ("ladies and gentlemen," "we are at," "the lot is open"); average sentence length ≤ 10 words; zero greeting-with-honorific lines; the test asserts the Floor Manager set does NOT match Velo's `stellaris_voices.json` `velo_lines` set on these dimensions. Verified by `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py::test_floor_manager_distinct_from_velo`.
8. Demand-driven cadence: `reach_session_due(state, current_day)` returns True iff (pending arrivals counter `>= REACH_SESSION_SIZE=4`) OR (`current_day - state.last_auction_day["crimson_reach"] >= REACH_DEMAND_MAX_GAP_DAYS=8`). `reach_advance_demand` is deterministic per game-day (seeded `f"{day}_reach_arrivals"`); reload at the same day produces the same counter. Counter resets on `_close_session` for the Reach venue. Verified by parametric test across 30 simulated game-days with locked seed (asserts the average gap between fired sessions falls within the `[4 lots / 0.35 prob, 8-day cap]` range).
9. Persona pool: Salko attends every Reach session the player attends (PERSONA_SALKO is in `session_personas` after `enter_preview` for every Reach session). 3 `reach_buyer_N` instances (N=1..3) drawn per session via `make_reach_flavor`. PERSONA_PRENTISS and PERSONA_KADE are NOT in `session_personas` for any Reach session (verified by 10-session deterministic test). NAMED_RIVAL_IDS does NOT change.
10. Legality consequences: winning a `contraband` lot fires `Player.modify_reputation("stellaris_commerce_guild", -2)`; winning a `restricted_weapon` lot fires `-1`; winning a `salvage_lot` lot fires `0`; winning a `faction_commodity` lot fires `0`. Verified by parametric test across all 4 categories. Penalty applies before `auction_first_win` flag is set so the journal entry sees the post-penalty rep value. Penalty respects the standard rep clamp (-100..100) and uses `_pending_faction_deltas` for the player-feedback queue.
11. Reach UI dim tint: when `AuctionView.venue_id == "crimson_reach"`, `_bg_dim.get_alpha() == 180`; PREVIEW header underline + post-session bullet render in `(180, 50, 40)`. When `venue_id == "stellaris"`, the previously-shipping behavior is preserved exactly (`_bg_dim` alpha 150; default colors). Verified by view test with both venue_id values.
12. Auctioneer-lines key alias: `_velo_running_commentary` reads `self._voice_templates.get("auctioneer_lines")` first; falls back to `velo_lines` if absent. With SA-B3's `stellaris_voices.json` (which uses `velo_lines`), Velo's lines render unchanged. With SA-B4's `crimson_reach_voices.json` (which uses `auctioneer_lines`), Floor Manager lines render. Verified by view test with both files.
13. PREVIEW empty-state at Reach: when no Reach session is due, `_render_body_preview` shows the Floor Manager flavor template; no countdown line (Reach is demand-driven, not calendar). Falls back to a tight default if the voice file is absent.
14. POST_SESSION social UI at Reach: renders Salko's outcome-bucket line per session; does not render Prentiss / Kade lines (they did not attend); renders 1 ambient Reach Buyer comment from the 4-option pool (deterministic by session id); renders Sable read line if Sable on crew; retired-rival aside fires only if Salko auto-retired (per Captain Memory). All lines voice-checked.
15. Floor Manager auctioneer-line variants render in the BID_WINDOW substate: lot-open template fires on first round; "we are at X" template updates each tick the high bid changes; lot-closed-sold and lot-closed-withdrawn fire on LOT_RESOLUTION. Templates are read from `auctioneer_lines`; `{lot_headline}` and `{current_high_bid}` substitutions render correctly.
16. Salko flat-bid line displays in the bid log when Salko bids: "Eight thousand." (a flat number, stated alone). Reach's `rival_bids.salko` template is identical to Stellaris's; the venue separation is for content authoring discipline, not behavior.
17. Captain Memory at Reach: across a deterministic 10-session run with the locked Reach catalog and seeded RNG, Salko accumulates `OUTCOME_OUTBID` per the design doc rules; if Salko was already at 2 unresolved outbids from Stellaris and the player loses 1 lot to him at the Reach, his status transitions to `STATUS_WANDERER`; the retired-rival aside fires in the next session the player enters. Player wins / folds / no-overlap correctly suppress the outcome.
18. 3 NEW journal entries trigger correctly on their respective flag sets. Each entry's text passes the Writing Bible scanner and uses Reach-rhythm voice (terse, specific, no "couldn't help but," no "a testament to," no parallel-negation, no banned NPC names). Verified by `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py`.
19. Achievement stub `achievement_auction_reach_debut` registered (no metadata yet — SA-X7 owns content); unlock condition wired so winning the first Reach lot triggers the stub.
20. Full Reach session scenario (`tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_reach_blackmarket.py`): with locked seed and seeded player input, a journeyman-tier player enters the Reach Floor → demand-driven preview fires after the counter reaches 4 → 4-lot session runs end-to-end through preview → SESSION_OPEN → 4 × LOT_OPEN/BID_WINDOW/LOT_RESOLUTION (1 contraband win is forced via seed) → POST_SESSION → SESSION_CLOSE; assert legality penalty applied (-2 stellaris_commerce_guild rep); assert journal entries fired (first reach session + first floor manager + first contraband lesson); assert POST_SESSION social UI renders Salko + 1 ambient Reach Buyer line; assert no UI element leaks; assert demand counter resets on session close.
21. SA-B2 / SA-B3 locked decisions are NOT re-litigated. SA-B4 only adds new Reach-specific decisions and content; SA-B3's Stellaris artifacts (`stellaris_lots.json`, `stellaris_voices.json`, Cassian Velo entry, all Stellaris helpers) are read-only. `_tier_distance` extension is additive — Stellaris ladder behavior is preserved exactly.
22. Pre-phase test baseline preserved: pass count >= 9714; no new failures vs. baseline. New tests bring the count to roughly 9740-9755 passing. Touched-files-only `ruff format` + `ruff check` + `mypy` clean. Writing Bible scanner clean. Dialogue-integrity scanner clean. SI-3 flag scanner clean.

**Risks / open questions.**
- ~~Reach venue location: repurpose `crimson_market` (Wrecker's Bazaar) or add a new `unique` location.~~ **LOCKED §B4.1**: ADD a new `unique` location `crimson_black_market` (display name "The Reach Floor") in `data/galaxy/locations.json` under the `crimson_reach` system. The existing `crimson_market` (marketplace) is NOT modified. *Rationale*: `crimson_market` is the everyday Wrecker's Bazaar marketplace and the Reach's general-purpose buy/sell venue; the Black Market auction is fictionally a separate establishment that operates on the Reach Floor and is gated by Wreckers' Guild membership. Mirrors SA-B3's `stellaris_auction_house` separation from `stellaris_market`.
- ~~Wreckers' Guild tier mapping: design doc §1.4 uses Associate / Member / Trusted / Veteran; SA-1 ships unjoined / apprentice / journeyman / master.~~ **LOCKED §B4.2**: SA-B4 uses SA-1's canonical tier ids verbatim. Lot tier gating: `apprentice`-tier lots are basic contraband + common salvage_lot; `journeyman` adds restricted_weapon + faction_commodity; `master` adds premium contraband + headliners. Design doc §1.4's Associate/Member/Trusted/Veteran labels are NOT introduced. *Rationale*: SA-1's `WRECKERS_GUILD_CONFIG` is the canonical store for Wreckers' tiers; reusing its tier ids prevents two parallel ladders for the same organization. The design doc was written before SA-1 locked the canonical names.
- ~~`_tier_distance` ladder is hard-coded to Stellaris tiers.~~ **LOCKED §B4.3**: Extend `_tier_distance` with a venue-aware ladder dispatch. New helper `tier_ladder_for_venue(venue_id) -> tuple[str, ...]` returns `("none", "apprentice", "regular", "certified", "patron")` for Stellaris and `("none", "apprentice", "journeyman", "master")` for Reach. `_tier_distance(player_tier, required_tier, venue_id=...)` reads the correct ladder. Stellaris call sites pass `venue_id="stellaris"` explicitly; Reach call sites pass `"crimson_reach"`. *Rationale*: minimum engine extension to support distinct ladders without breaking SA-B3's behavior.
- ~~Demand-driven cadence shape.~~ **LOCKED §B4.4**: per-game-day arrivals counter advances by 1 with probability 0.35 (`REACH_DEMAND_PROBABILITY`); session fires when counter reaches 4 (`REACH_SESSION_SIZE`) OR 8 game-days elapse since `last_auction_day["crimson_reach"]` (`REACH_DEMAND_MAX_GAP_DAYS`). Counter resets to 0 on session close. Determinism: each per-day draw seeds on `f"{game_day}_reach_arrivals"`. *Rationale*: produces an irregular feel (typical session every 5-12 game-days; minimum 4 days because counter resets to 0 each cycle) without unbounded uncertainty. SA-B6 may tune the probability and cap.
- ~~Reach Floor Manager voice authoring.~~ **LOCKED §B4.5**: SA-B4 authors the net-new voice sheet at `requirements/character_voices.md`. Proposed canonical name `Vex Tarn`, speaker_id `reach_floor_manager`. Tonal register is the antithesis of Velo: impatient, declarative, no honorifics, no ceremonial cadence, ends conversations rather than wrapping them. The voice-register distinctness is a hard acceptance criterion (#7) verified by static scan. *Rationale*: per design doc §10.6, SA-B4 owns this voice sheet authoring; per SA-PREP-1's NPC inventory at `character_voices.md:641`, `reach_floor_manager` is flagged net-new.
- ~~Voice file key naming (`velo_lines` vs venue-neutral).~~ **LOCKED §B4.6**: AuctionView reads `auctioneer_lines` first, falls back to `velo_lines` for SA-B3 backward compatibility. SA-B4's `crimson_reach_voices.json` uses `auctioneer_lines`. SA-B3's `stellaris_voices.json` is NOT modified. *Rationale*: minimum engine touch with backward compatibility. SA-B6 polish may rename SA-B3's key to `auctioneer_lines` after playtest; SA-B4 does not.
- ~~Named rivals at Reach.~~ **LOCKED §B4.7**: Salko attends every Reach session the player attends (per design doc §4.5 player_target_escalation). Prentiss does NOT attend Reach (heritage-collector register; antiquities are Stellaris-only). Kade does NOT attend Reach (Commerce Guild institutional buyer; will not be seen at a Wreckers' venue). NO new named rival is introduced for Reach. The 4-lot session is filled by Salko + 3 `reach_buyer_N` ambient personas. *Rationale*: adding a new named rival would require a net-new voice sheet outside the SA-B4 scope; Salko's quiet rivalry register reads cleanly at the Reach already, and ambient Reach Buyers fill the room without diluting the cast.
- ~~Legality-consequence magnitudes.~~ **LOCKED §B4.8**: contraband win = `-2` Stellaris Commerce Guild rep; restricted_weapon win = `-1`; salvage_lot win = `0`; faction_commodity win = `0`. NO Crimson Reach faction rep changes (the Reach faction is consequence-neutral toward Reach Floor activity per design doc §1.1 "no-questions-asked culture"). NO Frontier Alliance rep changes. *Rationale*: conservative magnitudes that signal "Stellaris is watching" without becoming punitive at apprentice tier (a player who wins a contraband lot per session loses 2 rep per session — perceptible over 10 sessions but not catastrophic). SA-B6 may tune.
- ~~Reach catalog size.~~ **LOCKED §B4.9**: 12-16 lots. *Rationale*: 4 lots per session × 5-session exclusion window → ≥20 slot-rotation depth → 12-16 lots gives clean rotation without overwhelming variety. Smaller than Stellaris's 18-24 because Reach sessions are smaller.
- ~~Reach headliner count.~~ **LOCKED §B4.10**: 1-2 headliners in catalog. *Rationale*: Reach culture lacks the ceremonial register that makes Stellaris headliners feel like events; one occasional 3-round legendary contraband or restricted weapon adds dramatic punctuation without overplaying the seam. SA-B6 may add or remove based on playtest feel.
- ~~Reach season tags.~~ **LOCKED §B4.11**: Reach ships with NO season tags (all lots have `season_tag = None`); season multiplier is unconditionally 1.0× at Reach. *Rationale*: per design doc §10.10 + the SA-B3 open question on cross-venue season drift, Reach's irregular cadence makes calendar-based seasons less meaningful. SA-B6 may add Reach-specific demand-tied seasons after playtest.
- ~~Reach UI dim styling.~~ **LOCKED §B4.12**: when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`, AuctionView reads ReachDarkLayout's `accent_color = (180, 50, 40)` and uses it for PREVIEW header underline + post-session bullets; `_bg_dim.set_alpha(180)` (vs. 150 for Stellaris). The ReachDarkLayout zone-grid implementation is NOT copied — auction view is a list view, not a zone grid. *Rationale*: matches the design doc deliverable "Reach-themed dim-by-default UI consistent with ReachDarkLayout" without bloating SA-B4 with a layout refactor.
- ~~First-time tip overlay.~~ **LOCKED §B4.13**: shared with Stellaris via `seen_auction_first_session_tip`. NO new tip flag. Reach-specific affordances taught via Floor Manager dialogue + 3 journal entries. *Rationale*: avoids tip-overlay-soup; SA-X3 tutorial pass refines if needed.
- ~~Polish fold-in scope.~~ **LOCKED §B4.14**: 3 flag-gated journal entries + 2 banter trigger flags + 1 achievement stub fold into SA-B4. Crew banter content (SA-X6), achievement metadata (SA-X7), per-venue visual identity refinement (SA-X10), tutorial-pass refinement (SA-X3), cross-anchor narrative threading (SA-X1), and reputation-balance pass (SA-X2) all stay in their own Phase VI cohesion sprints. *Rationale*: matches SA-B3's polish discipline.
- ~~Test-file boundary.~~ **LOCKED §B4.15**: SA-B4 adds 4 new test files + 1 additive class on `test_data_loader_auctions.py` (does NOT modify SA-B3's classes). *Rationale*: keeps engine vs. content boundary clean; SA-B3 file siblings stay untouched.
- **OPEN — defer to implementation**: the exact Floor Manager off-floor dialogue branch count. SA-B4 ships at least 4 branches per the deliverables; if the Floor Manager's voice carries 2-3 additional branches naturally (e.g., a tier-promotion congratulation when player crosses to journeyman, a comment on Salko's presence), the implementer may add them within scope. If branch count would push past 1.5 weeks, raise as `SA-B4-FOLLOW-1` rather than blocking the sprint.
- **OPEN — defer to implementation**: whether to surface the legality consequence in dialogue (Vex notes "the Stellaris Commerce Guild noticed that one") or only in the rep-delta notification. SA-B4 implementer chooses based on playtest feel; default to rep-delta only (avoids preachy in-fiction narration of the player's choice).

**Plan.**

1. **Status flip + read-only context confirmation (~30 min).** Move `Status` from `in-progress (planning)` to `in-progress`. Read sa_bidding_design.md sections 1.4, 2, 3, 4.5 (Salko + Reach Flavor specs), 5, 6, 7, 9.4-9.5, 11; Salko voice sheet at `character_voices.md:1368-1410`; Sable Trent at 1457-1503; agent_principles.md cast-variety section. Re-verify `WRECKERS_GUILD_CONFIG` tier ids in `spacegame/models/wreckers_guild.py:54-65`. Verify `data_loader.load_auction_lots` and `load_auction_voices` are already wired for `crimson_reach` (data_loader.py:1208-1229 — they are). Touches: read-only + one ROADMAP status edit. *Risk*: skipping risks the implementer authoring a parallel tier ladder or duplicating Wreckers' Guild config.

2. **Add flag helpers + extend bidding.py engine surface (~2 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: in `tests/test_models/test_bidding_reach.py`, write the tier-ladder dispatch test (`tier_ladder_for_venue("crimson_reach")` returns the Reach ladder; Stellaris ladder unchanged; `_tier_distance` returns correct distances at boundary positions for both ladders). Write the demand-cadence test (`reach_session_due` returns False before threshold and True at threshold; counter resets on close; deterministic under the seeded RNG). Write the Wreckers'-tier-helper test (`wreckers_tier_for_membership(player)` returns `Player.get_sub_reputation_tier("wreckers_guild")`). Then implement: in `spacegame/constants/flags.py`, add `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter()`, `auction_first_reach_session()`, `auction_first_contraband_win()` per SI-3 cookbook. In `spacegame/models/bidding.py`, add `tier_ladder_for_venue(venue_id)`, refactor `_tier_distance` to accept an optional `venue_id` parameter (default keeps Stellaris ladder for SA-B3 callers), add `wreckers_tier_for_membership(player)`, add `reach_session_due(state, current_day)`, add `reach_advance_demand(state, current_day)`, add module-level `REACH_DEMAND_PROBABILITY = 0.35` and `REACH_DEMAND_MAX_GAP_DAYS = 8`. Extend `_close_session` to reset Reach demand counter on Reach session close. *Risk*: changing `_tier_distance` signature could break SA-B3 callers — keep `venue_id` keyword-only with default `"stellaris"` and verify SA-B3 tests still pass before moving on. Touches: `spacegame/models/bidding.py`, `spacegame/constants/flags.py`, `tests/test_models/test_bidding_reach.py` (NEW).

3. **Author Vex Tarn voice sheet + NPC entry + dialogue tree (~3 hr).** TDD: a per-node voice-bible compliance test in `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py` red-first, covering Floor Manager dialogue + voice-distinctness assertions (no ceremonial keywords, average sentence length ≤ 10 words, sentence-length distribution distinct from Velo's). Then author. Voice-sheet section in `requirements/character_voices.md`: 8-12 sample lines across the emotional range; "what he never says" includes Velo's ceremonial register; verbal habits section explicit about Reach-rhythm cadence per `agent_principles.md:73`. NPC entry in `data/characters/npcs.json`: `id="reach_floor_manager"`, `name="Vex Tarn"`, `title="Reach Floor Manager"`, `home_system_id="crimson_reach"`, `dialogue_id="reach_floor_manager_main"`. Dialogue tree in `data/dialogue/dialogues.json`: 4+ branches (first-encounter sets `seen_first_floor_manager_encounter`; demand-cadence info; tier-locked variant routed when `Player.get_sub_reputation_tier("wreckers_guild") == "unjoined"`; exit). Update `character_voices.md:641` speaker_id registry row marking `reach_floor_manager` as registered for SA-B4. Voice-check before commit. *Risk*: the cast-variety alarm in `agent_principles.md:82` — Vex must be specifically himself, not a generic "rough talker." Read Salko + Malia + Vex aloud as a set before commit; if any two collapse into each other, give Vex something specifically his (a contained obsession, a learned silence, a contradiction). Touches: `requirements/character_voices.md`, `data/characters/npcs.json`, `data/dialogue/dialogues.json`, `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py` (NEW).

4. **Author Reach lot catalog (data) (~2 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_models/test_bidding_reach.py::TestReachLotCatalog` validates count (12-16), category mix (only the 4 Reach categories), Wreckers'-tier coverage, headliner count (1-2), zero `season_tag`, zero modules. Then author `data/auctions/crimson_reach_lots.json` with 12-16 lots distributed across the 4 Reach categories. Use design doc §3.4 example 3 (unstamped fuel additives) as the model. Voice-check headlines/descriptions against `aurelia_voice_examples.md` 16-item diagnostic; Reach-rhythm cadence; specific to the lot, not gestural. *Risk*: catalog Writing Bible scan — author with `aurelia_voice_examples.md` open; descriptions concrete and specific. Touches: `data/auctions/crimson_reach_lots.json` (NEW), `tests/test_models/test_bidding_reach.py`.

Then author `data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json`. Floor Manager lines in Vex's register (lot-open / "we are at" / lot-closed-sold / lot-closed-withdrawn / session-open / session-close — all terse, declarative, no ceremonial cadence); Salko flat-bid template identical to SA-B3's; Reach Buyer ambient lines specific and Reach-flavored; Sable post-session reads register the floor's different rhythm; empty-state Floor Manager flavor in Vex's register; `tier_locked` template short and final ("The floor is for members. Talk to the Guild first."). Voice-check end-to-end. *Risk*: Sable's read templates can drift toward universal-wisdom register; copy a paired example from `aurelia_voice_examples.md` if drafting feels generic. Touches: `data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json` (NEW), `tests/test_models/test_data_loader_auctions.py`, `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py`.

6. **Add `crimson_black_market` location + station-hub navigation + tier gating (~2 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py::TestStationHubNavigation` covers (a) unjoined player → no Enter button + tier-locked message renders; (b) apprentice/journeyman/master player → Enter button renders + routes to `GameState.AUCTION` with `venue_id="crimson_reach"`; (c) talk-to-Floor-Manager affordance surfaces for all tiers but routes to the tier-locked dialogue branch for unjoined. Implement: add `crimson_black_market` to `data/galaxy/locations.json` under `crimson_reach` system; `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS["crimson_black_market"] = GameState.AUCTION` in `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py`; in `_render_detail_panel` (or whatever method renders unique-card detail), branch on the location id and the player's Wreckers' tier — suppress Enter for unjoined, render the `tier_locked` template line instead. *Risk*: regression on SA-1's wreckers-guild Enter button — that path uses a different location id (`crimson_wreckers_guild`) and a different gate. Test that explicitly. Touches: `data/galaxy/locations.json`, `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py`, `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py` (NEW).

7. **`_prepare_reach_session` engine helper + legality-consequence wiring (~3 hr).** Test first: a scenario test in `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_reach_blackmarket.py` that drives a journeyman-tier player through full hub-entry → demand-driven preview → 4-lot session → legality penalty → close. Implement `_prepare_reach_session` in `spacegame/engine/game.py` mirroring `_prepare_stellaris_session` (line 2418-2536); on hub entry to `crimson_black_market`, call `reach_advance_demand(state, current_day)` for any elapsed game-days since last entry, check `reach_session_due`, generate the lot pool with `wreckers_tier_for_membership(player)` and `tier_ladder_for_venue("crimson_reach")`, hand personas (Salko + 3 reach_buyer_N) and voice templates to AuctionView via `set_active_personas` / `set_voice_templates`. In `_ensure_auction_view`, extend the `on_lot_won` callback so when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"` AND `lot.category in {contraband, restricted_weapon}`, call `Player.modify_reputation("stellaris_commerce_guild", -2 if contraband else -1)` BEFORE the existing `auction_first_win` flag set, then set `auction_first_contraband_win()` flag if category is contraband. Wire the hub-entry dispatch at line 1942's neighborhood so `crimson_black_market` routes to `_prepare_reach_session` and `stellaris_auction_house` continues to route to `_prepare_stellaris_session`. *Risk*: the existing `on_lot_won` closure captures `venue_display`; verify the venue_id branch doesn't shadow that. Touches: `spacegame/engine/game.py`, `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_reach_blackmarket.py` (NEW).

8. **Auction view: auctioneer-lines alias + Reach dim tint (~1.5 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py::TestVenueRendering` asserts (a) `_velo_running_commentary` reads `auctioneer_lines` from voice templates with `velo_lines` fallback; (b) when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`, `_bg_dim.get_alpha() == 180`; (c) PREVIEW header underline + post-session bullets render in `(180, 50, 40)`; (d) Stellaris venue rendering is unchanged (alpha 150, default colors). Implement: in `_velo_running_commentary` and any other location reading `velo_lines`, change to `self._voice_templates.get("auctioneer_lines") or self._voice_templates.get("velo_lines")` patterns. In `__init__`, branch on `venue_id` for `_bg_dim` alpha. In `_render_panel_chrome` / PREVIEW header / POST_SESSION bullet renderers, branch on `venue_id` for accent color. Read ReachDarkLayout's `accent_color` constant directly — `from spacegame.views.station_layouts import ReachDarkLayout` then `ReachDarkLayout.accent_color`. *Risk*: substate UI lifecycle — every new color/alpha branch must NOT touch the lifecycle map at line 97 or the substate creation/destruction order. Touches: `spacegame/views/auction_view.py`, `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py`.

9. **Author 3 journal entries (~1 hr).** First Reach session entered (Vex's voice register, factual, short, names "the Reach Floor"); first Floor Manager encounter (Vex's voice register; short specific moment); first contraband win + rep loss (player's understated register; names the rep change concretely; no moralizing). Each entry has a `trigger_flag` matching the helpers from task 2. Test surface: existing journal-trigger scanner + the scenario test from task 7 asserting all 3 entries fire. Voice-check before commit. Touches: `data/journal/entries.json`, `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_reach_blackmarket.py`.

Unlock condition: `auction_state.won_lots` includes a lot whose `venue == "crimson_reach"` for the first time. SA-X7 owns the metadata; SA-B4 owns the stub-id registration. Touches: wherever SA-B2 / SA-B3 register stub achievements (`spacegame/models/bidding.py` constant block per SA-B3 review notes is the precedent), `tests/test_models/test_bidding_reach.py`.

11. **Validation chain (~1 hr).** `ruff format` on touched files only (per `agent_principles.md:110` — never project-wide during a sprint). `ruff check` on touched files. `mypy` on touched files. `pytest -n auto -q`. Confirm pass count >= 9714 and skip count == 98. SI-3 scanner clear. Writing Bible scanner clear. Dialogue-integrity scanner clear. If any pre-existing failure surfaces, note in Activity log; do not chase. Touches: validation only.

12. **Status flip to `review` + phase report + commit (~15 min).** Move `Status` to `review`. Append `**Last phase report.**` block (overwriting any prior phase report block in this section) per agent convention. Commit with `SA-B4: ...` prefix. Do NOT push. Touches: `requirements/roadmap/ROADMAP.md`.

**Decisions locked in this planning phase.**

1. **Reach venue location**: NEW `unique` location `crimson_black_market` ("The Reach Floor") added to `data/galaxy/locations.json` under `crimson_reach`. `crimson_market` (Wrecker's Bazaar marketplace) untouched.
2. **Wreckers' Guild tier mapping**: SA-B4 reuses SA-1's canonical tier ids (`unjoined` / `apprentice` / `journeyman` / `master`) verbatim. Design doc §1.4's Associate/Member/Trusted/Veteran labels NOT introduced. Lot tier gating: apprentice → basic contraband + common salvage_lot; journeyman → restricted_weapon + faction_commodity; master → premium contraband + headliners.
3. **`_tier_distance` venue-aware dispatch**: new `tier_ladder_for_venue(venue_id)` helper; Stellaris ladder unchanged; Reach ladder = `("none", "apprentice", "journeyman", "master")`. `_tier_distance` accepts optional keyword-only `venue_id` (default `"stellaris"`); SA-B3 callers continue to work.
4. **Demand-driven cadence**: per-game-day arrivals counter, +1 with probability 0.35; session fires at counter >= 4 OR 8 game-days since last close. Counter resets on close. Deterministic via seeded RNG.
5. **Reach Floor Manager voice authoring**: SA-B4 authors net-new voice sheet for `Vex Tarn` (speaker_id `reach_floor_manager`). Tonal register is the antithesis of Velo: impatient, declarative, no honorifics, no ceremonial cadence. Voice-distinctness verified by static scan (acceptance #7).
6. **Voice file key alias**: AuctionView reads `auctioneer_lines` first, falls back to `velo_lines`. SA-B3's `velo_lines` key continues to work; SA-B4's Reach file uses `auctioneer_lines`.
7. **Named rivals at Reach**: Salko attends; Prentiss + Kade do NOT. NO new named rival. 3 Reach Buyer ambient personas fill the room.
8. **Legality consequences**: contraband win = `-2` stellaris_commerce_guild rep; restricted_weapon = `-1`; salvage_lot / faction_commodity = `0`. Crimson Reach + Frontier Alliance rep unchanged.
9. **Catalog size**: 12-16 lots in Reach catalog.
10. **Headliner count**: 1-2 headliners in Reach catalog.
11. **Season tags at Reach**: NONE (all `season_tag = None`); season multiplier unconditionally 1.0× at Reach.
12. **Reach UI dim styling**: AuctionView reads ReachDarkLayout's `accent_color = (180, 50, 40)` for PREVIEW header underline + post-session bullets when `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`; `_bg_dim.set_alpha(180)`. Zone-grid implementation NOT copied.
13. **`_prepare_reach_session` engine helper**: mirrors `_prepare_stellaris_session`; routes from `crimson_black_market` hub entry. Legality penalty fires from `on_lot_won` extension before flag set.
14. **First-time tip overlay**: shared with Stellaris via `seen_auction_first_session_tip`. NO new tip flag.
15. **Test-file boundary**: SA-B4 adds 4 NEW test files + 1 additive test class on SA-B3's data-loader test file. SA-B3's classes untouched.

#### SA-B5 — Player-Initiated Auctions

**Status**: done
**Phase**: Phase III | **Size**: L | **Effort**: 1.5 weeks
**Depends on**: SA-B2 | **Blocks**: SA-B6

**Goal.** Player becomes a seller in the same Bidding engine SA-B2 ships. At the Stellaris Auction House, the player consigns an eligible item from cargo or parts inventory, sets a reserve price, pays a non-refundable listing fee, and watches the AI buyer pool (existing SA-B2 personas, reused unchanged) bid against each other for the player's lot during the next scheduled session. Up to 3 active listings concurrently. Sales credit the player at the hammer price; reserve-not-met withdraws the lot back to inventory; the listing fee is paid in either case. Achievement stub `auction_seller` registered (per design doc §9.5 hand-off note). Mirrors SA-B3's content-and-integration discipline: voice content lives in data; first-time tip overlay teaches the mechanic; 3 flag-gated journal entries register narrative beats; banter trigger flags wired for SA-X6; tier-gated entry uses Stellaris Port standing. Reach as a player-listing venue is explicitly deferred to SA-B6 polish — Reach's demand-driven cadence and informal floor manager voice are out of scope here.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/sa_bidding_design.md` (sections 2 lifecycle (mirrored), 3 lot schema (basis for the player-listing schema), 5 player input model (mirror reference: §5.1 actions and §5.2 increment scale apply unchanged to AI buyers; §5.4 reserve mechanic flips so the *player* sets the reserve and the *AI* is held to it), 8 save/load schema (§8.3 explicit migration discipline contract — any AuctionState additions must use `data.get(field, default)`), 11 locked decisions (especially §11.10 boss-drop legendary modules stay boss-acquirable, §11.11 same engine, mirrored direction), 9.5 achievement stub `achievement_auction_seller`, 12 hand-off table for SA-B5 reads {2, 3, 5, 8, 11})
- `requirements/agent_principles.md` (in full; cast-variety section especially — SA-B5 ships zero new NPCs but adds player-facing UI strings and journal entries that must voice-check; honesty-over-politeness matters when locking decisions on what items are listable)
- `requirements/aurelia_voice_examples.md` (16-item diagnostic; required reading before authoring the SellLotView empty-state, listing confirmation copy, the `consigned_lot_lines` Velo extension, and 3 new journal entries)
- `requirements/character_voices.md` (Cassian Velo lines authored by SA-B3 — read to keep the consigned-lot Velo extension on-register; Sable Trent voice if a Sable post-listing read fires; do NOT introduce new NPCs)
- `requirements/cultural_guide.md` (Stellaris Port culture; the "Aurelia Expanse" register so listing-fee + reserve-price language doesn't read as terrestrial e-commerce)
- `requirements/station_anchors.md` (Phase III SA-B5 paragraph at line 151; Bidding integration commitments at 154-160; commitment that the player "feels like they're part of the economy rather than just a customer")
- `requirements/onboarding_design.md` (PT-M `FirstTimeTipOverlay` style — short declarative no-flavor copy; "Got it." dismiss button; new tip flag pattern matches `seen_auction_first_session_tip`)
- `requirements/si3_flag_registry_cookbook.md` (cross-module flag helper conventions for the four new SA-B5 flags)
- `spacegame/models/bidding.py` (`AuctionState` schema and `to_dict`/`from_dict` migration discipline; lifecycle enum `AuctionLifecycle`; `_resolve_lot` is the model integration point for player-lot crediting; `_close_session`/`close_session_for_day` are the hooks for listing-history archival; `_open_next_lot` opens lots without distinguishing seller — SA-B5's player-tagged lots flow through unchanged; achievement counters `auction_lots_won_*` pattern is the model SA-B5's `auction_listings_sold` mirrors)
- `spacegame/models/bidding_lot.py` (`AuctionLot` frozen dataclass; SA-B5 adds an optional `seller_id: Optional[str] = None` field — None = catalog lot, "player" = consigned; backward-compatible with all SA-B3/B4 lots; `with_recently_seen` immutable-update pattern is the precedent for any helper SA-B5 needs)
- `spacegame/models/bidding_persona.py` (existing `make_prentiss`, `make_kade`, `make_salko`, `make_stellaris_speculator`; SA-B5 reuses these unchanged — `compute_effective_value` and `compute_ceiling` work bilaterally because they read `lot.category` + `lot.base_appraisal`, not seller identity)
- `spacegame/models/player.py` (`auction_state` field at line 90; `current_cargo` accessor on `Ship`; `parts_inventory: dict[str, int]` at line 141; `unlocked_modules: set[str]` is the boss-drop legendary set and is OFF LIMITS for listing per design §11.10; `faction_reputation["stellaris_commerce_guild"]` for tier gating; `dialogue_flags` for first-time-tip + journal trigger flags; `ground_equipment` is OFF LIMITS — SA-B5 lists ship-side items only; `salvage_hold_manager` is per-system buffer and OFF LIMITS — items must be in cargo to be listable)
- `spacegame/views/auction_view.py` (PREVIEW substate at lines 580-614 — SA-B5 adds a "List a Lot" button on this substate when player tier >= regular; lifecycle wiring via `on_lot_won` / `on_session_complete` callbacks at lines 2470-2474 — SA-B5 adds `on_player_lot_sold`/`on_player_lot_withdrawn`; `_render_body_preview` shows scheduled-session info — SA-B5 adds an "Active Listings" subsection so the player sees their consigned lots; `_render_body_bid_window` renders rival panel — SA-B5 special-cases player-seller lots so the player sees "Your lot" instead of bid buttons)
- `spacegame/views/CLAUDE.md` (BaseView lifecycle contract: `on_enter`/`on_exit` super calls; `_create_ui`/`_destroy_ui` symmetry; render order; mini-game view recreation pattern)
- `spacegame/views/first_time_tip.py` (`FirstTimeTipOverlay` API used at `auction_view.py:163-275`; SA-B5 mirrors the pattern with a new flag `seen_first_listing_tip()`)
- `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py` (`UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS` and `AUCTION_VENUE_BY_LOCATION_ID` at lines 97-113; SA-B5 does NOT add a hub entry — listing flows from inside the auction view's PREVIEW substate, not as a separate hub card; the existing `stellaris_auction_house` route is the entry point)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` (lines 1971-1990 hub→auction transition; lines 2362-2495 `_ensure_auction_view` — SA-B5 extends this with `on_player_lot_sold` / `on_player_lot_withdrawn` callbacks; lines 2598-2740 `_prepare_stellaris_session` — SA-B5 wraps with a step that pulls eligible `active_listings` from `auction_state` and prepends them to `session_lots` before the persona attendance pass; new `_ensure_sell_lot_view` factory mirrors `_ensure_auction_view`)

- `spacegame/constants/flags.py` (auction flag helpers at lines 616-747; SA-B5 adds `seen_first_listing_tip`, `auction_first_listing_created`, `auction_first_sale`, `auction_first_listing_withdrawn` per SI-3 cookbook)
- `data/auctions/stellaris_lots.json` + `data/auctions/stellaris_voices.json` (read-only — SA-B5 adds 2 keys to `stellaris_voices.json` (`consigned_lot_lines`, `player_listing_post_session`) but does NOT modify SA-B3's existing keys; SA-B5 does not author new lots in `stellaris_lots.json`)
- `data/journal/entries.json` (existing SA-B2/B3/B4 auction entries are the schema model; SA-B5 adds 3 entries: first listing created, first sale, first listing withdrawn)
