**Touch zones.**
- `spacegame/config.py` (ADD `GameState.SELL_LOT`; new constants for listing economics — `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS`, `LISTING_FEE_RATE`, `LISTING_FEE_FLOOR`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_MIN`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_MAX`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_DEFAULT`, `LISTING_TIER_REQUIRED`)
- `spacegame/models/bidding_lot.py` (ADD `seller_id: Optional[str] = None` field with `from_dict` default; `to_dict` emits the field only when non-None to keep SA-B3/B4 catalogs byte-identical)
- `spacegame/models/bidding.py` (NEW `_PlayerListing` frozen dataclass with fields `listing_id`, `item_kind` ("commodity" / "part"), `item_id`, `quantity`, `declared_appraisal`, `reserve_pct`, `listing_fee_paid`, `listed_on_day`, `headline`, `description`, `category`, `to_auction_lot()` converter; extend `AuctionState` with `active_listings: list[_PlayerListing] = []`, `listing_history: list[dict] = []`, `auction_listings_sold: int = 0`, `auction_listings_attempted: int = 0`, `auction_listing_fees_paid: int = 0`; new methods `create_listing(player, item_kind, item_id, quantity, declared_appraisal, reserve_pct, current_day) -> tuple[bool, str, Optional[_PlayerListing]]` (validates, deducts fee, deducts item, appends), `cancel_listing(listing_id, player) -> tuple[bool, str]` (only allowed before the session opens; returns item, fee NOT refunded), `eligible_listings_for_session(current_day) -> list[_PlayerListing]` (returns the up-to-N listings that should join the next session); extend `_resolve_lot` so when the resolving lot's `seller_id == "player"`, the sale price credits the player, the listing moves to `listing_history`, and the player-lot achievement counter increments; extend `to_dict`/`from_dict` with safe defaults)
- `spacegame/models/player.py` (ADD `@property auction_listings_sold` mirroring the existing 4 auction-counter properties at lines 694-712; the `AchievementManager`'s `stat_key` lookup reads it via `getattr(player, "auction_listings_sold")`; no other Player changes — listings live on `auction_state`)
- `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py` (NEW — `SellLotView(BaseView)`. Constructor takes `ui_manager`, `player`, `data_loader`. Two-pane layout: left = item-selection list (eligible cargo + parts grouped by kind, with quantity / category / inferred base appraisal / fee-preview); right = listing form (selected item summary, declared-appraisal field, reserve-pct slider with default 0.70 and bounds 0.50-0.95, fee-preview live-updates, "Confirm Listing" button gated on tier + slot availability + sufficient credits for fee + valid reserve). Empty-state when no eligible items: terse Velo-anonymized line ("Nothing on consignment today.") read from the new `consigned_lot_lines.empty_inventory` voice key. FirstTimeTipOverlay fires on first entry — flag `seen_first_listing_tip`. `next_state = GameState.AUCTION` on confirm or back. Tier-gate: render an inline lock message and disable Confirm when player tier < `regular` Stellaris standing. Loading state during fee deduction; error state when listing creation returns `(False, msg)`. Standard `on_enter`/`on_exit`/`_create_ui`/`_destroy_ui` lifecycle per `views/CLAUDE.md`)
- `spacegame/views/auction_view.py` (PREVIEW substate: ADD a "List a Lot" button visible when `venue_id == "stellaris"` AND player tier >= `regular`; routes to `GameState.SELL_LOT` via `next_state`. Render a small "Active Listings" subsection in `_render_body_preview` summarizing `auction_state.active_listings` with a one-line summary per listing; if zero active listings AND no scheduled session AND tier >= regular, surface the listing affordance prominently. BID_WINDOW substate: when the active lot has `seller_id == "player"`, suppress raise/hold/fold buttons (player cannot bid on own lot) and render a "Your lot — watching" banner instead; AI bidders behave normally. LOT_RESOLUTION: render a player-seller-specific message ("Sold for X, after Stellaris fees: Y" — though there are no Stellaris fees beyond the listing fee in §11.11; the hammer price credits in full) or ("Reserve not met. The lot returns to your hold."). POST_SESSION: surface a Sable post-listing read template if Sable on crew, read from the new `player_listing_post_session` voice key)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` (NEW `_ensure_sell_lot_view` factory; route `GameState.SELL_LOT` from `_handle_state_transitions` mirroring the `GameState.AUCTION` block at line 1971; in `_prepare_stellaris_session`, after `generate_lot_pool` and before persona attendance, call `state.eligible_listings_for_session(current_day)` and prepend the converted player AuctionLot objects to `session_lots` (player listings always appear before catalog lots in the session order so the player isn't waiting through 6 AI lots before their consignment); extend `_ensure_auction_view` with new callbacks `on_player_lot_sold(listing, sale_price)` and `on_player_lot_withdrawn(listing)`; the sold callback credits `player.credits += sale_price`, sets `auction_first_sale` flag if first time, fires `auto_auction_first_sale` journal entry; the withdrawn callback returns the item to player inventory, sets `auction_first_listing_withdrawn` flag if first time, fires `auto_auction_first_listing_withdrawn`; news ticker for player sales >= 50,000 credits emits "A consigned lot sells at Stellaris Auction House. Price undisclosed.")
- `spacegame/constants/flags.py` (ADD `seen_first_listing_tip()`, `auction_first_listing_created()`, `auction_first_sale()`, `auction_first_listing_withdrawn()` per SI-3 cookbook)
- `data/auctions/stellaris_voices.json` (EXTEND with 2 new top-level keys: `consigned_lot_lines` containing 4 templates (lot-open-anonymous, "we are at X" anonymous variant, lot-closed-sold-anonymous, lot-closed-withdrawn-anonymous; all in Velo's ceremonial register but seller-anonymized); `player_listing_post_session` containing 4 Sable templates (sold-above-reserve, sold-near-reserve, withdrawn-no-bids, withdrawn-bids-below-reserve); `consigned_lot_lines.empty_inventory` for the Sell Lot view's no-eligible-items empty state. Existing `velo_lines`, `rival_bids`, `post_session`, `sable_reads`, `empty_state`, `retired_rival` keys are NOT modified)
- `data/journal/entries.json` (3 NEW auto-entries: `auto_auction_first_listing_created` keyed on `auction_first_listing_created`; `auto_auction_first_sale` keyed on `auction_first_sale`; `auto_auction_first_listing_withdrawn` keyed on `auction_first_listing_withdrawn`)
- `data/progression/achievements.json` (ADD 1 entry `auction_seller` after `auction_reach_debut`: `name: "Consignment"`, `description: "List and sell a lot at the Stellaris Auction House."`, `category: "economy"`, `stat_key: "auction_listings_sold"`, `threshold: 1`, `reward_type: "credits"`, `reward_value: 500`, `hidden: true`)
- `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py` (NEW — `_PlayerListing` schema/serialization round-trip; `AuctionState.create_listing` validates eligibility, declared appraisal range, reserve_pct range, slot count, tier; deducts fee on success; deducts item on success; returns `(False, msg)` on failure paths (insufficient credits, invalid reserve, slot full, ineligible item, tier locked); `cancel_listing` returns the item and does NOT refund the fee; `eligible_listings_for_session` returns up to N listings for the next session; `_resolve_lot` for player-seller lots credits the player at hammer price, archives the listing to `listing_history`, increments `auction_listings_sold`; reserve-not-met withdraws and returns item without crediting; AuctionState `to_dict`/`from_dict` round-trip preserves `active_listings`, `listing_history`, the 3 new counters; old saves without these fields default cleanly per §8.3 contract; `seller_id` field on AuctionLot serializes only when non-None and round-trips cleanly)
- `tests/test_views/test_sell_lot_view.py` (NEW — view lifecycle (on_enter/on_exit symmetry; UI elements created and destroyed); item-selection list renders cargo + parts (commodities + parts) and excludes ineligible items (modules in `unlocked_modules`, ground equipment, salvage_hold contents); empty-state renders the voice template line when no eligible items; tier-locked branch renders inline lock message and disables Confirm when player tier < regular; FirstTimeTipOverlay fires on first entry and is suppressed by `seen_first_listing_tip` thereafter; declared-appraisal field rejects non-positive values; reserve-pct slider clamps to [0.50, 0.95]; fee-preview updates live with declared-appraisal changes; Confirm with valid form deducts fee + item, appends listing, sets `auction_first_listing_created` flag, returns to AUCTION; Confirm fails gracefully with error-state UI when listing creation returns False)
- `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_player_listing.py` (NEW — PREVIEW shows "List a Lot" button when player tier >= regular at Stellaris; button absent when tier < regular OR venue_id != "stellaris"; "Active Listings" subsection renders one row per listing in `auction_state.active_listings`; BID_WINDOW for a player-seller lot suppresses raise/hold/fold buttons and renders the "Your lot — watching" banner; LOT_RESOLUTION for a player-seller lot displays the sold/withdrawn message variant; POST_SESSION renders Sable's `player_listing_post_session` line if a player lot resolved this session AND Sable is on crew; voice-template extension keys (`consigned_lot_lines`, `player_listing_post_session`) load through the existing data-loader path)
- `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_player_listing.py` (NEW — full scenario: regular-tier Stellaris player → Sell Lot view → consign 1 cargo commodity (declared appraisal 8,000, reserve 0.70) → fee deducted, item removed, listing appended → next scheduled Stellaris session → player lot is first lot in session → AI buyers bid (deterministic with locked seed) → lot sells at hammer price above reserve → player credited at hammer price → first-sale journal entry fires → `auction_first_sale` flag set → `auction_listings_sold` increments to 1 → second scenario branch: same player consigns a second lot but sets reserve at 0.95 (above any AI ceiling per locked seed) → AI bids stop below reserve → withdrawn → item returned to inventory → fee NOT refunded → `auction_first_listing_withdrawn` flag set; assert all callbacks fire in correct order; assert no UI element leaks; assert achievement stub `auction_seller` registered)
- `tests/test_data_integrity/test_player_listing_voice.py` (NEW — Writing Bible scan over the new `consigned_lot_lines` + `player_listing_post_session` voice templates AND the 3 new journal entries AND the SellLotView UI strings; voice-distinctness assertion that `consigned_lot_lines` matches Velo's ceremonial register (NOT Floor Manager terse register) so the consigned-lot announcement reads as a Stellaris event; Sable's `player_listing_post_session` reads must not collapse into universal-wisdom register — copy a paired example from `aurelia_voice_examples.md` if drafting feels generic)

**Deliverables.**
- **`_PlayerListing` model** — frozen dataclass in `spacegame/models/bidding.py` carrying the listing-specific fields the player chose (item kind, item id, quantity, declared appraisal, reserve_pct, listing fee paid, listed_on_day, headline, description, category). `to_auction_lot()` produces an `AuctionLot` with `seller_id="player"`, `venue="stellaris"`, `is_headliner=False`, `season_tag=None`, `faction_gate=None`, `rep_tier_required="regular"` (the listing tier-gate is mirrored on the resulting lot for symmetry), `recently_seen_count=0`, `contraband=False` (locked decision §B5.5: the SA-B5 sprint ships only non-contraband listings), `source_module_id=None`, `base_appraisal=declared_appraisal`. Backwards-compatible serialization via `data.get(field, default)` per §8.3.
- **AuctionState player-listing extension** — 3 new fields (`active_listings`, `listing_history`, plus 3 counters `auction_listings_sold`, `auction_listings_attempted`, `auction_listing_fees_paid`); 3 new methods (`create_listing`, `cancel_listing`, `eligible_listings_for_session`); `_resolve_lot` extended so when `lot.seller_id == "player"`, the sale price credits the player (caller-side via the engine callback), the listing dict moves to `listing_history` with the resolution outcome, and `auction_listings_sold` increments only on `sold=True`. All save/load defaults safe; old saves load cleanly.
- **`AuctionLot.seller_id` field** — `Optional[str] = None`; `to_dict` emits the field only when non-None so SA-B3/B4 catalog files stay byte-identical and the data-loader's `from_dict` reads the field as `None` for catalog lots.
- **SellLotView** — net-new view at `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py`. Two-pane layout (item-selection left, listing form right), tier-locked banner for sub-regular players, empty-state from the `consigned_lot_lines.empty_inventory` voice key, FirstTimeTipOverlay on first entry, fee-preview live-updates with the declared-appraisal field, reserve-pct slider clamped to [0.50, 0.95], Confirm button gated on (tier >= regular) AND (active_listings count < `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS=3`) AND (player credits >= computed listing fee) AND (declared appraisal > 0) AND (reserve_pct in valid range). On confirm, calls `auction_state.create_listing(...)` and routes back to `GameState.AUCTION`. Standard BaseView lifecycle; UI elements destroyed in `_destroy_ui`.
- **Listing economics constants** — `LISTING_FEE_RATE = 0.05` (5% of declared appraisal), `LISTING_FEE_FLOOR = 100` (minimum fee in credits), `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_MIN = 0.50`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_MAX = 0.95`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_DEFAULT = 0.70`, `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS = 3`, `LISTING_TIER_REQUIRED = "regular"`. All in `spacegame/config.py`. SA-B6 may tune.
- **Eligibility rules** — listable items: (a) any commodity in `player.ship.current_cargo` (quantity > 0), (b) any ship part in `player.parts_inventory` (count > 0). NOT listable: `unlocked_modules` (boss-drop legendary set per §11.10), `ground_equipment` (ground-side, not ship-side), `salvage_hold_manager` contents (per-system buffer, must transfer to cargo first), installed equipment on the ship (player must un-equip before listing). Eligibility check happens both in SellLotView's filtering and in `create_listing` validation. Locked decision §B5.4.
- **AuctionView player-seller integration** — PREVIEW shows a "List a Lot" button (Stellaris-only, regular+ tier); "Active Listings" subsection lists current consignments. BID_WINDOW special-cases player-seller lots: bid action buttons hidden, "Your lot — watching" banner rendered, AI bidders behave normally. LOT_RESOLUTION renders sold/withdrawn message variants for player lots. POST_SESSION renders Sable's `player_listing_post_session` line when applicable.
- **Engine wiring (`_prepare_stellaris_session` extension + new callbacks)** — Before persona attendance, the prepare helper calls `state.eligible_listings_for_session(current_day)` and prepends converted player lots to `session_lots`. Two new callbacks on AuctionView: `on_player_lot_sold(listing, sale_price)` credits `player.credits += sale_price`, sets `auction_first_sale` flag on first sale, fires `auto_auction_first_sale` journal entry, increments stat counter via the model. `on_player_lot_withdrawn(listing)` returns the item to inventory (commodity → `ship.add_cargo`, part → `parts_inventory[id] += quantity`), sets `auction_first_listing_withdrawn` flag on first withdrawal, fires `auto_auction_first_listing_withdrawn`. News ticker emits a single anonymous headline for player sales >= 50,000 credits ("A consigned lot sells at Stellaris Auction House. Price undisclosed.").
- **Voice file extension** — 2 new top-level keys in `data/auctions/stellaris_voices.json`: `consigned_lot_lines` (4 Velo templates seller-anonymized + 1 SellLotView empty-inventory line) and `player_listing_post_session` (4 Sable post-session-read templates covering sold-above-reserve, sold-near-reserve, withdrawn-no-bids, withdrawn-bids-below-reserve). All in their respective voice registers. SA-B3's existing keys are NOT modified.
- **Polish fold-in #1 — FirstTimeTipOverlay on first SellLotView entry.** Flag `seen_first_listing_tip`. Copy: short, declarative, no flavor — "Listing fee is charged whether the lot sells or not. Set a reserve to refuse low bids." "Got it." dismiss button. Mirrors `seen_auction_first_session_tip`.
- **Polish fold-in #2 — 3 flag-gated journal entries.** `auto_auction_first_listing_created` (terse, in-fiction; player notes the fee is paid and the lot is on the floor); `auto_auction_first_sale` (records the hammer price concretely; no celebration); `auto_auction_first_listing_withdrawn` (records the reserve was not met and the item is back; the fee is gone). All voice-checked against the Writing Bible scanner AND `aurelia_voice_examples.md` 16-item diagnostic.
- **Polish fold-in #3 — Banter trigger flags + achievement stub.** 3 banter trigger flags (`auction_first_listing_created`, `auction_first_sale`, `auction_first_listing_withdrawn`) registered for SA-X6 consumption. Achievement stub `auction_seller` registered in `data/progression/achievements.json` with `stat_key="auction_listings_sold"`, `threshold=1`. SA-X7 owns final achievement metadata; SA-B5 owns the unlock condition (model counter increments on first player-seller-lot sold).
- **Tier-gated entry** — Stellaris Port standing tier `>= regular` (rep >= 0 per existing thresholds in `stellaris_tier_for_standing`) required to access the SellLotView. Sub-regular players see the "List a Lot" button rendered as locked with an inline tier-locked message. Listing creation also enforces the tier check at the model layer (`create_listing` returns `(False, "Listing requires Stellaris regular standing or above.")`).
- **Save/load coverage** — `AuctionState.to_dict` / `from_dict` extend with safe defaults for the 3 new fields and 3 new counters; `AuctionLot.to_dict` / `from_dict` round-trip the new optional `seller_id`; `_PlayerListing` carries its own `to_dict` / `from_dict`. Pre-SA-B5 saves load cleanly with empty listings + zero counters. Verified by round-trip test.
- **Tests** — 4 NEW test files (model, sell-lot-view, auction-view-player-listing addition, scenario, data-integrity-voice) covering ~25-35 new test functions across model methods, view lifecycle, scenario end-to-end, and voice-bible compliance.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. `AuctionLot` gains optional `seller_id: Optional[str] = None` field. SA-B3 catalog (`stellaris_lots.json`) and SA-B4 catalog (`crimson_reach_lots.json`) load with `seller_id=None` for every lot. `to_dict` emits the field only when non-None — SA-B3/B4 lot dicts are unchanged on round-trip. Verified by `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py::TestSellerIdField`.
2. `_PlayerListing` frozen dataclass round-trips through `to_dict`/`from_dict` cleanly. Old saves predating SA-B5 load with empty `active_listings` and `listing_history` and zero `auction_listings_sold` / `auction_listings_attempted` / `auction_listing_fees_paid` counters per §8.3 migration discipline. Verified by `TestAuctionStateMigration`.
3. `AuctionState.create_listing` validates: (a) item kind in {"commodity", "part"}; (b) item exists in player inventory at quantity >= requested; (c) declared_appraisal > 0; (d) reserve_pct in [0.50, 0.95]; (e) `len(active_listings) < MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS=3`; (f) player Stellaris tier >= "regular"; (g) player credits >= computed fee. Failure cases each return distinct `(False, str)` messages without mutating state. Success deducts fee from credits, deducts item from inventory, appends `_PlayerListing` to `active_listings`, increments `auction_listings_attempted`, increments `auction_listing_fees_paid`. Verified parametrically across all 7 failure paths and the success path.
4. Listing fee math: `fee = max(LISTING_FEE_FLOOR, int(declared_appraisal * LISTING_FEE_RATE))` = `max(100, int(declared * 0.05))`. Boundary-tested at appraisals 0 / 1 / 1999 / 2000 / 50000 / 1000000. Fee is paid in either sale outcome (sold or withdrawn); never refunded.
5. `cancel_listing(listing_id, player)` succeeds only when the listing is still `active` (not already pulled into a live session); returns the item to player inventory; does NOT refund the fee. Failure path (listing already in active session) returns `(False, "Listing is already on the auction floor; cannot cancel.")`. Verified by `TestCancelListing`.
6. `eligible_listings_for_session(current_day)` returns up to `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS=3` listings whose `listed_on_day <= current_day`. Listings already converted to lots in a prior session (tracked by listing id presence in any active session lot) are excluded.
7. Player-seller lot resolution: when a session lot has `seller_id == "player"` and `_resolve_lot` fires, on `sold=True` the listing dict moves to `listing_history` with `outcome="sold"` + `sale_price` + `closed_on_day`; `auction_listings_sold` increments. On `sold=False` (reserve not met), the listing dict moves to `listing_history` with `outcome="withdrawn"`; `auction_listings_sold` does NOT increment. Verified by `TestResolvePlayerLot`.
8. `_prepare_stellaris_session` integrates eligible player listings: when called with the player having 1+ eligible `active_listings`, the prepared `session_lots` list begins with the converted player lots followed by the catalog lots, capped at the standard or headliner session size; persona attendance and speculator-category cycling are unaffected. When the player has zero eligible listings, behavior is byte-identical to SA-B3's pre-SA-B5 prepare flow. Verified by `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_player_listing.py::TestSessionPreparation`.
9. AuctionView PREVIEW: at `venue_id == "stellaris"` AND player Stellaris tier >= `regular`, the "List a Lot" button renders and routes to `GameState.SELL_LOT` on press. At tier < `regular`, the button renders disabled with a tier-locked label ("Stellaris regular standing required."). At `venue_id == "crimson_reach"`, the button does NOT render. The "Active Listings" subsection renders one row per listing in `auction_state.active_listings`. Verified by `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_player_listing.py::TestPreviewListingAffordances`.
10. AuctionView BID_WINDOW for player-seller lots: when the active lot has `seller_id == "player"`, raise/hold/fold buttons are NOT rendered (they are the only player-action surface, and the player is the seller — they cannot bid on their own lot); a "Your lot — watching" banner renders instead; AI bidders behave normally; the round timer + AI counter-bid logic are unchanged. Player-as-bidder lots (catalog lots) render the standard bid UI. Verified by `TestBidWindowPlayerSeller`.
11. AuctionView LOT_RESOLUTION for player-seller lots: `sold` outcome renders "Sold for {sale_price:,} credits."; `withdrawn` outcome renders "Reserve not met. The lot returns to your hold."; both messages voice-check clean. Verified by `TestLotResolutionPlayerSeller`.
12. AuctionView POST_SESSION: when at least one player lot resolved in the session AND Sable is on crew, a Sable line from `player_listing_post_session` renders (bucket selected by outcome — sold-above-reserve / sold-near-reserve / withdrawn-no-bids / withdrawn-bids-below-reserve); when Sable is not on crew, the line is omitted; when no player lot resolved this session, the line is omitted. Voice-check clean. Verified by `TestPostSessionPlayerListing`.
13. SellLotView lifecycle: `on_enter` calls `super().on_enter()` and creates UI elements; `on_exit` calls `super().on_exit()` and destroys all UI elements (no leaks). FirstTimeTipOverlay fires on first entry (flag `seen_first_listing_tip`); on second entry the overlay does NOT fire. Empty-state renders the `consigned_lot_lines.empty_inventory` voice line when player has zero eligible items. Tier-locked branch renders the inline lock message and disables Confirm when player tier < `regular`. Verified by `tests/test_views/test_sell_lot_view.py`.
14. SellLotView form behavior: declared-appraisal field rejects non-positive integers; reserve-pct slider clamps to [0.50, 0.95] with default 0.70; fee-preview updates live with declared-appraisal changes; Confirm button enabled only when all gates pass (tier >= regular, slot count < 3, sufficient credits, valid form). Confirm with valid form calls `auction_state.create_listing(...)`, sets `auction_first_listing_created` flag if first time, fires `auto_auction_first_listing_created` journal entry, returns to `GameState.AUCTION`. Confirm failure renders error-state UI from the returned `(False, msg)`.
15. Engine callbacks: on player-lot sale, `on_player_lot_sold(listing, sale_price)` credits `player.credits += sale_price`, sets `auction_first_sale` flag if first time, fires `auto_auction_first_sale` journal entry. On player-lot withdrawal, `on_player_lot_withdrawn(listing)` returns the item to inventory (commodity via `ship.add_cargo(item_id, quantity, price_per_unit=0)`; part via `parts_inventory[item_id] += quantity`), sets `auction_first_listing_withdrawn` flag if first time, fires `auto_auction_first_listing_withdrawn` journal entry. Verified end-to-end in the scenario test.
16. News ticker integration: player sales with hammer price >= 50,000 credits emit a single anonymous headline ("A consigned lot sells at Stellaris Auction House. Price undisclosed."); sales below the threshold emit no headline. Verified by parametric test.
17. Voice content for `consigned_lot_lines` (4 Velo templates + 1 empty-inventory) reads in Velo's ceremonial register (matches the cadence of `velo_lines` templates per a static keyword/sentence-length scan); seller-anonymized (no "{player_name}" substitutions). Voice content for `player_listing_post_session` (4 Sable templates) does not collapse into universal-wisdom register (sentence-length distribution + diagnostic-keyword scan from `aurelia_voice_examples.md`). 3 new journal entries pass the Writing Bible scanner AND the 16-item diagnostic. Verified by `tests/test_data_integrity/test_player_listing_voice.py`.
18. Achievement stub `auction_seller` registered in `data/progression/achievements.json` with `stat_key: "auction_listings_sold"`, `threshold: 1`, `category: "economy"`, `hidden: true`. The existing AchievementManager picks up the entry without code changes. Player property `auction_listings_sold` returns `auction_state.auction_listings_sold`. `_mark_auction_seller_if_first` is NOT needed — the model counter increment is the unlock signal. Verified by `TestAuctionSellerAchievement`.
19. Full scenario (`tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_player_listing.py`): with locked seed and seeded player input, a regular-tier player at Stellaris → enters SellLotView → consigns 1 cargo commodity (declared appraisal 8,000, reserve 0.70) → fee 400 credits deducted → item removed → listing appended → next scheduled session → player lot is first lot in session → AI buyers (deterministic) bid → lot sells at hammer price >= reserve_price → player credited → first-sale journal + flag fires → counter increments. Second branch: regular-tier player consigns a second lot with reserve 0.95 → AI bids stop below reserve → withdrawn → item returned → fee NOT refunded → first-withdrawal journal + flag fires.
20. SA-B2 / SA-B3 / SA-B4 locked decisions are NOT re-litigated. SA-B5 only adds new player-seller-specific decisions and content; SA-B3's Stellaris artifacts (Velo dialogue tree, SA-B3 lot catalog) are extended additively (2 new voice keys); SA-B4's Reach artifacts are NOT modified. AI persona behavior is unchanged — `compute_effective_value` and `compute_ceiling` work bilaterally because they read `lot.category` + `lot.base_appraisal`, not seller identity.
21. Pre-phase test baseline preserved: pass count >= 9780; no new failures vs. baseline. New tests bring the count to roughly 9810-9825 passing. Touched-files-only `ruff format` + `ruff check` + `mypy` clean. Writing Bible scanner clean. Dialogue-integrity scanner clean. SI-3 flag scanner clean.

**Risks / open questions.**
- ~~What can the player list?~~ **LOCKED §B5.1**: Items in `player.ship.current_cargo` (any commodity) AND `player.parts_inventory` (any ship part). NOT listable: `unlocked_modules` (boss-drop legendary set per §11.10), `ground_equipment`, `salvage_hold_manager` contents (must transfer to cargo first), installed ship equipment. *Rationale*: keeps SA-B5 on a clean ship-side eligibility surface; preserves the boss-drop loop for legendary modules; aligns with player intuition that "items in my hold" is what gets consigned.
- ~~Which venue(s) accept player listings?~~ **LOCKED §B5.2**: Stellaris Auction House only. Crimson Reach Black Market does NOT accept player listings in SA-B5. *Rationale*: Stellaris's calendar-based session schedule maps cleanly to listing scheduling; Reach's demand-driven cadence makes "schedule a player lot" awkward; Reach's no-questions-asked culture is more naturally a buyer-side experience. SA-B6 polish may consider adding Reach player listings if playtest demands it.
- ~~How do player lots integrate with sessions?~~ **LOCKED §B5.3**: Player lots are prepended to the next scheduled Stellaris session's `session_lots` list. Up to 3 player lots per session (capped by `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS`); session size is unchanged (catalog lots are dropped from the tail to make room if needed). AI buyer pool is the existing 3 named rivals + 2 Stellaris Speculators — bilaterally compatible because persona value functions read `lot.category` + `lot.base_appraisal`. *Rationale*: maximizes engine reuse (§11.11); player experiences a session that contains both their consignment and the AI catalog, reinforcing the "I'm participating in the economy" framing from `station_anchors.md:151`.
- ~~Listing fee structure.~~ **LOCKED §B5.4**: `fee = max(LISTING_FEE_FLOOR=100, int(declared_appraisal * LISTING_FEE_RATE=0.05))`. Fee is paid up front at listing creation; never refunded (sold OR withdrawn OR cancelled). *Rationale*: 5% rate is below typical e-commerce listing fees but above zero — a perceptible cost; floor of 100 prevents fee abuse on micro-listings; non-refundable behavior matches the design doc's "listing fee charged regardless of sale" deliverable. SA-B6 may tune.
- ~~Reserve price interaction.~~ **LOCKED §B5.5**: Player sets `reserve_pct` in [0.50, 0.95], default 0.70. Reserve price is hidden from AI buyers (mirroring §5.4). Below 0.50 not allowed (the "give it away" floor — Stellaris doesn't accept consignments where the reserve is half value or less; this is also a balance lever to prevent player-listing arbitrage). SA-B5 ships only non-contraband listings (`contraband=False` on the converted lot); contraband listings would carry legality-consequence math that has not been designed for the player-as-seller direction. *Rationale*: locks the reserve range tight so playtest can validate; defers contraband listings to SA-B6.
- ~~Listing slot limit.~~ **LOCKED §B5.6**: `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS = 3`. Player can have at most 3 listings active simultaneously. Cancellation frees a slot (without refunding the fee); a sold or withdrawn listing also frees a slot at session close. *Rationale*: matches the design-doc deliverable text ("configurable, e.g., 3 active at a time"); 3 is enough to feel meaningful without becoming a market-manipulation surface for the player. SA-B6 may tune.
- ~~Where does the player initiate a listing?~~ **LOCKED §B5.7**: New `SellLotView` at `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py` accessed from the AuctionView's PREVIEW substate via a "List a Lot" button. New `GameState.SELL_LOT`. Routes back to `GameState.AUCTION` on confirm or back. NOT a modal inside AuctionView (the form is large enough — item-selection list, declared-appraisal input, reserve slider, fee preview — to justify its own view); NOT a separate hub-card location (avoids hub clutter and keeps the player's mental model "this is the auction house's other side"). *Rationale*: separates concerns cleanly; matches the BaseView lifecycle pattern per `views/CLAUDE.md`.
- ~~Velo's voice for player lots.~~ **LOCKED §B5.8**: Velo announces player-listed lots in his ceremonial register but seller-anonymized — no "{player_name}" substitution, no "consigned by Captain X" tag. The lot is identifiable only by category and headline. New `consigned_lot_lines` voice key in `stellaris_voices.json` carries 4 Velo templates (lot-open, "we are at X", lot-closed-sold, lot-closed-withdrawn) + 1 SellLotView empty-inventory line. *Rationale*: keeps the seller's identity ambiguous for narrative texture; mirrors real auction-house anonymity for consigned lots; avoids the awkward case where Velo announces the player's own name during the player's session.
- ~~CaptainMemory effects when an AI rival wins a player lot.~~ **LOCKED §B5.9**: NO `OUTCOME_OUTBID` or other CaptainMemory entry fires when an AI rival wins a player-listed lot. The player isn't *losing* — the rival is *paying the player*. New outcome constants are NOT introduced. *Rationale*: keeps Captain Memory focused on adversarial encounters; a rival buying from the player is a positive-sum interaction, not a rivalry tension; design doc §6.3 is intentionally narrow ("player loses to rival in same lot") and SA-B5 does not extend it.
- ~~Tier-gated entry.~~ **LOCKED §B5.10**: Stellaris Port standing tier `>= "regular"` (rep >= 0 per `stellaris_tier_for_standing`) required to access SellLotView and create listings. Apprentice players see the "List a Lot" button rendered as locked with a tier-locked message. Listing creation enforces the tier check at the model layer too (defence in depth). *Rationale*: institutional venues don't accept consignments from unproven sellers; mirrors real auction houses; gives the apprentice tier a concrete progression target ("regular tier unlocks consignment").
- ~~First-time tip overlay.~~ **LOCKED §B5.11**: New tip flag `seen_first_listing_tip()` per SI-3 cookbook. Copy: "Listing fee is charged whether the lot sells or not. Set a reserve to refuse low bids." Fires on first entry to SellLotView. *Rationale*: this is the kind of mechanic where blind-firsting is punishing (the player loses the fee on a withdrawn listing); a one-shot tip teaches the irreversibility of the fee and the role of the reserve, then never fires again. Onboarding §(a) overlay style.
- ~~Polish fold-in scope.~~ **LOCKED §B5.12**: 3 flag-gated journal entries + 3 banter trigger flags + 1 achievement stub (`auction_seller`) + 1 first-time tip overlay fold into SA-B5. Crew banter content (SA-X6), achievement metadata text (SA-X7), per-venue visual identity refinement (SA-X10), tutorial-pass refinement (SA-X3), cross-anchor narrative threading (SA-X1), reputation-balance pass (SA-X2), and Reach player-listing variant (SA-B6) all stay in their own sprints. *Rationale*: matches SA-B3 / SA-B4 polish discipline.
- ~~Test-file boundary.~~ **LOCKED §B5.13**: SA-B5 adds 5 new test files. Does NOT modify SA-B2 / SA-B3 / SA-B4 test classes; extends the data-loader test only if the loader needs new logic (it does not — the additional voice keys flow through unchanged). *Rationale*: keeps the engine-vs-content boundary clean; prior sprints' tests stay untouched.
- **OPEN — defer to implementation**: Whether the player-listing inserts into the *very next* Stellaris session deterministically, or has its own draw weight that competes with catalog lots for inclusion. SA-B5 implementer ships the deterministic-prepend behavior (locked §B5.3); if playtest reveals the player's lots dominate sessions and the AI catalog feels squeezed, SA-B6 may introduce a draw-weight mechanic. Default to deterministic prepend for SA-B5 — predictability is more important than balance at this stage.
- **OPEN — defer to implementation**: Whether listings can be *cancelled* after the session preview opens but before the lot is called. SA-B5 implementer ships `cancel_listing` only available pre-session (per locked §B5 acceptance criterion 5); if playtest reveals "I changed my mind during preview" is a common ask, SA-B6 may extend the cancellation window. Default conservative — cancellation windows are user-visible state and complicate the UI.

**Plan.**

1. **Status flip + read-only context confirmation (~30 min).** Move `Status` from `in-progress (planning)` to `in-progress`. Re-verify `requirements/sa_bidding_design.md` sections 2, 3, 5, 8, 11; SA-B3's `_prepare_stellaris_session` at `engine/game.py:2598-2740`; the existing `AuctionLot` schema at `bidding_lot.py:56-167`; `AuctionState.to_dict`/`from_dict` migration discipline at `bidding.py:1188-1260`; the `FirstTimeTipOverlay` API at `views/first_time_tip.py`. Touches: read-only + one ROADMAP status edit. *Risk*: skipping risks the implementer authoring a parallel listing-state surface or breaking SA-B3's lot-catalog round-trip.

2. **Add `seller_id` field to `AuctionLot` + flag helpers (~1.5 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: in `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py`, write `TestSellerIdField::test_default_is_none`, `test_to_dict_omits_when_none`, `test_to_dict_emits_when_player`, `test_from_dict_round_trip`, `test_existing_catalog_loads_unchanged` (asserts `data/auctions/stellaris_lots.json` and `data/auctions/crimson_reach_lots.json` still load with `seller_id is None` for every lot). Then implement: in `spacegame/models/bidding_lot.py`, add `seller_id: Optional[str] = None` field; extend `to_dict` to omit when None; extend `from_dict` to default None. In `spacegame/constants/flags.py`, add `seen_first_listing_tip()`, `auction_first_listing_created()`, `auction_first_sale()`, `auction_first_listing_withdrawn()` per SI-3 cookbook conventions. *Risk*: changing `to_dict` could break SA-B3/B4 round-trip tests — verify both catalogs' tests still pass before moving on. Touches: `spacegame/models/bidding_lot.py`, `spacegame/constants/flags.py`, `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py` (NEW).

3. **Listing economics constants + new GameState (~30 min, TDD red-first).** Test first: `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py::TestListingEconomics::test_constants_match_locked_decisions` validates `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS == 3`, `LISTING_FEE_RATE == 0.05`, `LISTING_FEE_FLOOR == 100`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_MIN == 0.50`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_MAX == 0.95`, `LISTING_RESERVE_PCT_DEFAULT == 0.70`, `LISTING_TIER_REQUIRED == "regular"`. Then implement: add the constants to `spacegame/config.py`; add `GameState.SELL_LOT = "sell_lot"` to the enum at line 331. *Risk*: constant naming collisions — search the existing config.py first. Touches: `spacegame/config.py`, `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py`.

4. **`_PlayerListing` dataclass + AuctionState extension (~3 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: write `TestPlayerListingDataclass::test_round_trip` (to_dict / from_dict cleanly preserve all fields); `TestAuctionStateMigration::test_old_save_loads_with_safe_defaults` (a save dict missing `active_listings`, `listing_history`, and the 3 counters loads with empty lists and zero counters); `test_create_listing_validates_*` (parametric across all 7 failure paths: invalid item kind, item missing from inventory, declared_appraisal <= 0, reserve_pct out of [0.50, 0.95], slot full, tier < regular, insufficient credits); `test_create_listing_success_deducts_fee_and_item`; `test_cancel_listing_returns_item_does_not_refund_fee`; `test_eligible_listings_for_session_returns_up_to_max`; `test_resolve_player_lot_sold_credits_and_archives`; `test_resolve_player_lot_withdrawn_does_not_increment_sold_counter`. Then implement: in `spacegame/models/bidding.py`, add the `_PlayerListing` frozen dataclass with `to_auction_lot()` converter; extend `AuctionState` with the 3 new fields + 3 new counters; add `create_listing`, `cancel_listing`, `eligible_listings_for_session` methods; extend `_resolve_lot` so when `lot.seller_id == "player"`, the listing dict moves to `listing_history` and (on sold) `auction_listings_sold` increments; extend `to_dict`/`from_dict` with safe defaults per §8.3. Add `@property auction_listings_sold` to `Player` mirroring the existing 4 auction-counter properties. *Risk*: `_resolve_lot` extension could break SA-B3/B4 catalog-lot resolution — every catalog lot has `seller_id is None`, so the new branch must fire only when seller_id == "player"; verify SA-B3/B4 tests stay green before moving on. Touches: `spacegame/models/bidding.py`, `spacegame/models/player.py`, `tests/test_models/test_bidding_player_listing.py`.

5. **Author voice extension + 3 journal entries + achievement stub (~2 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_data_integrity/test_player_listing_voice.py` red-first asserting (a) Writing Bible scanner clean over the 2 new voice keys + 3 new journal entries; (b) `consigned_lot_lines` register matches Velo's ceremonial cadence (sentence-length distribution + ceremonial-keyword presence); (c) `player_listing_post_session` Sable templates do not collapse into universal-wisdom register (16-item diagnostic). Then author: in `data/auctions/stellaris_voices.json`, add 2 new top-level keys without modifying existing keys: `consigned_lot_lines` containing 4 Velo templates (lot-open-anonymous, "we are at X" anonymous variant, lot-closed-sold-anonymous, lot-closed-withdrawn-anonymous) + 1 `empty_inventory` SellLotView line; `player_listing_post_session` containing 4 Sable templates covering the 4 outcome buckets. In `data/journal/entries.json`, add 3 auto-entries: `auto_auction_first_listing_created` keyed on `auction_first_listing_created`; `auto_auction_first_sale` keyed on `auction_first_sale`; `auto_auction_first_listing_withdrawn` keyed on `auction_first_listing_withdrawn`. In `data/progression/achievements.json`, add `auction_seller` per the deliverable spec. Voice-check end-to-end against `aurelia_voice_examples.md` 16-item diagnostic before commit. *Risk*: Sable's reads can drift toward universal-wisdom register; copy a paired example from `aurelia_voice_examples.md` if drafting feels generic. Touches: `data/auctions/stellaris_voices.json`, `data/journal/entries.json`, `data/progression/achievements.json`, `tests/test_data_integrity/test_player_listing_voice.py` (NEW).

6. **`SellLotView` (~4 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: `tests/test_views/test_sell_lot_view.py` covering lifecycle (on_enter/on_exit symmetry; `_create_ui`/`_destroy_ui`); FirstTimeTipOverlay fires on first entry only; tier-locked branch when player tier < regular; empty-state when no eligible items; item-selection list filters out modules / ground_equipment / salvage_hold; declared-appraisal field validation; reserve-pct slider clamping; fee-preview live-updates; Confirm with valid form deducts fee + item, sets `auction_first_listing_created`, fires journal entry, returns to `GameState.AUCTION`; Confirm with insufficient credits surfaces error-state UI. Then implement: `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py` extending BaseView. Two-pane layout per views/CLAUDE.md; UI elements destroyed in `_destroy_ui`; FirstTimeTipOverlay wired with the `seen_first_listing_tip` flag. *Risk*: pygame_gui slider element may not exist — fall back to numeric input + step-button if needed; tests must not depend on slider-specific events. Touches: `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py` (NEW), `tests/test_views/test_sell_lot_view.py` (NEW).

7. **AuctionView player-seller integration (~2.5 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_player_listing.py::TestPreviewListingAffordances` (button renders/suppresses correctly per tier + venue); `TestActiveListingsSubsection` (renders one row per listing in `auction_state.active_listings`); `TestBidWindowPlayerSeller` (raise/hold/fold suppressed; "Your lot — watching" banner renders; AI bidders behave normally); `TestLotResolutionPlayerSeller` (sold and withdrawn message variants render correctly); `TestPostSessionPlayerListing` (Sable line renders when applicable, omitted otherwise). Then implement: extend `_create_ui_preview` to render the "List a Lot" button under the speed selector when `venue_id == "stellaris"` AND tier >= regular; extend `_render_body_preview` to render the "Active Listings" subsection; extend `_create_ui_bid_window` to suppress bid buttons when active lot is player-seller; extend `_render_body_bid_window` with the "Your lot — watching" banner; extend `_render_body_lot_resolution` for the sold/withdrawn message variants; extend `_render_body_post_session` to render Sable's `player_listing_post_session` line. Touches: `spacegame/views/auction_view.py`, `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_player_listing.py` (NEW).

8. **Engine wiring (`_prepare_stellaris_session` extension + `_ensure_sell_lot_view` + callbacks) (~2.5 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_player_listing.py::TestSessionPreparation` (prepare integrates eligible listings; player lots prepended; session size capped); `TestPlayerLotSoldCallback` (sold callback credits player, sets flag, fires journal); `TestPlayerLotWithdrawnCallback` (withdrawn callback returns item, sets flag, fires journal); `TestNewsTickerHighValueListing` (>= 50,000 credits emits headline; below threshold no headline); `TestSellLotRoutingFromAuction` (`GameState.AUCTION` → "List a Lot" button → `GameState.SELL_LOT` → confirm → back to `GameState.AUCTION`). Then implement: in `spacegame/engine/game.py`, add `_ensure_sell_lot_view` factory; route `GameState.SELL_LOT` from `_handle_state_transitions` mirroring the AUCTION block; in `_prepare_stellaris_session`, after `generate_lot_pool` and before persona attendance, call `state.eligible_listings_for_session(current_day)` and prepend converted player lots to `session_lots`; extend `_ensure_auction_view` with `on_player_lot_sold` and `on_player_lot_withdrawn` callbacks; news-ticker emit on hammer >= 50,000 anonymous headline. *Risk*: the existing `on_lot_won` callback fires for catalog lots too — verify the player-seller branch is `if lot.seller_id == "player"` not `if lot.venue == "stellaris"`. Touches: `spacegame/engine/game.py`, `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_player_listing.py` (NEW).

9. **Full scenario test (~1 hr).** Compose a deterministic regular-tier player at Stellaris → enters SellLotView → consigns 1 cargo commodity (declared 8,000 / reserve 0.70) → fee 400 deducted → next session → player lot first → AI buyers bid (seeded) → sells above reserve → credit + flag + journal fire. Second branch: regular-tier consigns second lot reserve 0.95 (above any AI ceiling at this catalog under the locked seed) → withdrawn → item returned → fee NOT refunded → withdrawal flag + journal fire. Assert all callbacks in correct order; assert achievement stub registered; assert no UI element leaks. *Risk*: AI persona ceilings depend on persona axes + lot category — pick a category in cargo (e.g., a high-value commodity) where the seeded ceilings cleanly straddle the two reserve targets. Touches: `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_player_listing.py`.

10. **Validation chain (~1 hr).** `ruff format` on touched files only (per `agent_principles.md:110` — never project-wide). `ruff check` on touched files. `mypy` on touched files. `pytest -n auto -q`. Confirm pass count >= 9780 and skip count == 98. SI-3 scanner clear. Writing Bible scanner clear. Dialogue-integrity scanner clear. If a pre-existing failure surfaces, note in Activity log; do not chase. Touches: validation only.

11. **Status flip to `review` + phase report + commit (~15 min).** Move `Status` to `review`. Append `**Last phase report.**` block (overwriting any prior phase report block in this section) per agent convention. Commit with `SA-B5: ...` prefix. Do NOT push. Touches: `requirements/roadmap/ROADMAP.md`.

**Decisions locked in this planning phase.**

1. **Listable items**: ship cargo (`current_cargo`) AND parts inventory (`parts_inventory`). NOT listable: `unlocked_modules` (boss-drop set per §11.10), `ground_equipment`, `salvage_hold_manager` contents, installed equipment.
2. **Listing venue**: Stellaris Auction House only. Crimson Reach player listings deferred to SA-B6.
3. **Session integration**: Player lots prepended to the next scheduled Stellaris session's `session_lots`; up to 3 per session per `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS`; AI buyer pool unchanged.
4. **Listing fee structure**: `fee = max(LISTING_FEE_FLOOR=100, int(declared_appraisal * LISTING_FEE_RATE=0.05))`; non-refundable in any outcome.
5. **Reserve range**: `reserve_pct` in [0.50, 0.95], default 0.70. Hidden from AI buyers (mirrors §5.4). Contraband listings out of scope for SA-B5.
6. **Listing slot limit**: `MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS = 3`. Cancellation frees a slot but does not refund the fee.
7. **Listing UI surface**: NEW `SellLotView` + `GameState.SELL_LOT`; accessed from AuctionView PREVIEW substate; routes back on confirm or back. NOT a modal; NOT a separate hub-card location.
8. **Velo's voice for player lots**: ceremonial register, seller-anonymized. New `consigned_lot_lines` voice key with 4 Velo templates + 1 SellLotView empty-inventory line.
9. **CaptainMemory effects**: NONE. AI rivals winning a player lot is positive-sum and does not record an outcome. Design doc §6.3 stays narrowly about adversarial encounters.
10. **Tier-gated entry**: Stellaris Port standing tier `>= "regular"` (rep >= 0). Sub-regular sees a tier-locked lock message; model-layer check too (defence in depth).
11. **First-time tip overlay**: NEW flag `seen_first_listing_tip`. Copy: "Listing fee is charged whether the lot sells or not. Set a reserve to refuse low bids."
12. **Polish fold-in scope**: 3 flag-gated journal entries + 3 banter trigger flags + 1 achievement stub (`auction_seller`) + 1 first-time tip overlay fold into SA-B5. Crew banter content (SA-X6), achievement metadata (SA-X7), per-venue visual identity (SA-X10), tutorial-pass refinement (SA-X3), cross-anchor narrative (SA-X1), reputation-balance (SA-X2), Reach player-listing variant (SA-B6) all stay in their own sprints.
13. **Test-file boundary**: SA-B5 adds 5 NEW test files. Does NOT modify SA-B2/B3/B4 test classes. Voice file is extended additively — SA-B3's existing keys untouched.

**Activity log.**

#### SA-B6 — Bidding polish + tuning

**Status**: done
**Phase**: Phase III | **Size**: M | **Effort**: 1 week
**Depends on**: SA-B3, SA-B4, SA-B5 | **Blocks**: SA-X1, SA-X2, SA-X4, SA-X5, SA-X6, SA-X7, SA-X8, SA-X9

**Goal.** Close out the SA-B2/B3/B4/B5 chain with a focused polish + tuning pass. Six concrete pieces of work: (1) author a Bidding tuning notes doc capturing the playtest signal and the resulting parameter adjustments; (2) ship the SA-B3 review's deferred post-session line rotation so the authored 3-option arrays in `stellaris_voices.json` actually surface across sessions; (3) extend Reach Salko post-session buckets from 1 line each to 2-3 each so the Floor Manager venue does not collapse to repeating the same Salko comment; (4) remove the unreachable `on_session_complete` delta-detection branch in `auction_view.update()` flagged by the SA-B2 review; (5) introduce `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` in `captain_memory.py` and wire symmetric player-win accumulation so a player who beats a named rival three sessions in a row auto-retires that rival to `STATUS_WANDERER` (the deferred §10.5 design-doc question, locked here); (6) run an accessibility audit of `auction_view.py` + `sell_lot_view.py` against `ui_design_standards.md` and document the result. SA-B6 also locks four open SA-B2/B5 decisions (Reach player-listing variant, listing draw weight, listing cancellation window, voice-key rename) with rationales rather than executing them, keeping the sprint at the M-sized boundary.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/sa_bidding_design.md` §4 (persona specs — tuning input), §5.2 (increment scale — tuning target), §10 (deferred items including §10.5 OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED, §10.8 balance tuning) per the §12 hand-off table line for SA-B6
- `requirements/agent_principles.md`

- `spacegame/views/auction_view.py` (REMOVE dead `on_session_complete` delta-detection block in `update()` flagged by SA-B2 review; ADD seed-rotation index in `_post_session_lines()` so `options[seed_index % len(options)]` is selected per session id; minor accessibility fixes per the audit if any surface)
- `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py` (minor accessibility fixes per the audit if any surface; do not refactor)
