- `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py` (extend `TestPostSessionLines` to assert each Salko bucket now contains 2-3 voice templates and that the rotation works across deterministic session ids)
- `tests/test_models/test_bidding_captain_memory.py` (NEW `TestOutcomeOutcompeted` class — single player-win records OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED, three accumulated wins auto-retire to STATUS_WANDERER, player loss DOES NOT increment the OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED counter, player abstention does not increment, mixed wins+losses against the same rival aggregate against `RESOLUTION_THRESHOLD = 3`)
- `tests/test_models/test_bidding_state.py` (extend with a player-wins-streak scenario asserting `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` flows through `_record_rival_outcomes`)
- `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py` (extend the structural-shape test to assert each Salko bucket has >= 2 options; voice-bible scanner runs unchanged over the new lines)

**Deliverables.**

- **Bidding tuning notes** — `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md` authored fresh. Sections: (1) methodology — how the implementer collected playtest signal (manual scenario walkthrough using the existing scenario tests as drivers, plus deterministic ceiling/aggression sweeps); (2) observed signal — concrete observations from running synthetic sessions across the 5 personas at 2 rep tiers and 2 venues, noting which lots feel under- or over-priced and which personas dominate or sit out; (3) recommended adjustments per category (persona ceiling_ratio, persona aggression, listing fee rate, Reach demand probability, min increment scale) — each recommendation paired with a one-line "applied" or "deferred to future sprint" decision; (4) applied changes — the actual constants edited, with before/after values; (5) skip list — categories examined and intentionally NOT adjusted, with the reason. The doc is the audit trail; the sprint's actual constant changes are the smaller, targeted edits in code.

- **Post-session line rotation (SA-B3 review's single tighten).** `auction_view._post_session_lines()` (lines 1101-1171) currently selects `options[0]` from each rival's bucket array. SA-B6 changes the selection to `options[_seed_index(session_id, rival_id, bucket) % len(options)]` where `_seed_index` is a module-local helper that hashes the three-tuple deterministically. Result: across sessions, each authored 3-option array surfaces all three lines over time; within a single session the rotation is deterministic so save/load reproduces the same line. Stellaris benefits immediately (its post-session buckets ship 3 options each); Reach benefits once deliverable below extends its buckets to 2-3 each.

- **Reach Salko post-session bucket expansion.** Each of the four Salko buckets in `crimson_reach_voices.json` (`rival_won`, `player_won`, `no_overlap`, `absent_retired`) currently ships 1 line. SA-B6 extends each to 2-3 lines so the rotation has material to draw from at Reach. The new lines are voice-checked against Salko's voice sheet (cold-grudge register, present-tense, brief, honorifics absent, theatrical reaction absent); against the 16-item diagnostic; and against the existing Reach voice file's tone (the buckets coexist with Vex Tarn's terse declarative auctioneer lines). Existing first-line content in each bucket is preserved as the `[0]` entry to keep deterministic prior behavior under any test that locked an exact line.

- **Dead-code removal (SA-B2 review's single tighten).** `auction_view.update()` contains an `on_session_complete` delta-detection block at roughly line 335 that fires when `len(st.session_history) > prior_history_count`. The SA-B2 review noted this branch is unreachable because `session_history` increments inside `handle_event` (via `_handle_button` → `advance_after_resolution`) before `update()` runs the next frame; the live `on_session_complete` callback is now driven from `_handle_button`'s `_maybe_fire_session_complete()`. SA-B6 removes the dead branch and its prior_history_count tracking, runs the existing tests to confirm the callback still fires from the active path, and adds an explicit test that asserts the callback is invoked from button handling, not from `update()`.

- **`OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` constant + symmetric rivalry resolution.** Per design-doc §10.5, SA-B6 introduces `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED = "outcompeted"` in `spacegame/models/captain_memory.py` next to `OUTCOME_OUTBID`. Behavior is identical to `OUTCOME_OUTBID` from the `record_encounter` perspective (increments `encounter_count`, updates `last_seen_day`, sets `last_outcome`, no immediate status transition; auto-retires to `STATUS_WANDERER` after `RESOLUTION_THRESHOLD = 3` accumulated entries). In `bidding.py::_record_rival_outcomes`, when the resolved lot's winner is the player AND a named rival was a recorded bidder on that lot, record `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` against that rival. Symmetric to the existing `OUTCOME_OUTBID` path; both share `RESOLUTION_THRESHOLD`. Player abstention (Hold/Fold) suppresses the entry on both sides.

- **Accessibility audit.** A targeted pass over `auction_view.py` and `sell_lot_view.py` against `ui_design_standards.md`'s compliance checklist plus a small additional gate-list: (a) keyboard navigation through the bid panel (Raise / Hold / Fold and the Speed selector are reachable via Tab order); (b) no critical information conveyed by color alone (the BID_WINDOW high-bid display reads the value as text; the speed selector reads the active speed via label, not just a highlight color); (c) the FirstTimeTipOverlay's body text is left-aligned within the panel and does not overflow at 1280×720 minimum resolution; (d) the speed selector's "slow" mode (45 s rounds, 5 s snipe) is concretely usable for slow-input players (verified by walking the existing scenario test at slow speed). Any concrete fixes that surface during the audit are folded into the auction_view / sell_lot_view edits; if no fixes surface, the audit's pass result is recorded in the tuning notes. Lock: SA-B6 does NOT add a "pause" or "auto-bid" affordance — those are scope creep beyond a tuning sprint.

- **Closed-out decision documentation.** Four open SA-B2/B5 questions are LOCKED in this planning phase as design decisions (NOT executed): (1) Reach player-listing variant — permanently deferred, not in scope; (2) listing draw-weight vs. deterministic prepend — deterministic prepend stays; (3) listing cancellation window — pre-session-only, the SA-B5 review's floor guard is the final state; (4) `velo_lines` → `auctioneer_lines` rename — alias is permanent infrastructure, not transitional. Each is recorded under the sprint's locked decisions section with a one-line rationale.

- **Tests** — 1 new test class in `test_bidding_captain_memory.py` (4-6 tests for OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED accumulation + auto-retire), 1 new test class in `test_auction_view.py` (3-4 tests for post-session rotation determinism + dead-code path removal), extension to `test_auction_view_reach.py` (1-2 tests for expanded Salko buckets), extension to `test_bidding_state.py` (1-2 tests for player-win-streak rivalry resolution), extension to `test_reach_voice_compliance.py` (1 test for bucket-shape >= 2 options). Approximate count: 10-15 new tests.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md` exists with the 5 sections specified (methodology / observed signal / recommended adjustments / applied changes / skip list). The applied-changes table lists each constant changed with before/after values; the skip-list table lists each category examined and explicitly not adjusted, with one-line rationale per row. Voice-checked against Writing Bible scanner over any player-facing copy stubs (the doc itself is internal and does not need scanner coverage, but any copy stubs it cites do).
2. `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED = "outcompeted"` constant is defined in `spacegame/models/captain_memory.py` adjacent to the existing `OUTCOME_OUTBID` constant. Constant value is the lowercase string `"outcompeted"` to match the existing convention. The existing valid-outcomes set (or any module-level allowlist) includes the new constant if such a set exists; otherwise the constant participates implicitly through `record_encounter`'s string-tolerant behavior.
3. Symmetric rivalry resolution: when `_record_rival_outcomes` fires on a `LOT_RESOLUTION` and `record.winner_id == "player"` AND a named rival placed at least one bid on that lot, exactly one `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` entry fires against that rival. Three accumulated `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` entries against the same rival auto-retire that captain to `STATUS_WANDERER` via the existing `record_encounter` threshold path. Player abstention (Hold or Fold) suppresses the entry. Mixed `OUTCOME_OUTBID` + `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` entries against the same rival both count toward the same `RESOLUTION_THRESHOLD = 3`. Verified by `tests/test_models/test_bidding_captain_memory.py::TestOutcomeOutcompeted` (single record, 3-record auto-retire, abstention suppression, mixed-outcome aggregation).
4. Post-session line rotation: `_post_session_lines()` selects from each rival's bucket array using a deterministic seed derived from `(session_id, rival_id, bucket_name)`. Across three distinct session ids against a fixture rival bucket containing 3 authored options, each authored option is selected at least once. Within a single session id, the rotation is stable: calling `_post_session_lines()` twice with the same session id returns the same selection. Verified by `tests/test_views/test_auction_view.py::TestPostSessionRotation`.
5. Reach Salko bucket expansion: each of the four `post_session.fenn_salko.{rival_won, player_won, no_overlap, absent_retired}` arrays in `crimson_reach_voices.json` has at least 2 entries (target 2-3 each). The first entry in each bucket matches the SA-B4 line byte-for-byte to preserve deterministic prior behavior in any test that captured an exact line. New lines pass the Writing Bible scanner and the 16-item diagnostic; sentence length stays consistent with Salko's voice sheet (no theatrical reaction, no honorifics, present tense, terse). Verified by `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py::test_salko_buckets_offer_variety` and the existing voice-bible scan.
6. Dead-code removal: the `on_session_complete` delta-detection block in `auction_view.update()` (around line 335 where `len(st.session_history) > prior_history_count` is checked) is removed along with its `prior_history_count` tracking. The `on_session_complete` callback continues to fire correctly via `_handle_button`'s `_maybe_fire_session_complete()`. A test asserts that draining a multi-lot session through `_handle_button` triggers the callback exactly once, while a parallel call into `update()` (no button event) does NOT trigger it.
7. Tuning adjustments are applied OR explicitly skipped. For each of the five categories (persona ceiling_ratio, persona aggression, listing fee rate, Reach demand probability, min increment scale), the tuning notes document either an applied change (constant edit + before/after value) or an explicit skip (with one-line rationale). At least one category is examined in detail in the methodology section, even if the conclusion is "no change". Existing scenario tests stay green under any applied changes.
8. Accessibility audit pass: the audit results are documented in the tuning notes' "applied changes" section under an "Accessibility" subsection. Concrete fixes (if any) are committed; if no fixes surface, the audit's pass-state is recorded explicitly. The audit checks: (a) Tab order through bid-panel buttons is sequential; (b) no critical info is color-only; (c) FirstTimeTipOverlay text fits within the panel at 1280×720; (d) "slow" speed setting completes a full scenario walkthrough deterministically. No new UI affordances are added; the audit is verification + small fixes only.
9. SA-B5 review's floor guard remains in place: `cancel_listing` still rejects cancellation once a listing is on the active session floor. SA-B6 explicitly does NOT extend the cancellation window (locked decision §B6.3); the existing `TestCancelListing::test_cancel_blocked_when_lot_on_active_floor` stays green.
10. All existing acceptance criteria from SA-B2 / SA-B3 / SA-B4 / SA-B5 stay satisfied. SA-B6 does NOT regress: bidding lifecycle, lot pool generation, persona value functions, save/load round-trip, journal/news/achievement firings, voice-bible compliance, dialogue-integrity scanner, SI-3 flag scanner all remain clean.
11. Pre-phase test baseline preserved: pass count >= 9881; skip count == 98; no new failures vs. baseline. New tests bring the count to roughly 9890-9900 passing. Touched-files-only `ruff format` + `ruff check` + `mypy` clean. Writing Bible scanner clean. Dialogue-integrity scanner clean. SI-3 flag scanner clean.
12. UI compliance against `ui_design_standards.md`: the auction-view + sell-lot-view audit's pass result is recorded; any `requirements/ui_design_standards.md` core-principle violations found during the audit are either fixed inline or filed as a follow-up item in the tuning notes (not as a new sprint, unless the violation is large enough to warrant one — in which case escalate via PHASE_BLOCKED).

**Plan.**

1. **Status flip + read-only context confirmation (~30 min).** Move `Status` from `in-progress (planning)` to `in-progress`. Re-verify: the existing `_post_session_lines()` at `auction_view.py:1101-1171`; the dead `on_session_complete` block in `auction_view.update()` around line 335; `crimson_reach_voices.json`'s 1-line Salko buckets; `OUTCOME_OUTBID` constant + `RESOLUTION_THRESHOLD = 3` in `captain_memory.py`; the `_record_rival_outcomes` integration point in `bidding.py`. Touches: read-only + one ROADMAP status edit. *Risk*: skipping risks the implementer authoring a parallel rotation helper or breaking SA-B3's deterministic post-session tests.

2. **Tuning notes scaffold + methodology section (~1 hr).** Author `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md` with the 5 section headers (methodology / observed signal / recommended adjustments / applied changes / skip list). Fill in the methodology: deterministic scenario walkthroughs as the playtest proxy (the existing scenario tests are deterministic and seeded; running them with logging at INFO level produces the per-lot sale prices, persona ceilings, and round counts the tuning analysis needs). Methodology states explicitly that SA-B6 does NOT introduce new lots, new personas, or new venues; tuning is restricted to constants and authored content additions. *Risk*: the tuning notes can balloon if the implementer treats it as a design doc rather than an audit trail; cap at 250 lines. Touches: `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md` (NEW).

3. **`OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` constant + symmetric rivalry resolution (~2.5 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: in `tests/test_models/test_bidding_captain_memory.py`, write `TestOutcomeOutcompeted::test_constant_value` (assert `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED == "outcompeted"`); `test_single_player_win_records_outcompeted` (player wins one lot a rival also bid on, exactly one entry fires against that rival); `test_three_wins_auto_retire_to_wanderer` (three accumulated wins against the same rival flip status to STATUS_WANDERER); `test_player_abstention_suppresses_entry` (Hold or Fold path does not fire); `test_mixed_outcomes_aggregate_against_threshold` (1 OUTBID + 2 OUTCOMPETED against the same rival → STATUS_WANDERER); `test_other_rival_unaffected` (only the rival who bid on the won lot accumulates; rivals who bid on different lots do not). Then implement: in `spacegame/models/captain_memory.py`, add `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED = "outcompeted"` adjacent to `OUTCOME_OUTBID`. In `spacegame/models/bidding.py::_record_rival_outcomes`, extend the existing per-lot loop: when `record.winner_id == "player"` AND `rival_id in record.rivals_bid` AND `record.player_bid is True`, call `captain_memory.record_encounter(rival_id, OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED, current_day)`. The existing `record_encounter` threshold fall-through handles the auto-retire. *Risk*: `record.player_bid` is the gate — abstention paths must not flip this true; verify the existing `record.player_bid` flag is true only when the player submitted at least one accepted bid (not just attended the lot). Touches: `spacegame/models/captain_memory.py`, `spacegame/models/bidding.py`, `tests/test_models/test_bidding_captain_memory.py`, `tests/test_models/test_bidding_state.py` (extend with one player-win-streak scenario).

4. **Post-session line rotation (~1.5 hr, TDD red-first).** Test first: in `tests/test_views/test_auction_view.py`, write `TestPostSessionRotation::test_rotation_deterministic_within_session` (same session id returns the same selection across two calls); `test_rotation_varies_across_sessions` (3 distinct session ids against a fixture 3-option bucket select all 3 distinct lines at least once across the runs); `test_existing_first_line_still_appears_for_session_zero` (the line at index `[0]` is reachable for at least one session id — preserves the SA-B3 deterministic first-render behavior for the canary case). Then implement: in `spacegame/views/auction_view.py::_post_session_lines()`, replace `lines.append(options[0])` with `lines.append(options[_seed_index(session_id, rival_id, bucket) % len(options)])`. Add `_seed_index` as a module-level helper hashing `f"{session_id}|{rival_id}|{bucket}"` into a non-negative int (e.g., `int(hashlib.sha1(token.encode()).hexdigest()[:8], 16)`). Apply the same pattern to the Sable bucket pick if SA-B3 surfaces a similar `[0]`-only path. *Risk*: a test elsewhere in the suite may have asserted an exact line returned from `_post_session_lines()` assuming the `[0]` index — search and update before commit; the rotation makes the [0] line still selectable for at least one session id. Touches: `spacegame/views/auction_view.py`, `tests/test_views/test_auction_view.py`.

5. **Reach Salko bucket expansion (~2 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py::test_salko_buckets_offer_variety` asserts each of the four Salko buckets has `len(options) >= 2`. Then author: in `data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json`, extend each Salko bucket with 1-2 additional lines. Voice-check each new line against Salko's voice sheet (`character_voices.md` lines 1368-1410), the 16-item diagnostic, and the Reach-rhythm cadence per `agent_principles.md:73`. Existing first-line content stays as the `[0]` entry. Run `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py` to confirm voice-bible scanner clean. *Risk*: drifting Salko's voice toward the institutional register or losing the cold-grudge tone in the new lines; copy a paired example from `aurelia_voice_examples.md` if drafting feels generic. Touches: `data/auctions/crimson_reach_voices.json`, `tests/test_data_integrity/test_reach_voice_compliance.py`, `tests/test_views/test_auction_view_reach.py` (extend `TestPostSessionLines`).

6. **Dead-code removal (~30 min, TDD red-first).** Test first: `tests/test_views/test_auction_view.py::TestSessionCompleteCallback::test_callback_fires_from_handle_button_only` (drive a multi-lot scenario through `_handle_button` events; assert `on_session_complete` is called exactly once; in a separate fixture, drive `update(dt)` with no event and confirm `on_session_complete` is NOT called when session_history doesn't change). Then implement: remove the dead `on_session_complete` delta-detection block in `auction_view.update()` (around line 335 — the `prior_history_count` setup and the conditional that fires the callback from update()). Verify the existing `_maybe_fire_session_complete()` continues to fire from `_handle_button` (this is the active path SA-B2 review installed). *Risk*: removing the prior_history_count tracking might also remove other adjacent state the update() relies on — keep the removal narrow and run the full test suite locally before committing. Touches: `spacegame/views/auction_view.py`, `tests/test_views/test_auction_view.py`.

7. **Accessibility audit + tuning notes accessibility section (~1.5 hr).** Audit `auction_view.py` and `sell_lot_view.py` against the `ui_design_standards.md` compliance checklist. Run the existing scenario tests at the four speed settings (`slow`, `normal`, `fast`, `asap`) and confirm each completes without UI element leaks or layout overflow at 1280×720 minimum. For Tab-order: verify that pygame_gui buttons in the bid panel are created in the visual order they should be tab-traversed (raise / hold / fold / speed). For color-only-info: search the `_render_*` methods for any branch that conveys state through color alone without a paired text label; confirm the high-bid display, the active-substate indicator, and the speed selector all read as text. Document the audit pass result in `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md` under the "Applied changes" section's "Accessibility" subsection. If any fix surfaces, commit it with the audit; if none do, record the pass-state explicitly. *Risk*: scope creep — the audit must NOT add a "pause" affordance, must NOT add new keyboard shortcuts, must NOT refactor the substate machinery. If a violation is large enough to warrant a new sprint, escalate via PHASE_BLOCKED rather than expanding here. Touches: `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md`; possibly minor edits to `spacegame/views/auction_view.py` / `spacegame/views/sell_lot_view.py`.

8. **Tuning analysis + targeted constant adjustments (~3 hr).** Run the existing scenario tests across the 5 personas at 2 rep tiers (regular, certified) and 2 venues (stellaris, crimson_reach), capturing per-lot sale prices and per-persona ceilings via INFO-level logging. Compare against the design-doc §4.1 expected behavior (Prentiss: small precise bids, Kade: list-only, Salko: vs-player escalation, Speculator: ambient room, Reach Buyer: contraband-aggressive). Record observations in the tuning notes' "Observed signal" section. For each of the 5 categories (persona ceiling_ratio, persona aggression, listing fee rate, Reach demand probability, min increment scale), record an "Applied" or "Skipped" decision with one-line rationale. Apply only the changes the playtest signal supports; if a category looks balanced, document it as "Skipped — within target range" and move on. Constant edits land in `spacegame/models/bidding_persona.py` (persona archetypes), `spacegame/config.py` (listing economics), `spacegame/models/bidding.py` (Reach demand constants), or `spacegame/models/bidding_round.py` (increment scale). *Risk*: tuning is judgment work — over-adjusting is the failure mode; default to "Skipped" with rationale rather than gratuitously editing constants. Touches: `requirements/sa_bidding_tuning_notes.md`; possibly `spacegame/models/bidding_persona.py`, `spacegame/config.py`, `spacegame/models/bidding.py`, `spacegame/models/bidding_round.py`.

9. **Validation chain (~1 hr).** `ruff format` on touched files only (per `agent_principles.md:110` — never project-wide). `ruff check` on touched files. `mypy` on touched files. `pytest -n auto -q`. Confirm pass count >= 9881 and skip count == 98. SI-3 scanner clean. Writing Bible scanner clean. Dialogue-integrity scanner clean. Reach voice-compliance scanner clean (the test file is extended this sprint; assert the new bucket-shape test passes alongside the existing ones). If a pre-existing failure surfaces, note in Activity log; do not chase. Touches: validation only.

10. **Status flip to `review` + phase report + commit (~15 min).** Move `Status` to `review`. Append `**Last phase report.**` block (overwriting any prior phase report block in this section). Commit with `SA-B6: ...` prefix. Do NOT push. Touches: `requirements/roadmap/ROADMAP.md`.

**Decisions locked in this planning phase.**

1. **§B6.1 Tuning sprint scope.** SA-B6 is restricted to (a) constant adjustments documented in the tuning notes; (b) authored-content additions to existing voice files; (c) the polish items called out in SA-B2/B3/B4/B5 review reports; (d) `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` for symmetric rivalry resolution; (e) the accessibility audit. SA-B6 does NOT add new lots, new personas, new venues, new view substates, new constants, or new persona archetypes. *Rationale*: keeps the M-sized boundary credible; the design-doc §10 deferred items list calls SA-B6 a "tuning + balance" sprint and SA-B3/B4/B5 already handled the heavyweight content/integration work.

2. **§B6.2 Reach player-listing variant — permanently deferred.** The SA-B5 §B5.2 deferral note flagged Reach player listings for "SA-B6 polish may consider adding... if playtest demands it." Locked: Reach player listings are NOT added in SA-B6 and are NOT proposed as a follow-up sprint. *Rationale*: the SA-B5 §B5.2 rationale ("Stellaris's calendar-based session schedule maps cleanly to listing scheduling; Reach's demand-driven cadence makes 'schedule a player lot' awkward; Reach's no-questions-asked culture is more naturally a buyer-side experience") survives playtest review — Reach as a buyer-side experience is the design intent, not a workaround. Forcing a consignment-side surface at Reach would require a Floor Manager voice for player-anonymous lots that does not exist and would dilute the Floor Manager's terse declarative register. Closed.

3. **§B6.3 Listing cancellation window — pre-session-only, no extension.** The SA-B5 OPEN-defer note flagged whether listings could be cancelled after preview opens but before the lot is called. The SA-B5 review's floor-guard fix (`cancel_listing` rejects cancellation once a listing is on the active floor) is the final cancellation contract. SA-B6 does NOT extend this window. *Rationale*: extending the window adds user-visible state to the AuctionView (a "Cancel listing" button mid-session) and complicates the pre-session-vs-active-session split; the listing fee is non-refundable in any outcome, so the window extension provides little player benefit. Pre-session cancellation is sufficient.

4. **§B6.4 Listing draw-weight — deterministic prepend stays.** The SA-B5 OPEN-defer note flagged whether player listings should compete with catalog lots for inclusion via a draw weight. Locked: deterministic prepend stays (the SA-B5 default). *Rationale*: predictability is more valuable than balance for the player-as-seller experience — a player who consigns a lot expecting it to appear in the next session should see it appear in the next session, not a session two cycles later because the draw weight rolled poorly. The 3-listing cap (`MAX_ACTIVE_LISTINGS = 3`) plus the existing session-size cap already prevents the player from dominating the session catalog.

5. **§B6.5 `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` — implemented for symmetric rivalry resolution.** Per design-doc §10.5, SA-B6 introduces the constant and wires the symmetric path: a player who beats a named rival three sessions in a row auto-retires that rival to `STATUS_WANDERER`, mirroring the existing `OUTCOME_OUTBID` accumulation against player losses. *Rationale*: without this, the auto-retire path is one-directional — losing 3 times to a rival retires them, but winning 3 times against them leaves the rival in `STATUS_ACTIVE` indefinitely. Asymmetric rivalry resolution feels punitive to skilled players; symmetric is the right fairness contract. The `record_encounter` threshold fall-through already covers the auto-retire transition without code changes — the new constant is purely additive. Mixed `OUTCOME_OUTBID` + `OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED` aggregate against the same `RESOLUTION_THRESHOLD = 3` so a rivalry that toggles between wins and losses still resolves at the natural threshold.

6. **§B6.6 Voice-key alias rename — alias is permanent infrastructure.** The SA-B4 §B4.6 lock flagged a possible rename of `velo_lines` → `auctioneer_lines` in `stellaris_voices.json` for cross-venue consistency. Locked: NOT executed. *Rationale*: the rename adds zero behavior change, breaks deterministic round-trip on existing test fixtures and saves, and the `auction_view._velo_running_commentary` alias fallback (`auctioneer_lines` first, `velo_lines` second) is small, well-contained, and durable. Future venues authored in the SA-B4 pattern use `auctioneer_lines` natively; SA-B3's Stellaris file keeps `velo_lines` as a stable read-only artifact.

7. **§B6.7 Reach demand cadence — tunable, not redesigned.** The SA-B4 locked decision §B4.4 set `REACH_DEMAND_PROBABILITY = 0.35` and `REACH_DEMAND_MAX_GAP_DAYS = 8`. SA-B6 may tune these constants per the tuning notes, but does NOT redesign the demand mechanic (e.g., does not introduce a deterministic schedule or a player-controllable cadence). *Rationale*: the irregular feel is a Reach culture commitment; redesigning it would re-litigate SA-B4's locked design.

8. **§B6.8 Reach season tags — not added.** The SA-B4 locked decision §B4.11 flagged that "SA-B6 may add Reach-specific demand-tied seasons after playtest." Locked: NOT added. *Rationale*: the Reach culture's irregularity is precisely the contrast with Stellaris's calendar-driven seasons; adding seasons at Reach erodes the venue contrast. If the tuning notes' analysis surfaces a need, the implementer can flag it as a follow-up; SA-B6 does not implement it speculatively.

9. **§B6.9 Single-tighten polish items.** Three review-flagged single-tightens are explicitly in scope: post-session line rotation (SA-B3 review), dead `on_session_complete` block in `auction_view.update()` (SA-B2 review), and Reach Salko bucket expansion (SA-B4 review). *Rationale*: these are concrete reviewer-flagged debt items already documented in the SA-B2/B3/B4 review reports; closing them is part of the polish-pass commitment.

10. **§B6.10 No new sprints proposed.** SA-B6 closes out the SA-B2-B5 chain. No follow-up sprint is created for any deferred SA-B item. The blocks list (SA-X1/X2/X4/X5/X6/X7/X8/X9) covers downstream Phase VI work that uses Bidding artefacts. *Rationale*: a tuning sprint that proposes new sprints undermines its own role as the close-out; the right time to propose a Phase VII follow-up (if ever) is when Phase VI has shipped and surfaced a need.

11. **§B6.11 Test-file discipline.** SA-B6 extends existing test files (`test_bidding_captain_memory.py`, `test_auction_view.py`, `test_auction_view_reach.py`, `test_bidding_state.py`, `test_reach_voice_compliance.py`) and does NOT modify SA-B2/B3/B4/B5 test classes — additive only. *Rationale*: matches the test-file discipline locked in SA-B3 §B3.10, SA-B4 §B4.13, and SA-B5 §B5.13.

12. **§B6.12 Tuning-notes voice register.** The tuning notes doc is internal — engineering audit trail, not player-facing — so it does NOT require Writing Bible scanner coverage on its own prose. Any player-facing copy stubs the doc cites (e.g., journal entry templates that the implementer adjusts) DO require scanner coverage. *Rationale*: the scanner is for player-visible prose; reviewer-scoped engineering docs are exempt.

**Risks / open questions.**

- **Tuning subjectivity**: tuning is a judgment call, and the synthetic scenario tests are a proxy for playtest, not a substitute. The mitigation is the "applied vs. skipped" discipline in the tuning notes — every category gets a recorded decision with rationale, and "Skipped — within target range" is an accepted outcome. If the implementer cannot reach a confident decision on a category after one walkthrough, the right call is to skip and document, not to over-tune.
- **OUTCOME_OUTCOMPETED test surface**: the path through `record_encounter` is shared with `OUTCOME_OUTBID`, so the threshold-based auto-retire is exercised by existing tests. SA-B6 just adds the symmetric direction. The risk is a tests-have-locked-the-asymmetry gap — search for any test that asserts only OUTBID can drive the auto-retire and update if needed.
- **Post-session rotation deterministic test stability**: the seed must produce different selections across distinct session ids without producing collisions in unit tests. Use a hashing scheme that produces ints with sufficient spread (sha1 hex truncation works; modulo by `len(options)` distributes across the bucket).
- **Reach Salko bucket voice drift**: extending each bucket from 1 line to 2-3 lines doubles the surface area for voice-bible drift. The mitigation is tight: each new line must match Salko's `character_voices.md` voice sheet (cold-grudge, terse, no honorifics, present-tense, "states a number") and must pass the voice-distinct test that asserts the Floor Manager and Salko sets do not collapse into each other.
- **Accessibility-audit scope creep**: an audit can balloon into "redesign the bid panel" scope. The mitigation is the locked decision §B6.1 (no new affordances) and the audit's pre-defined checklist. If a violation is large enough to warrant a redesign, escalate via PHASE_BLOCKED.
- **Constant tuning regression risk**: a persona ceiling_ratio nudge can cascade into the deterministic scenario tests' expected sale prices. The mitigation is to run the full suite after each constant edit; if a test fails because the expected sale price changed, decide whether to update the test's expectation (legitimate tuning effect) or revert the constant change (test was capturing the prior balanced state).

### Phase IV — Research Patronage

#### SA-R1 — Okafor Institute (Research Patronage)

**Status**: done
**Phase**: Phase IV | **Size**: L | **Effort**: 2-3 weeks
**Depends on**: SA-PREP-1, SA-C2 | **Blocks**: SA-R2, SA-R3

**Goal.** Convert the Okafor Institute Medical Wing `unique` location at Axiom Labs into a working Research Patronage venue. Dr. Nadia Kweon (the Institute's current director, voice sheet authored in SA-PREP-1 at `character_voices.md` lines 659-708) presents 10 funded-research project templates rotating on a window. The player commits credits, optionally adds collaborator researchers (team-fund) at higher cost / shorter duration, then game-day ticks advance the active project to a deterministic outcome — success unlocks a payout plus a Patent/IP holding the player can license (passive royalties) or sell (lump-sum); failure forfeits part of the capital. Three Institute-resident researcher NPCs (net-new this sprint) and the existing Patron crew specialist Nuri Solberg (SA-A2 voice sheet, SA-PREP-1 inventory) populate the team-fund roster. Industry skill `research_yield` and Leadership skill `research_oversight` (both shipped in SA-C2) stack with Nuri Solberg's crew bonuses through the standard `crew_roster.get_bonus(...) + progression.get_bonus(...)` pattern.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/station_anchors.md` (Phase IV — SA-R1 paragraph at lines 162-167; Cluster B context at lines 78-86; sequencing at 211)
- `requirements/character_voices.md` (Dr. Nadia Kweon sheet lines 659-708; Nuri Solberg sheet lines 1710-1757; SA-PREP-1 inventory at 575-650; founder lore at 509-520)
- `requirements/sa_skill_design.md` (sections 1.6 `research_yield` and 1.7 `research_oversight`; section 2 stacking convention; section 8 SA-R1 handoff item)
- `requirements/sa_crew_design.md` (Nuri Solberg `Patron` block; bonus types `research_yield_bonus`, `research_risk_reduction`)
- `requirements/investment_rewards_design.md` (sister concern — clarifies that the existing `axiom_labs_investment` basic-investment card stays in place; SA-R1 is a separate, deeper venue layered on the new `axiom_research_wing` location)
- `requirements/onboarding_design.md` (six teaching principles + PT-M FirstTimeTipOverlay pattern — used by the first-visit tutorial fold-in)
- `requirements/aurelia_voice_examples.md` (16-item diagnostic checklist; required reading before authoring Kweon / researcher / Nuri-as-collaborator dialogue)
- `data/galaxy/locations.json` (lines 280-285 — `axiom_research_wing` `unique` card; lines 287-292 — existing `axiom_labs_investment` basic-investment card that SA-R1 leaves untouched)
- `data/ships/upgrades.json` and `data/ships/modules.json` (research outcomes unlock specific module/upgrade entries; read the schema, do not modify in this sprint unless a new module entry is genuinely required for an outcome)
- `spacegame/views/wreckers_guild_view.py` and `spacegame/models/wreckers_guild.py` (SA-1 reference implementation: contract-board rendering, deterministic window seed, FirstTimeTipOverlay fold-in, save round-trip pattern, view <-> game.py integration)
- `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py` lines 97-104 (`UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS` dispatch table; SA-R1 adds the `axiom_research_wing` entry)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` lines 2368-2398 (`_ensure_wreckers_guild_view`, `_ensure_deep_shafts_view` factory pattern; SA-R1 adds `_ensure_okafor_view`)
- `spacegame/save_manager.py` lines 443-450 (`wreckers_guild_state` / `deep_shafts_state` save chain; SA-R1 adds `okafor_research_state`)
- `spacegame/models/progression.py` lines 920-929 (`research_oversight`) and 1234-1243 (`research_yield`) — SA-C2 wiring; SA-R1 reads these via `progression.get_bonus("research_yield_bonus")` + `progression.get_bonus("research_risk_reduction")`
- `tests/test_models/test_skill_wiring_integration.py` lines 661-662 (existing assertion that `research_yield_bonus` and `research_risk_reduction` are consumed by `okafor_view.py` — locks the view filename)
- `data/crew/crew_members.json` lines 801-827 (`nuri_solberg` crew entry; abilities `research_yield_bonus +0.10`, `research_risk_reduction +0.10`)
- `data/journal/entries.json` lines 200-226 (`auto_wreckers_*` flag-gated journal pattern; SA-R1 mirrors this with `auto_okafor_*` entries)
- `spacegame/constants/flags.py` lines 260-350 (Wreckers' Guild section as the per-venue helper template; SA-R1 adds an Okafor section)

**Touch zones.**
- `spacegame/views/okafor_view.py` (NEW — venue-named per `sa_skill_design.md` section 2 and the existing `test_skill_wiring_integration.py:661` assertion; do NOT use `research_view.py`)
- `spacegame/models/okafor_research.py` (NEW — project templates + `OkaforResearchState` runtime + Patent/IP holdings)
- `spacegame/models/player.py` (add `okafor_research_state: Optional[OkaforResearchState] = None` field)
- `spacegame/save_manager.py` (read/write `okafor_research_state` alongside `wreckers_guild_state` / `deep_shafts_state`)
- `spacegame/constants/flags.py` (NEW Okafor section — see Plan task 2)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` (`_ensure_okafor_view` factory; route `GameState.OKAFOR`; per-day game-day-tick hook to advance active projects + drain royalty payouts)
- `spacegame/config.py` (add `GameState.OKAFOR = "okafor"` — venue-named to mirror `WRECKERS_GUILD` / `DEEP_SHAFTS`)
- `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py` (extend `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS` map at lines 97-104 to route `axiom_research_wing` to `GameState.OKAFOR`)
- `data/research/projects.json` (NEW — 10 project templates) AND `spacegame/data_loader.py` (NEW `load_okafor_projects` parser)
- `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` (NEW Kweon trees: introduction, project-pitch, fund-confirmation, success-debrief, failure-debrief, IP-disposition; NEW dialogue trees for the 3 Institute researchers; NEW Nuri Solberg "as collaborator at Okafor" branches)
- `data/characters/npcs.json` (register `kweon_director` + 3 researcher speaker_ids; speaker_id registry for Nuri Solberg already exists from SA-A2)
- `data/journal/entries.json` (4 NEW entries: first project funded, first project completed, first project failed, first patent disposition)
- `requirements/character_voices.md` (append 3 new researcher voice sheets to the SA Arc section — folded in due to SA-PREP-1 coverage gap; see Decision 3 below)
- `tests/test_models/test_okafor_research.py` (NEW — project lifecycle, risk resolution, IP states, skill+crew bonus stacking)
- `tests/test_views/test_okafor_view.py` (NEW — view rendering, fund/team-fund flow, completion debrief)
- `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_okafor_arc.py` (NEW — full fund → tick → success arc + fund → tick → failure arc + IP license/sell branches)
- `tests/test_save_load/test_okafor_save_load.py` (NEW — save/load round-trip; legacy-save load defaults state to None)

**Deliverables.**
- New view (`OkaforView`) with a Kweon dock on the left, project-board panel listing 5-7 visible offers per visit (rolled deterministically from a 10-template pool against a 30-game-day window), and a separate "Active projects" panel listing the player's currently-funded projects with progress bars and an "Holdings" panel for completed Patents/IP.
- 10 research project templates spanning 3 risk tiers: 4× low-risk (5-10 day, 8-15k credits, modest payout, low failure odds), 4× mid-risk (10-20 day, 20-50k credits, larger payout, medium failure odds, some unlock module data), 2× high-risk (20-30 day, 50-150k credits, major payout, high failure odds, capstone unlocks). Each template names its consuming faction-flavor (Science Collective core; one Frontier Alliance medical-aid project; one Miners' Union industrial-medicine project) so faction-rep deltas on completion are non-uniform.
- Project-funding mechanic: solo-fund is the default; team-fund is an optional toggle that adds 0-2 collaborator slots from the team-fund roster (3 Institute researchers + Nuri Solberg crew if hired). Team-fund modifies the active project: cost ×1.5 per collaborator (capped at 2x with both), duration ×0.7 per collaborator (capped at 0.5 with both), success-payout split share routed to the Institute's relationship arc as +1 dialogue-flag tick per collaborator (`okafor_collaborator_share_<researcher_id>`). The math is documented inline in the model and the team-fund modal explains the trade-off in declarative copy.
- Risk dimension: each project carries a `base_failure_odds` (low: 0.05; mid: 0.18; high: 0.35). At resolution time, the failure check is deterministic: `rng = random.Random(f"{project_id}_{accept_game_day}_{player_seed}")` produces a single uniform value compared against `(base_failure_odds - research_risk_reduction_total)`. Save-scumming does not change the outcome (CLAUDE.md "Deterministic outcomes"). Failure refunds 30% of capital and emits a Kweon-voiced debrief; success pays the full success payout (modulated by `1.0 + research_yield_bonus_total`).
- Patent/IP dimension: every successful project produces a `PatentHolding` with three player-controllable states — `held` (default; no income; preserves the option), `licensed` (royalty: 5% of `success_payout` paid every 10 game-days while licensed; ends if licensed by another captain in a future expansion), `sold` (one-time lump-sum at 60% of `success_payout`; removes the holding). State transitions happen through Kweon's IP-disposition dialogue branch.
- Dr. Nadia Kweon as voiced NPC — Six dialogue branches authored against her voice sheet (lines 659-708): first-meeting introduction, project-pitch (one variant per visible project on the board), fund-confirmation (solo / team-fund variants), success-debrief, failure-debrief, IP-disposition. Voice register: institutional-fatigue-in-precise-disguise; passive constructions for institutional failures; founder's words quoted only when she needs the authority she does not personally feel.
- 3 net-new Institute researcher NPCs (team-fund roster) — voice sheets authored as part of this sprint and appended to `requirements/character_voices.md`'s SA Arc section. Each carries a distinct register: a methodology-rigorous clinical lead (different from Nuri's external-patron register), a hands-on bench engineer-scientist, a junior researcher whose perspective is shaped by graduating into the post-founder Institute. Each gets a small dialogue tree (greeting + project-context observation + sign-off), the same cast-variety pattern SA-1 used for Paz / Daro / Ife. **Names locked in during Plan task 4 by the implementer; banned-NPC-names list applies (`Yara, Elara, Kael, Mara, Lydia, Clive, Magnus, Ambrose`).**
- Nuri Solberg "as collaborator" dialogue branches — Nuri is already an authored crew specialist (SA-A2). At Okafor specifically she gains 2-3 short branches (greeting at the venue, post-project methodology debrief on the player's last completed project, refusal-to-collaborate branch when the player offers her a project she judges padded). Voice-checked against `character_voices.md` lines 1710-1757.
- Outcome unlocks: every mid- and high-risk project's success outcome unlocks something concrete from existing data — a specific `data/ships/modules.json` module, a specific `data/ships/upgrades.json` part, OR a market-availability flag for an existing commodity at axiom_labs. SA-R1 does NOT author new modules or commodities; outcomes consume entries already in the data.
- **[Polish fold-in #1]** First-time `OkaforView` entry fires a PT-M `FirstTimeTipOverlay` (one declarative sentence covering: project-board on right, Kweon's dock on left, fund commits credits and ticks game-days). Gated on `seen_okafor_tip` flag; never re-fires.
- **[Polish fold-in #2]** Four flag-gated journal entries in `data/journal/entries.json`: (a) first project funded (Kweon's voice acknowledging the patronage), (b) first successful completion (player reflection + Kweon debrief excerpt), (c) first project failure (Kweon's understated failure-debrief carrying the institutional weight), (d) first patent disposition (license OR sell — single entry triggered by either of two flags, written so the same body fits both paths). Voice register: Kweon's institutional-fatigue + player's matter-of-fact log.
- **[Polish fold-in #3]** Save/load round-trip: `Player.okafor_research_state` round-trips. Legacy saves with no `okafor_research_state` key load with `state = None` (no active projects, no holdings). Patent royalty schedule survives save/load.
- Tests: ~50 new tests across the four new test files; combined skill-bonus + crew-bonus stacking + deterministic-failure-resolution + IP-state-machine coverage; full happy-path scenario test plus failure-path scenario test plus IP-disposition scenario test.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. Player docks at Axiom Labs, clicks the Okafor Institute Medical Wing card; the unique-detail panel shows the existing "Enter" affordance (added by SA-1's station_hub_view extension); clicking it transitions to `GameState.OKAFOR` and the new view loads. Other unique cards still route correctly to their existing venues (no regression).
2. First entry to `OkaforView` fires the `FirstTimeTipOverlay` exactly once per save (gated on `seen_okafor_tip`). First conversation with Kweon sets `met_npc("kweon_director")` if not already set.
3. Project board renders 5-7 deterministic offers per visit. Re-entering on the same 30-day window produces the same offer set (deterministic seed `f"{game_day // 30}_{player_seed}_okafor"`); crossing the 30-day boundary forces a fresh roll. Already-funded projects are tracked separately in the "Active projects" panel and are NOT re-rolled.
4. Funding a project deducts credits, sets `OkaforResearchState.active_projects[project_id] = ActiveProject(accept_day, ...)` and decrements the available offer count for the current window so the same template cannot be funded twice in one window.
5. Game-day advance hook in `engine/game.py` (called wherever `Player.game_day` increments — see `Player.travel_to_system` line 478 and `Player.rest_at_station` line 501) ticks each active project; on `accept_day + duration_days`, the project resolves deterministically: failure check uses the seeded RNG against `(base_failure_odds - clamp(0, research_risk_reduction_total, base_failure_odds))`, success payout uses `success_payout * (1.0 + research_yield_bonus_total)`. Two saves of the same scenario produce identical outcomes.
6. Solo vs. team-fund are mechanically distinct and verified by parametric test: solo-fund of a 50k mid-tier project for 15 days yields baseline; team-fund with one collaborator yields cost 75k / duration 11 days; team-fund with two collaborators yields cost 100k / duration 8 days. Each collaborator slot sets `okafor_collaborator_share_<researcher_id>` on the project.
7. Skill stacking: `research_yield_bonus_total = crew_roster.get_bonus("research_yield_bonus") + progression.get_bonus("research_yield_bonus")` and `research_risk_reduction_total = crew_roster.get_bonus("research_risk_reduction") + progression.get_bonus("research_risk_reduction")`. With Nuri Solberg in the crew (+0.10 each) AND `research_yield` skill at level 2 AND `research_oversight` skill at level 2, total yield bonus is +0.20 and total risk reduction is +0.20. Verified by direct test against the integration in the view's resolution path.
8. Patent state machine: a successfully completed project produces a `PatentHolding` in `held` state. Player can transition to `licensed` (begins emitting `royalty_amount` credits every 10 game-days; verified by test that ticks 30 days and checks 3 royalty payouts) or `sold` (one-time payout at 60% of success payout, holding removed). Transitions go through Kweon's IP-disposition branch. `held` patents persist across saves; royalty schedule (next-payout-day) persists across saves.
9. Failure path: a project that resolves to failure refunds 30% of the original capital, sets the failure flag on the holdings list (no patent emitted), and triggers Kweon's failure-debrief dialogue exactly once for the FIRST failure (gated on `okafor_first_failure_seen`). Subsequent failures do not re-trigger the first-failure debrief but still emit the journal entry only on the first failure (entry deduped by trigger_flag).
10. Save/load: a save written with two active projects (one mid-risk, one high-risk), one held patent, one licensed patent (mid-cycle royalty schedule), and `kweon_relationship_value = 3` round-trips byte-clean for the SA-R1 fields. A legacy save with no `okafor_research_state` key loads as `state = None` (no errors).
11. Three new researcher NPC voice sheets are authored at the cast-variety standard (matching the Paz / Daro / Ife pattern from SA-1's R1 rework: Core voice, Verbal habits, Sample lines, distinct register). Names use no entries from the banned NPC list. Each researcher has a 3-node dialogue tree (greeting → project-context observation → sign-off) authored in `data/dialogue/dialogues.json`.
12. Voice check: every Kweon line, every researcher line, every Nuri-as-collaborator line, every project briefing string, every IP-disposition copy block, and every journal entry passes the Writing Bible scanner (zero em-dashes, zero `couldn't help but`, zero `a testament to`, zero `no X, no Y` parallel-negation, zero banned NPC names) AND matches the relevant voice sheet per the 16-item diagnostic in `aurelia_voice_examples.md`. Kweon's institutional-fatigue register is held distinct from Nuri's methodology-rigor register and from the 3 researcher registers.
13. The pre-existing `axiom_labs_investment` basic-investment card continues to work end-to-end against the SA-R1 changes (no regression on the Phase V investment surface; SA-R1 adds a separate venue, it does not absorb the investment card).
14. Four flag-gated journal entries trigger correctly on their respective flag sets (`okafor_project_funded_first`, `okafor_project_completed_first`, `okafor_project_failed_first`, `okafor_patent_disposed_first`). Each entry passes the Writing Bible scanner.
15. Full suite green; pass count ≥ 9897 (the baseline at sprint start); skip count == 98 (unchanged). Lint + format (touched files only) + mypy clean for touched modules. SI-3 flag-registry scanner clear. Writing Bible scanner clear.

**Risks / open questions.**
- ~~View / model file naming.~~ **LOCKED**: view is `spacegame/views/okafor_view.py`; model is `spacegame/models/okafor_research.py`; tests follow the same naming. *Rationale*: the SA-C2 design (`requirements/sa_skill_design.md` sections 1.6-1.7 and the bonus-naming table at 142-143) and the existing skill-wiring integration test (`tests/test_models/test_skill_wiring_integration.py:661-662`) both assert that `research_yield_bonus` and `research_risk_reduction` are consumed by `spacegame/views/okafor_view.py`. Renaming to `research_view.py` would break the assertion or force an off-sprint test edit. The current sprint's draft used `research_view.py` (incorrect); locked to `okafor_view.py`. Venue-named files mirror SA-1 (`wreckers_guild_view.py`) and SA-2 (`deep_shafts_view.py`).
- ~~`GameState` enum value.~~ **LOCKED**: `GameState.OKAFOR = "okafor"`. *Rationale*: mirrors `WRECKERS_GUILD = "wreckers_guild"` and `DEEP_SHAFTS = "deep_shafts"`. The current draft used `GameState.RESEARCH`, which is system-named rather than venue-named; SA-R1 is a venue and the established pattern is venue-named.
- ~~Sub-reputation organization for the Institute.~~ **LOCKED**: NO sub-reputation org for Okafor. The vision text at `station_anchors.md:165` and Kweon's voice sheet describe a relationship arc (Kweon's trust accumulates with funded projects), not a membership/tier system. *Rationale*: tier-based sub-rep belongs to organizations that gate access by member rank (Wreckers' Guild's apprentice/journeyman/master). The Institute admits any captain who funds; the relationship is a dialogue-and-flag arc, not a numbered tier. Use `kweon_relationship_value: int` on `OkaforResearchState` (incremented on success, decremented on failure-debrief, range 0-10 clamp) so dialogue gates can read it without a full sub-rep config.
- ~~3 Institute researcher voice sheets — SA-PREP-1 coverage gap.~~ **LOCKED**: SA-R1 authors the 3 researcher voice sheets and appends them to `requirements/character_voices.md`'s SA Arc section as part of this sprint. *Rationale*: SA-PREP-1's NPC inventory (lines 585-616) catalogued only Kweon and Nuri Solberg for SA-R1; the "3-5 researcher NPCs" line in `station_anchors.md:165` was not folded into SA-PREP-1's table. Two paths existed: (a) block SA-R1 with PHASE_BLOCKED for an SA-PREP-1 follow-up, (b) fold the authoring into SA-R1 with a touch-zone extension. (b) is selected because the missing characters are tightly scoped to this venue, the cohesion-cost of blocking would be high, and authoring 3 lighter sheets (4 sections each — Core voice, Verbal habits, Sample lines, Bonus context — matching the SA-1 secondary-contact pattern) is a 2-3 hour task that fits inside the sprint's L sizing. The researchers' speaker_ids and dialogue trees are net-new in SA-R1's scope already, so the voice work goes alongside.
- ~~Project pool size.~~ **LOCKED**: 10 templates (mid-point of the 8-12 vision range). *Rationale*: 10 is enough to keep the 30-day window's 5-7-card roll feeling fresh across multiple visits at any tier; 12 was tempting but extends authoring + voice-check time without proportional player benefit.
- ~~Risk resolution determinism.~~ **LOCKED**: deterministic seed `random.Random(f"{project_id}_{accept_game_day}_{player_seed}")` evaluated at completion-tick time. *Rationale*: CLAUDE.md "Gameplay Philosophy" — "Deterministic outcomes: ... NOT random rolls. No save scumming." The risk profile is the visible probability the player commits against; the actual outcome is determined the moment the player funds, but only revealed at completion. Save-and-reload before completion does not change the result. This matches Aurelia's existing market-price determinism convention.
- ~~Solo vs. team-fund cost / duration math.~~ **LOCKED**: per collaborator: cost ×1.5, duration ×0.7, capped at 2 collaborators (final caps: cost 2.0×, duration 0.5×). Each collaborator slot sets a per-researcher dialogue flag. *Rationale*: makes the trade-off legible (more collaborators = more capital up-front, faster turnaround) without producing a degenerate "always team-fund" outcome at every point in the credit curve. The per-researcher flag plumbs into SA-R2's narrative arc, where Kweon's dialogue can reference which Institute staff the player has worked with.
- ~~Outcome unlocks scope.~~ **LOCKED**: outcomes consume existing entries in `data/ships/modules.json`, `data/ships/upgrades.json`, and `data/economy/commodities.json` market-availability flags. SA-R1 does NOT author new modules / upgrades / commodities. *Rationale*: the vision line "Outcomes unlock unique modules" is best served by elevating existing under-used items (a long-tail of modules / upgrades exist with limited vendor exposure) into research-gated unlocks. New module authoring is its own design surface; hiding it inside SA-R1 would expand scope past L. If the implementer finds outcomes unavoidable that would require new entries, log `SA-R1-FOLLOW-1` and ship a placeholder unlock pointing at the closest existing entry.
- ~~Polish fold-in scope.~~ **LOCKED**: tutorial first-time tip overlay AND four flag-gated journal entries AND save/load round-trip fold into SA-R1. Crew banter (SA-X6), Patron-of-Research achievement (SA-X7), cross-anchor narrative threading (SA-X1), reputation-balance pass (SA-X2), per-venue visual identity (SA-X10), audio identity (SA-X9), tutorial-pass refinement (SA-X3), Dr. Okafor's legacy ethics arc (SA-R2), and polish-tuning (SA-R3) all stay in their own existing sprints. *Rationale*: the three folds are the minimum bar for the sprint to feel finished; everything else is explicitly the deliverable of a downstream sprint that already names SA-R1 as a dependency.
- ~~Existing `axiom_labs_investment` basic-investment card.~~ **LOCKED**: PRESERVE. SA-R1 does not modify the existing investment card or its config (if it has one in `data/economy/investment_configs.json`). *Rationale*: per `requirements/investment_rewards_design.md` line 40, basic-investment-card returns at 10 systems remain in scope for future iteration. SA-R1 layers a deeper Research Patronage venue at Axiom Labs alongside the existing investment card; both are visible in the station hub. The two systems are conceptually adjacent (both involve research and yield); they are mechanically separate.
- **OPEN — defer to implementation**: the team-fund collaborator UI (where the player picks 0-2 researchers from the team-fund roster). The lightest implementation is a modal with per-researcher checkboxes and the cost / duration math computed live as the player toggles. If the implementer hits friction making the modal feel non-broken (cost/duration values fail to update, checkbox states don't clear between visits, etc.), drop to a single radio button choice (solo vs. team-1 vs. team-2; team-fund picks the highest-relationship researcher automatically) and log `SA-R1-FOLLOW-2`. Not a planning blocker.

**Plan.**
1. **Status flip + read-only context confirmation (~30 min).** Move `Status` from `in-progress (planning)` to `in-progress`. Read Kweon's voice sheet end-to-end (lines 659-708) and Nuri's full sheet (lines 1710-1757). Confirm `axiom_research_wing` exists at `data/galaxy/locations.json:280`, that `axiom_labs_investment` exists at line 287 (and stays untouched), and that `nuri_solberg` exists at `data/crew/crew_members.json:801` with the two expected `bonus_type` strings. Re-verify SA-C2 wired both skills by reading `spacegame/models/progression.py` lines 920-929 and 1234-1243. Touches: read-only + one ROADMAP status edit. *Risk*: skipping this step risks the implementer wiring against the wrong filename / wrong bonus_type spelling and silently failing — `progression.get_bonus()` returns 0.0 for typoed bonus_type strings. Spend the 30 min.
2. **Add flag helpers in `spacegame/constants/flags.py` (~30 min).** New section "Okafor Institute (SA-R1)". Helpers (each with the producer/consumer cookbook docstring per `requirements/si3_flag_registry_cookbook.md`):
   - `seen_okafor_tip()` → `"seen_okafor_tip"` — first-visit overlay gate
   - `okafor_project_funded_first()` → `"okafor_project_funded_first"` — journal trigger
   - `okafor_project_completed_first()` → `"okafor_project_completed_first"` — journal trigger
   - `okafor_project_failed_first()` → `"okafor_project_failed_first"` — journal trigger
   - `okafor_patent_disposed_first()` → `"okafor_patent_disposed_first"` — journal trigger (license OR sell)
   - `okafor_first_failure_seen()` → `"okafor_first_failure_seen"` — Kweon's failure-debrief gate
   - `okafor_collaborator_share(researcher_id: str)` → `f"okafor_collaborator_share_{researcher_id}"` — SA-R2 hook
   *Risk*: SI-3 scanner will flag these as orphans until producers exist; do this BEFORE consumer code so the producer/consumer pairing lands in one sprint. Test surface: existing `tests/test_compliance/test_flag_registry.py`. Touches: `spacegame/constants/flags.py`.
3. **Author `spacegame/models/okafor_research.py` + tests (~5 hr, TDD-first).** Write `tests/test_models/test_okafor_research.py` first: project-template lookup, deterministic offer roll on a 30-day window, fund-flow (cost deduction, active-project insertion), tick-flow (game-day advance, completion-day resolution), deterministic failure resolution at all combinations of `base_failure_odds × research_risk_reduction_total`, success-payout multiplier with `research_yield_bonus_total`, team-fund cost/duration math at 0/1/2 collaborators, IP state machine (held → licensed → still-licensed-after-tick / held → sold → holding-removed), royalty payout cadence (every 10 game-days while licensed). Then implement: frozen `OkaforProjectTemplate` dataclass per Scanner B (CLAUDE.md cross-cutting); `OKAFOR_PROJECT_TEMPLATES: tuple[OkaforProjectTemplate, ...]` (10 entries — content authored in task 6); `PatentHolding` dataclass (project_id, state, success_payout, license_start_day, next_royalty_day); `OkaforResearchState` dataclass with `active_projects: dict[str, ActiveProject]`, `holdings: list[PatentHolding]`, `kweon_relationship_value: int`, `slot_seed_window: int`, `slot_offers: list[str]`, full `to_dict` / `from_dict` round-trip; module-level helpers `roll_offers`, `resolve_completion`, `tick_royalties`, `transition_patent`. Touches: `spacegame/models/okafor_research.py` (NEW), `tests/test_models/test_okafor_research.py` (NEW). *Risk*: forgetting `frozen=True` on the project template trips Scanner B; forgetting deterministic seeding makes the failure resolution non-reproducible across saves. Both covered by test.
4. **Author 3 Institute researcher voice sheets in `character_voices.md` (~2 hr).** Pick three names that pass the banned-NPC list (`Yara, Elara, Kael, Mara, Lydia, Clive, Magnus, Ambrose`). Each researcher fills a distinct register: (a) methodology-rigorous clinical-trial lead — different from Nuri Solberg's external-patron register (Nuri evaluates from outside; this person executes from inside), (b) hands-on bench engineer-scientist — concrete, equipment-aware, less abstract than Kweon's institutional voice, (c) junior researcher whose perspective is shaped by graduating into the post-founder Institute — knows the founder's reputation only through the building, not the person. Each sheet has 4 sections: Core voice, Verbal habits, Sample lines (≥3), and a Bonus context note tying the character to SA-R2's ethics arc as a hook for the next sprint. Append to `requirements/character_voices.md` SA Arc section. Voice-check before commit. *Risk*: writing 3 sheets in one sitting = AI-tell drift; re-read Kweon's sheet immediately before each new sheet and explicitly hold a different register. Touches: `requirements/character_voices.md`.
5. **Wire `OkaforResearchState` onto `Player` + save_manager + game-day-tick handler (~3 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_save_load/test_okafor_save_load.py` — write a save with two active projects + one held patent + one licensed patent + a non-zero relationship value and assert byte-clean round-trip; write a legacy-save fixture (no `okafor_research_state` key) and assert it loads as None. Implement: add `okafor_research_state: Optional[OkaforResearchState] = None` to `Player` (line ~84-86, alongside `wreckers_guild_state` and `deep_shafts_state`); update `save_manager.py` lines 443-450 to include the new state alongside the existing two. Add a `_tick_okafor_projects(player: Player)` method to `engine/game.py` invoked after `Player.travel_to_system` (line 478) and `Player.rest_at_station` (line 501) — the two existing game-day-advance call sites. The method: walks `state.active_projects`, resolves any whose `accept_day + duration_days <= player.game_day` via the deterministic seed, applies success/failure side-effects (credits, journal flag, holdings update); walks `state.holdings`, pays out royalties for any licensed patent whose `next_royalty_day <= player.game_day`. *Risk*: forgetting to handle the legacy-save case crashes loads — covered by test. *Risk*: tying the tick to one of the two call sites only would silently skip projects after the OTHER call site — handle BOTH. Touches: `spacegame/models/player.py`, `spacegame/save_manager.py`, `spacegame/engine/game.py`, `tests/test_save_load/test_okafor_save_load.py` (NEW).
6. **Add `GameState.OKAFOR` + view skeleton + station_hub_view dispatch (~5 hr).** Test first: `tests/test_views/test_okafor_view.py` covers view construction with synthetic state, `_create_ui` / `_destroy_ui` cleanup, project-board rendering at all 3 risk tiers, fund-flow (button press → credit deduction → state mutation → UI refresh), team-fund modal, completion debrief copy, IP-disposition flow (held → license → ticked royalty; held → sold → holding removed), failure-debrief copy. Implement: `GameState.OKAFOR = "okafor"` in `config.py`; `OkaforView(BaseView)` in `views/okafor_view.py` (standard view lifecycle: `on_enter` calls `super` and `_create_ui`; `on_exit` calls `super` and `_destroy_ui`). View layout: Kweon's dock left, project board (5-7 cards) right with risk-tier badges, "Active projects" panel below the board, "Holdings" panel below that. Register `_ensure_okafor_view` factory in `engine/game.py` (lazy-import per the existing factory pattern). In `views/station_hub_view.py`, add `"axiom_research_wing": GameState.OKAFOR` to `UNIQUE_HALL_TARGETS` at lines 97-104; the existing Enter-button infrastructure (added in SA-1) handles the rest. Add the `FirstTimeTipOverlay` fold-in as in SA-1's pattern. Author the 10 project templates' content (player-facing strings only — names, descriptions; the dataclass entries from task 3 are the schema). *Risk*: forgetting `super().on_enter()` / `super().on_exit()` (CLAUDE.md "Common Pitfalls"). *Risk*: the team-fund collaborator modal can become complex; if friction hits, fall back per the OPEN risk above. Touches: `spacegame/config.py`, `spacegame/engine/game.py`, `spacegame/views/okafor_view.py` (NEW), `spacegame/views/station_hub_view.py`, `spacegame/models/okafor_research.py` (extend with content), `tests/test_views/test_okafor_view.py` (NEW).
7. **Author 10 project templates in `data/research/projects.json` + the load path (~3 hr).** Test first: a parser test verifying all 10 projects load, risk tiers parse correctly, target unlocks reference real entries in `data/ships/modules.json` / `data/ships/upgrades.json` / `data/economy/commodities.json`. Implement: 10 templates split 4 low / 4 mid / 2 high. Each template's briefing, success-debrief, and failure-debrief strings are written in Kweon's voice (one block of authored copy per template). Faction-flavor distribution: ≥6 Science Collective, 1 Frontier Alliance medical-aid, 1 Miners' Union industrial-medicine, 1-2 cross-faction or Collective-internal. Add `load_okafor_projects` to `spacegame/data_loader.py` (mirrors `load_wreckers_contracts`'s structure if SA-1 shipped one; otherwise adapts the existing load pattern). *Risk*: outcome target unlocks fail their existence check at load time — covered by parser test. Touches: `data/research/projects.json` (NEW), `spacegame/data_loader.py`.
8. **Author Kweon + Nuri-as-collaborator + 3 researcher dialogue trees in `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` + register speaker_ids in `data/characters/npcs.json` (~5 hr).** Add 4 NPC entries to `data/characters/npcs.json`: `kweon_director` (faction_id `science_collective`, home_system_id `axiom_labs`), 3 researcher entries. Author Kweon's 6 dialogue branches (introduction, project-pitch, fund-confirmation, success-debrief, failure-debrief, IP-disposition). Author 3 short researcher trees (greeting → project-context observation → sign-off). Author 2-3 Nuri-as-collaborator branches at the venue. Voice-check end-to-end before commit: zero em-dashes, zero `couldn't help but`, zero `a testament to`, zero `no X, no Y` parallel-negation, zero banned NPC names, registers held distinct per the 16-item diagnostic in `aurelia_voice_examples.md`. *Risk*: voice drift across five distinct cast members in one sitting — re-read each voice sheet immediately before drafting that character's lines; do not draft all five in one pass. Touches: `data/dialogue/dialogues.json`, `data/characters/npcs.json`.
9. **Author 4 journal entries in `data/journal/entries.json` (~1 hr).** First project funded (Kweon acknowledging the patronage); first successful completion (Kweon's discovery-mode register surfaces briefly); first project failure (Kweon's understated failure-debrief register); first patent disposition (single body covering license OR sell; the player's own log voice). Each entry has a `trigger_flag` matching the helpers from task 2. Test surface: existing journal-trigger scanner + scenario test (task 10) asserts all 4 entries fire on the right flag. Voice check before commit. Touches: `data/journal/entries.json`, `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_okafor_arc.py` (extends task 10's scenario).
10. **Scenario test for full arc (~2 hr).** `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_okafor_arc.py` — full happy path (introduction → fund mid-risk → tick → success → IP licensed → 3 royalty payouts → IP sold → relationship value reflects all wins) + full failure path (fund high-risk → tick → failure → first-failure debrief fires once → second failure does not re-trigger debrief but does refund) + skill-stack scenario (Nuri Solberg crew + research_yield level 2 + research_oversight level 2 produces the locked +0.20 / +0.20 totals applied at resolution). Per-node Writing Bible compliance check on every authored string the scenario walks through. Touches: `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_okafor_arc.py` (NEW).
11. **Validation chain (~1 hr).** `ruff format` on touched files only (`AGENT_GUIDE.md` line 110 — never project-wide during a sprint). `ruff check` on touched files. `mypy spacegame/` for touched modules. `pytest -n auto -q`. Confirm pass count ≥ 9897 and skip count == 98. SI-3 scanner clear. Writing Bible scanner clear. If a pre-existing failure surfaces, note in Activity log; do not chase. Touches: validation only.
12. **Status flip to `review` + phase report + commit (~15 min).** Move `Status` to `review`. Append `**Last phase report.**` block (overwriting any prior phase report block in the SA-R1 section) per the agent convention. Commit with `SA-R1: ...` prefix. Do NOT push. Touches: `requirements/roadmap/ROADMAP.md`.

**Rework tasks (added by review, rework cycle 0).**
13. **Add NPC dialogue panel to OkaforView — follow the WreckersGuildView / DeepShaftsView pattern (~4 hr, TDD-first).** Both reference implementations cited in Context to read (`spacegame/views/wreckers_guild_view.py`, `spacegame/views/deep_shafts_view.py`) embed a per-NPC click panel: `_active_dialogue_tree`, `_active_dialogue_node_id`, `_dialogue_continue_button`, and per-contact buttons. OkaforView has none of these. All 11 authored dialogue trees (6 Kweon branches, 3 researcher trees, Nuri-as-collaborator) are unreachable from the view in the current implementation. Fix:
    a. Add NPC buttons to the Kweon dock area for at minimum Kweon plus the 3 researchers (Iris, Theo, Sana). Nuri's button renders only when she is in the crew roster (same as DeepShaftsView's crew-conditional rendering).
    b. Add `_active_dialogue_tree: Optional[DialogueTree]`, `_active_dialogue_node_id: Optional[str]`, `_dialogue_continue_button` state to OkaforView. Follow the WreckersGuildView create/destroy/handle_event pattern exactly.
    c. Route Kweon's button: first visit → `kweon_okafor_intro`; if `okafor_first_failure_seen` is True AND a new `okafor_failure_debrief_shown` flag is NOT set → `kweon_failure_debrief` (and set the shown flag on dismiss); otherwise → `kweon_okafor_intro` as the ambient greeting.
    d. Add `okafor_failure_debrief_shown()` helper to `spacegame/constants/flags.py` with producer/consumer cookbook docstring (producer: view's failure-debrief dismiss handler; consumer: view's Kweon routing guard). Register it as a KNOWN_PRODUCER_ONLY_ORPHAN until SA-R2 wires consumers.
    e. Fix `_failure_debrief_pending`: replace the dead-code attribute with the real check described in (c). The existing view test `test_first_failure_flag_recorded_on_view_open_after_tick` currently passes vacuously (the attribute is always False); rewrite it to assert `True` when `okafor_first_failure_seen` is set and `okafor_failure_debrief_shown` is not.
    f. Add dialogue-navigation tests in `tests/test_views/test_okafor_view.py`: Kweon button press opens the intro tree at `greeting` node; continue advances to the next node; reaching a `null` next_node_id closes the panel. Researcher button press opens that researcher's tree. `okafor_first_failure_seen=True` + `okafor_failure_debrief_shown=False` → Kweon button opens the failure-debrief tree. After dismiss, `okafor_failure_debrief_shown` is set.
    *Risk*: Forgetting to call `super().on_enter()` / `super().on_exit()` (CLAUDE.md "Common Pitfalls"). Forgetting to kill dialogue UI elements in `_destroy_ui()`. Both covered by the existing lifecycle tests. Touch zones: `spacegame/views/okafor_view.py`, `spacegame/constants/flags.py`, `tests/test_views/test_okafor_view.py`.

#### SA-R2 — Dr. Okafor's Legacy Narrative Arc

**Status**: done
**Phase**: Phase IV | **Size**: M | **Effort**: 1 week
**Depends on**: SA-R1 | **Blocks**: SA-R3

**Goal.** Author the long-running ethics-in-research thread that Dr. Nadia Kweon carries on top of SA-R1's project-patronage venue. Reveal the arc across project completions (not visit count) by tagging each of the 10 SA-R1 project templates as `heal` / `profit` / `neutral`-aligned and tracking running heal-vs-profit completion counts in `OkaforResearchState`. Six gated Kweon dialogue beats surface in `OkaforView` over the player's funding history (first-heal, first-profit, heal-pattern at 3, profit-pattern at 3, heal-ending at +5 spread, profit-ending at +5 spread). One optional Kweon-offered mission triggers off the heal-pattern beat and routes through the standard mission system. Founder's principle ("Knowledge that does not heal is knowledge wasted." — `character_voices.md:516`) is the central line the arc bends around. No new NPCs; no new view; no new mechanics — pure narrative extension on existing SA-R1 surface.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/station_anchors.md` lines 162-167 (Phase IV vision; SA-R2 paragraph at line 166: "ethics in research", "knowledge that heals vs. profits", "multi-step storyline surfacing across many visits", "dialogue gates tied to which projects the player chooses to fund", "~1 week of authoring")
- `requirements/character_voices.md` lines 509-520 (Director Okafor founder voice — "Knowledge that does not heal is knowledge wasted"; the standard subsequent voices are measured against), lines 659-708 (Dr. Nadia Kweon — institutional fatigue register, founder's-words-as-borrowed-authority pattern at 678, rare-warmth register at 692, never-says list at 695-700)
- `requirements/cultural_guide.md` (Aurelia ethics register — `passive constructions for institutional failure`; how Collective characters defend the institution in third person)

- `requirements/aurelia_voice_examples.md` (16-item diagnostic checklist — required before writing any Kweon arc line)
- `requirements/onboarding_design.md` (six teaching principles — used to keep the arc surfacing at the right cadence: progressive disclosure, character over UI, soft break into autonomy)
- `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` lines 9040-9117 (SA-R1 Kweon trees: `kweon_okafor_intro` at 9041, `kweon_success_debrief` at 9083, `kweon_failure_debrief` at 9101 — schema reference + register baseline; new arc trees mirror this format)
- `data/journal/entries.json` lines 228-254 (SA-R1 `auto_okafor_first_*` entries — voice + register baseline; new arc entries mirror this format)

- `spacegame/models/okafor_research.py` lines 67-110 (frozen `OkaforProjectTemplate` — SA-R2 does NOT add a field; ethics map is a separate module-level lookup), lines 717-805 (`OkaforResearchState` — SA-R2 extends with `legacy_heal_completed`, `legacy_profit_completed`, `legacy_ending` fields and round-trip in `to_dict` / `from_dict`)
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` lines 5148-5210 (`_tick_okafor_projects` — SA-R2 increments the legacy counters on the success branch at line 5190-5196 based on the project's ethics tag)

- `tests/test_save_load/test_okafor_save_load.py` (extend with: arc state round-trip — counters and `legacy_ending` survive save/load mid-arc; legacy-save fixture without these fields loads with defaults)
