### Phase V — Financial Exchange

#### SA-F1 — Financial Exchange Design

**Status**: done
**Phase**: Phase V | **Size**: M | **Effort**: 1 week
**Depends on**: SA-PREP-1, SA-C2, SA-V | **Blocks**: SA-F2

**Goal.** Author `requirements/sa_financial_design.md` as the single source of truth for the Financial Exchange arc (SA-F2 through SA-F7), modelled on the SA-P1 / SA-B1 design-doc precedents. The doc specifies: futures contract lifecycle and pricing (must feel honest against the existing `market.py` simulation), shipping contract structure (deadlines, route-quality bonuses, late penalties), insurance premium math (ship value × combat record × faction standing), market manipulation surface (extending the existing `GalaxyEvent` infrastructure), the financial-crisis event scope (decision lockable here per `station_anchors.md` open question), and concrete cross-system integration commitments to `market.py`, the existing `investment.py`, the SA-C2 `spread_trader` skill, the SA-A2 Brix Tano crew member, the SA-V Odom→Meridian graduation hand-off, and the SA-PREP-1 Ilse Vey voice sheet. The doc's hand-off map names exactly which sections each downstream sprint reads. No production code, no tests beyond docs validation; the deliverable is the doc.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/station_anchors.md` (Phase V section, lines 169-187 — the SA-F arc scope and integration commitments; the eight locked decisions section, lines 234-244, particularly decision 6 on staging and decision 8 on the cross-anchor mega-arc; open questions at lines 247-254, particularly the financial-crisis scripted-vs-generated question)
- `requirements/investment_rewards_design.md` (full — open threads listed at lines 19-25; sister-doc reference at line 40 confirming Phase V supersedes this stub for Meridian only, while the per-system passive investment cards remain in scope for future iteration)
- `requirements/sa_politics_design.md` (sections 1, 11, 12 — design-doc precedent: scope/relationship section, locked-decisions section, hand-off map)
- `requirements/sa_bidding_design.md` (sections 1, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 — design-doc precedent: scope/relationship, save/load contract template, tutorial/journal/news/banter/achievement stub pattern, deferred items, locked decisions, hand-off map)
- `requirements/character_voices.md` (lines 1470-1511 — Ilse Vey voice sheet, authored in SA-PREP-1; line 536 Odom + Ilse Vey relationship; line 503-505 SA-F3 graduation register guidance)
- `requirements/sa_skill_design.md` (sections 1, 2 — `spread_trader` Commerce Tier 2 skill, prereq `tariff_negotiation`, bonus_type `speculator_premium_reduction`, +0.05 per level; line 244 hand-off table to SA-F2/F3 view path)
- `spacegame/models/market.py` (full — `Market` class, `_calculate_price()` formula, `_get_supply_demand_modifier()`, `_get_random_variance()` deterministic seeding, `update_day()` lifecycle, `to_dict()` save contract, `add_politics_shift()` precedent for stacking shifts on top of base prices)
- `spacegame/models/investment.py` (lines 1-100 — `InvestmentTier`, `InvestmentTemplate`, the existing per-system passive-return investment system that Phase V leaves unchanged)
- `spacegame/models/galaxy_event.py` (full — `GalaxyEventType` enum, `GalaxyEvent` dataclass, `is_active()`, `to_dict()`/`from_dict()`, chaining via `chain_id`/`chain_step`; precedent for the SA-F6 manipulation-event extension)
- `spacegame/models/news_ticker.py` (existing news ticker; SA-F design specifies template stubs only, content authored in SA-X5)
- `data/galaxy/locations.json` (lines 33-39 — `nexus_financial_exchange` unique-card entry with Meridian Financial Exchange description; this is the venue SA-F3 surfaces)
- `data/dialogue/dialogues.json` (lines 1331-1340 — SA-V's Odom `graduation_pointer` node naming Ilse Vey at Meridian; the hand-off SA-F3 picks up; design doc must not change this)
- `data/crew/crew_members.json` (lines 774-800 — Brix Tano speculator crew, `futures_intel` + `speculator_premium_reduction` 0.10; design doc references this as the crew-side counterpart to the `spread_trader` skill)
- `requirements/onboarding_design.md` (six principles; section 9a "First-time system tips" overlay style for the SA-F arc tutorial stubs the design doc establishes)
- `requirements/aurelia_voice_examples.md` (16-item diagnostic for any sample player-facing strings the design doc proposes — journal templates, news headline templates, Ilse Vey sample lines)
- `requirements/agent_principles.md` (full — read once per phase; scope discipline, when-to-block-instead-of-guess, honest engineering depth)

**Touch zones.**
- `requirements/sa_financial_design.md` (NEW — the design doc, sections 1-12 per the SA-P1 / SA-B1 precedent)

**Deliverables.**
- `requirements/sa_financial_design.md` authored as the single source of truth for SA-F2 through SA-F7, organized into 12 sections matching the SA-P1 / SA-B1 design-doc precedent:
  - **Section 1** — Scope and relationship to existing systems. Names what SA-F adds, what it does NOT touch (the per-system `investment.py` cards remain in place; Meridian is a higher-tier parallel system, not a replacement). Module layout for SA-F2 through SA-F7 (`spacegame/models/futures.py`, `spacegame/models/shipping_contract.py`, `spacegame/models/insurance.py`, `spacegame/models/market_manipulation.py` extending `GalaxyEvent`, `spacegame/views/meridian_view.py`, `data/galaxy/manipulation_events.json`, `data/missions/shipping_contracts.json`, `data/missions/crisis_arc.json`).
  - **Section 2** — Futures contract lifecycle: offer (broker posts contracts at Meridian terminal) → preview (player can compare against current market price + price history) → accept (entry cost = strike price × quantity × spread; spread reduced by `speculator_premium_reduction`) → mature (deadline elapses) → settle (payoff = (actual market price at maturity − strike price) × quantity, signed by long/short direction) → ledger close (rep delta, journal entry, news ticker template fire if headliner contract).
  - **Section 3** — Futures pricing formula (concrete, not "TBD"). Strike price derived from `Market.get_price(commodity_id)` plus a forward-curve premium that reflects current market trend (`PriceHistory.get_trend()`), system-pair distance penalty (futures span systems), and contract duration spread (longer durations widen the spread). Worked example for at least three commodities at three duration tiers, showing the math line-by-line. Settlement uses the actual `Market.get_price()` at maturity day; no proxy, no projection (Decision 2 below).
  - **Section 4** — Shipping contract structure. Schema fields (`origin_system_id`, `destination_system_id`, `commodity_id`, `quantity`, `payout_credits`, `deadline_day`, `late_penalty_credits`, `route_waypoint_bonus_system_id`, `route_waypoint_bonus_credits`, `cancellation_penalty_credits`, `faction_id`, `prerequisite_standing_tier`). Acceptance flow, in-flight tracking on the Player save, late-delivery penalty cascade, route-quality bonus payout if the player visits the optional waypoint between origin and destination. Faction implications: shipping for Commerce Guild raises Commerce Guild standing; for Frontier Alliance raises Alliance standing; mixed-cargo shipments default to no faction delta.
  - **Section 5** — Insurance sub-system. Premium formula: `monthly_premium = ship_value × base_rate × combat_record_modifier × faction_standing_modifier`. Base rate, combat-record curve, faction-standing curve specified concretely. Deductible structure (player picks tier at policy purchase: low-premium-high-deductible, balanced, high-premium-low-deductible). Claim flow on ship destruction (game.py death/respawn integration: payout − deductible − recent-claims-modifier). Lapse rules (missed premium for 7 game-days lapses the policy; reinstatement requires a clean inspection check).
  - **Section 6** — Market manipulation event templates (6-8 concrete templates). Each template extends the existing `GalaxyEvent` infrastructure via a new `GalaxyEventType.MARKET_MANIPULATION` enum value or a new `manipulation_subtype` field; design doc picks the cleaner option. Templates: cartel_pump (single-commodity coordinated buy raises price 25-40% for 5-10 days), supply_line_sabotage (single-system production tag shutdown for 7-14 days, mirrors LABOR_STRIKE), false_news_pump (news-ticker-driven price spike that reverts after 3 days), short_squeeze (price spike triggered by player futures volume), regulatory_capture (Commerce Guild investigation halts trading at one system for N days), commodity_dumping (price collapse from external supply influx), insider_leak (the player can detect the manipulation early via `futures_intel` + Acumen check), counter_manipulation (player-initiated, requires Speculator skill + capital).
  - **Section 7** — Cross-system integration commitments (per `station_anchors.md` Phase V integration list). Concrete wiring for: Market (futures settle against real prices), Investment (Meridian is parallel, not replacement; per-system cards continue), Game-day cycle (deadlines tick on existing `update_day()` chain), Skill tree (`spread_trader` reduces spread, summed with crew), Crew (Brix Tano `futures_intel` reveals probability bands, `speculator_premium_reduction` 0.10 stacks with skill 0.10 to cap at -0.20), Reputation (Nexus Prime standing tiers gate maximum contract size, mirroring Stellaris-Port-tier from SA-B1 decision 8), Combat (insurance ties to ship-loss outcomes via `game.py` respawn path), News (ticker template stubs, content authored in SA-X5).
  - **Section 8** — Save/load contract. New `Player` fields with default-safe values for old saves: `futures_state: FuturesState` (active contracts, contract history), `shipping_contracts: list[ShippingContract]`, `insurance_policies: list[InsurancePolicy]`. Galaxy-level state: `manipulation_events: list[ManipulationEvent]` registered on the existing `GalaxyEventManager` (or equivalent). Each new field uses `data.get("...", default)` per CLAUDE.md migration conventions; the doc shows the literal `from_dict` snippets SA-F2/F4/F5/F6 implementers paste in.
  - **Section 9** — Tutorial / journal / news / crew banter / achievement stubs (mirrors SA-B1 sections 9.1-9.5). For each downstream sprint: the trigger flag name, the FirstTimeTipOverlay copy (one declarative sentence per the onboarding overlay style), 3-5 journal entry templates (in captain's-notebook register; voice-checked sample text included), 3-5 news ticker headline templates (under 80 characters), crew-banter trigger flag list (set in code, gated dialogue authored in SA-X6), and stub achievement IDs (authored in SA-X7). All sample player-facing strings pass the Writing Bible scanner before commit.
  - **Section 10** — Open items deferred to SA-F2 / SA-F4 / SA-F5 / SA-F6 / SA-F7. Items the design doc names but does not lock numerically (e.g., exact tuning of base insurance rate; exact persona-voice content for secondary Meridian brokers beyond Ilse Vey; UI dimensions of the contract terminal; sound design for settlement-win and contract-default audio; the exact threshold values that gate the financial-crisis trigger in SA-F7). Each item names which downstream sprint owns the resolution.
  - **Section 11** — Locked decisions (8-12 items, each with rationale and source citation). Pre-locked decisions enumerated in the Risks/open-questions section below.
  - **Section 12** — Hand-off map. Table of which downstream sprint reads which sections. SA-F2 reads 1-3, 7, 8, 9, 11; SA-F3 reads 1, 2, 7, 9, 11 + the Ilse Vey voice sheet; SA-F4 reads 1, 4, 7, 8, 9, 11; SA-F5 reads 1, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11; SA-F6 reads 1, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11 + the existing `galaxy_event.py`; SA-F7 reads 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 11; SA-X1/X3/X5/X6/X7 read section 9 only.
- The design doc cites every locked decision back to its source (`station_anchors.md` line N, `sa_skill_design.md` line N, `aurelia_voice_examples.md` item N, etc.) so downstream implementers can trace the rationale.
- Sample player-facing strings included in section 9 (journal templates, news headline templates, FirstTimeTipOverlay copy, two Ilse Vey contract-terminal sample lines) are voice-checked: zero em-dashes, zero double-hyphens, zero banned phrases ("couldn't help but", "a testament to"), zero parallel-negation rhetoric, zero banned NPC names, and they match the 16-item diagnostic in `aurelia_voice_examples.md`.
- One Activity log entry recording the post-sprint test pass count (>= 10268, the pre-phase baseline). Docs-only sprint, so no test count delta is expected.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. `requirements/sa_financial_design.md` exists with sections 1 through 12 matching the SA-P1 / SA-B1 design-doc precedent. Verified by direct file read; section headings present in order; no "TBD" / "TODO" / "section pending" placeholders in any section header or top-level paragraph.
2. Section 1 names the four new model files (`futures.py`, `shipping_contract.py`, `insurance.py`, `market_manipulation.py`), the new view file (`meridian_view.py`), the two new data files (`data/galaxy/manipulation_events.json`, `data/missions/shipping_contracts.json`), and the new `data/missions/crisis_arc.json` for SA-F7. Verified by string-match on each path in section 1.
3. Section 2 specifies the futures contract lifecycle in five phases (offer / preview / accept / mature / settle) and names the exact `Market.get_price()` call used at settlement. Verified by string-match on `Market.get_price` in section 2.
4. Section 3 contains a concrete pricing formula (no "TBD") plus at least three worked examples covering at least two duration tiers and two commodity classes (one production-tag commodity and one consumption-tag commodity). Verified by section-3 inspection: arithmetic in each example resolves to a single integer payoff; the formula references `_get_supply_demand_modifier`, `PriceHistory.get_trend`, and the `speculator_premium_reduction` bonus by exact name.
5. Section 4 specifies the shipping contract schema with at least the 12 fields listed in the deliverable (origin_system_id through prerequisite_standing_tier) and concretely defines the late-penalty cascade, the route-quality bonus mechanic, and the cancellation penalty rule. Verified by field-name string-match.
6. Section 5 specifies the insurance premium formula as a multiplicative product of ship_value, base_rate, combat_record_modifier, and faction_standing_modifier; gives a numeric base_rate (or numeric range with the locked-tuning-source citation); specifies the three deductible tiers; and walks the claim flow including the `game.py` respawn integration step. Verified by formula-presence inspection and the three-tier table.
7. Section 6 specifies 6-8 manipulation event templates each with: name, trigger condition, market effect (price modifier or block), duration_days, faction implications, player counter-mechanic with skill-check threshold. Verified by template count and field completeness in each template.
8. Section 7 specifies seven cross-system integration commitments (Market, Investment, Game-day cycle, Skill tree, Crew, Reputation, Combat, News) with the exact code path each touches (file:function or file:line where stable). Verified by string-match on each system name in section 7 headings.
9. Section 8 specifies the save/load contract for at least four new fields (`futures_state`, `shipping_contracts`, `insurance_policies`, plus the galaxy-level `manipulation_events` registry) and shows the literal `data.get("field", default)` pattern for each, matching the existing `Market.load_supply_demand` precedent. Verified by inspection.
10. Section 9 establishes downstream-sprint stubs: at least one FirstTimeTipOverlay flag and copy per sub-system (futures, shipping, insurance, manipulation), at least three journal entry templates per sub-system, at least three news ticker headline templates per sub-system, a crew-banter flag list, and stub achievement IDs (per the SA-B1 9.5 precedent). Verified by counts in section 9.
11. Section 10 lists deferred items, each tagged to the owning downstream sprint (SA-F2 / SA-F4 / SA-F5 / SA-F6 / SA-F7). At least eight items.
12. Section 11 lists 8-12 locked decisions, each with rationale and at least one source citation (file path + line number, or sister-doc decision number). Decisions cover: futures pricing data source (real vs simulated); contract granularity; insurance premium structure; market manipulation taxonomy; financial-crisis event scope (scripted vs generated); investment-rewards-design.md scope-fence; speculator skill + crew bonus stacking cap; reputation gating tier table; settlement window range; existing-system non-displacement rule.
13. Section 12 hand-off map names every downstream sprint (SA-F2 through SA-F7 + SA-X1 / X3 / X5 / X6 / X7) and the section list each reads. Verified by string-match on every sprint ID.
14. Voice check: every sample player-facing string in the design doc (journal templates, news templates, FirstTimeTipOverlay copy, sample broker lines) passes the Writing Bible scanner. Verified by running the scanner against the new doc file before commit.
15. Full test suite green; pass count >= 10268 (pre-phase baseline); skip count == 98 (unchanged). Lint / format clean on the new design doc (it is a markdown file; this means at minimum it's parseable by the rest of the docs pipeline). No code changes expected; if any are required, they are out of scope and surface as `PHASE_BLOCKED` per the AGENT_GUIDE escalation pattern.

**Risks / open questions.**
- ~~Futures pricing data source: real game-day market data, simulated projections, or both?~~ **LOCKED**: real market data only. Settlement uses `Market.get_price(commodity_id)` at the maturity day. No proxy, no synthetic projection. *Rationale*: the SA-F1 goal text explicitly says "must feel honest against existing market simulation"; the SA-F4 acceptance criterion 3 ("Contracts integrate with existing cargo/travel system without regression") implies the same coupling. A simulated projection would create a parallel pricing model that drifts from the actual market the player can observe at the trade terminal; the player would experience this as cheating. The design doc cites this back to `station_anchors.md:172`.
- ~~Contract granularity: per-(commodity, system) integer-unit, or batch-of-N-units, or fractional-unit?~~ **LOCKED**: per-(commodity, system) pair, integer-unit, with a minimum-quantity gate at the broker terminal (broker won't post a futures contract for fewer than M units). *Rationale*: matches existing `market.py` granularity (the market simulates per-commodity per-system prices, integer quantities); avoids fractional-unit bookkeeping; the minimum-quantity gate prevents trivial fishing contracts that would clutter the terminal UI.
- ~~Insurance premium calculation: flat % of ship value, tier-based (player-selected), or combat-record-modulated?~~ **LOCKED**: all three multiply together — `monthly_premium = ship_value × base_rate × combat_record_modifier × faction_standing_modifier`. The player's tier selection at policy purchase governs the deductible-vs-premium tradeoff; combat-record-modulated discount/penalty stacks on top. *Rationale*: matches the three integration commitments from `station_anchors.md` Phase V ("Tied to ship value, combat record, and faction standing"). A single-axis premium would underuse the existing combat record and faction standing systems; the multiplicative model gives all three axes weight without introducing additive complexity.
- ~~Financial-crisis event (SA-F7): scripted single-event, or generated under conditions?~~ **LOCKED**: generated under conditions, with one tutorial-scripted "first crisis" that fires regardless to teach the loop. The system can produce subsequent crises when (market volatility threshold + active manipulation events + player futures volume) converge per a numeric rule SA-F7 tunes. *Rationale*: `station_anchors.md:254` open question — "Recommendation lockable in SA-F1." Generated is the right call because (a) it lets SA-F7's authored scripted arc serve as the teaching beat without precluding a genuine repeating crisis loop, (b) the player's investment in futures + insurance + shipping creates a real systemic exposure that should be able to be cashed in or punished by an emergent crisis, (c) a one-shot scripted crisis would reduce the late-game financial system to "wait for the scripted event" rather than "navigate a system that can convulse."
- ~~Existing per-system investment cards (`investment.py`): rework, deprecate, or leave alone?~~ **LOCKED**: leave alone. Meridian is a higher-tier parallel system, not a replacement. The investment cards continue as the player's first taste of capital deployment (introduced via SA-V's `the_longer_ledger` mission); Meridian is the graduation tier where the same instinct gets exercised at scale. *Rationale*: `station_anchors.md` integration commitment "Existing investment is the lower tier; Meridian is the higher tier"; `investment_rewards_design.md:40` sister-doc note confirming Phase V supersedes that stub for Meridian only, not for the per-system cards. Reworking the lower tier is explicitly the future investment_rewards_design.md scope and out of SA-F1.
- ~~Speculator skill (`spread_trader`) + Brix Tano crew (`speculator_premium_reduction`): independent, additive, or capped?~~ **LOCKED**: additive with a -0.25 cap (skill max 0.10 + crew max 0.10 + edge cases like festival/event-driven discounts up to 0.05). *Rationale*: `sa_skill_design.md:312` confirms the skill `bonus_per_level` is the lower bound of the SA-A1 range; the SA-A1 stacking pattern is "skill + crew sum, then floor at the SA-A1 upper bound." The cap prevents degenerate stacking with future Speculator-themed crew.
- ~~Reputation gating: which faction's standing gates contract sizes, and what are the tier names?~~ **LOCKED**: Nexus Prime / Commerce Guild standing. Tiers mirror the Stellaris Port pattern from SA-B1 decision 8: apprentice (low max contract size) → regular (mid) → certified (high) → patron (no cap). *Rationale*: per `station_anchors.md` Phase V integration commitment "Nexus Prime standing gates contract sizes." The Stellaris-tier pattern is already established in this codebase (auction lot tiers), so the Meridian tier shape is recognizable to the player.
- ~~Settlement window: what's the contract duration range?~~ **LOCKED**: 7 to 21 game-days, player picks at offer-acceptance time. Longer durations widen the spread (broker takes more spread for longer-horizon risk). *Rationale*: matches the `market.py` PriceHistory window (`max_days = 7`) at the lower bound and the existing investment_rewards-style cycle at the upper bound. Shorter than 7 days means the player has no price history to compare against; longer than 21 days outpaces the market simulation's meaningful drift.
- ~~Market manipulation event taxonomy: extend `GalaxyEvent` or create a new event class?~~ **LOCKED**: extend `GalaxyEvent` via a new `GalaxyEventType.MARKET_MANIPULATION` enum value plus a `manipulation_subtype: str` field for sub-classification (cartel_pump, supply_line_sabotage, etc.). *Rationale*: `galaxy_event.py` already has chaining (`chain_id`/`chain_step`), duration tracking, system targeting, price modifiers, blocked commodities, and shutdown tags — every primitive a manipulation event needs. A separate event class would duplicate the lifecycle code and force `Market._calculate_price()` to handle two parallel event types. The subtype field keeps the JSON content authoring (SA-F6's `data/galaxy/manipulation_events.json`) clean without inventing a new class.
- ~~Investment_rewards_design.md open threads: which fold into SA-F, which stay in the stub doc?~~ **LOCKED**: ALL five open threads in `investment_rewards_design.md` (pacing, cross-system intersection, narrative beats, risk dimension, tier visibility) stay in the stub doc and are explicitly OUT of scope for SA-F. SA-F adds Meridian as a higher tier; it does not modify the lower-tier per-system investment cards. *Rationale*: `investment_rewards_design.md:40` confirms the sister-doc relationship — Phase V supersedes the stub for Meridian only. The stub doc's threads are about making the existing per-system investment more satisfying; SA-F is about building the higher tier on top. Conflating them would balloon SA-F's scope.
- **OPEN — defer to SA-F2 / SA-F5 / SA-F6 / SA-F7 numeric tuning**: exact base insurance rate (Section 5 specifies the formula and a recommended range; SA-F5 + SA-F6 polish-equivalent will tune); exact financial-crisis trigger thresholds (Section 11 names that the trigger uses market-volatility + active-manipulation-events + player-futures-volume; SA-F7 owns the numeric constants); UI panel dimensions for the Meridian contract terminal (SA-F3 owns); secondary-broker voice content beyond Ilse Vey (SA-F3 owns; voice sheets for additional Meridian brokers per `character_voices.md` standards). Not planning blockers.
- **OPEN — defer to implementation**: sample journal entry exact wording (Section 9 specifies templates and voice register; the implementer voice-checks each sample line end-to-end against the Writing Bible scanner during composition). Not a planning blocker.

**Plan.**
1. **Status flip + dependency-output verification (~30 min).** Status stays `in-progress (planning)` for this phase. Re-read the SA-V Odom→Meridian graduation pointer at `data/dialogue/dialogues.json:1331-1340` (must NOT be modified by SA-F1; the design doc only references it). Re-read Ilse Vey voice sheet at `character_voices.md:1470-1511` (authored in SA-PREP-1; SA-F3 will surface her in dialogue, SA-F1 cites her sample lines in section 9 templates). Re-read `sa_skill_design.md` lines 83-89 (`spread_trader` Commerce Tier 2, prereq `tariff_negotiation`, bonus_type `speculator_premium_reduction`, +0.05/level), line 141 (the SA-A1 hand-off table entry), and `data/crew/crew_members.json:774-800` (Brix Tano at nexus_prime, `futures_intel` 1.0 + `speculator_premium_reduction` 0.10). Re-read `spacegame/models/market.py` `_calculate_price()` (lines 180-244) and the `add_politics_shift` precedent (lines 587-598) for the stack-on-top-of-base-prices pattern. Re-read `spacegame/models/galaxy_event.py:1-120` for the extension-point fields. *Risk*: skipping this risks the design doc proposing module names or function calls that don't match the actual codebase; spend the 30 min.

2. **Author Section 1 — Scope, relationship, module layout (~45 min).** Open `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`, mirror the SA-B1 sections 1.1-1.6 structure. State explicitly: SA-F adds Meridian as a higher-tier parallel system; the existing `investment.py` and per-system investment cards are unchanged; `market.py` is read-only from SA-F's side except for futures settlement reading `get_price()`; `galaxy_event.py` is extended via a new event-type and subtype field. Module layout enumerates the eight new files (4 models + 1 view + 3 data files) with the owning sprint in parens. Cite station_anchors.md line numbers for each integration claim. *Risk*: drift from the SA-B1 structure makes downstream readers re-orient; mirror the precedent verbatim where it fits. Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md` (NEW).

3. **Author Section 2 + Section 3 — Futures lifecycle and pricing formula (~2 hr).** Section 2 gives the five-phase lifecycle (offer / preview / accept / mature / settle) with a state diagram in ASCII or markdown table form. Section 3 gives the pricing formula concretely: state the inputs (current `Market.get_price()`, `PriceHistory.get_trend()`, system-pair distance via galaxy graph hop count, contract duration in game-days, broker spread baseline, `speculator_premium_reduction` modifier from skill + crew); state the formula; show three worked examples — (a) short-duration future on a producing-system commodity (low spread, narrow expected delta), (b) mid-duration future on a consumption-system commodity (higher spread, wider delta), (c) long-duration future on a neutral-tag commodity (widest spread, dominated by variance). Each worked example resolves to a single integer payoff with arithmetic shown. Cite `market.py:_calculate_price` and `market.py:_get_supply_demand_modifier` by exact line for traceability. Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

4. **Author Section 4 — Shipping contract structure (~1.5 hr).** Schema fields (12 enumerated above). Acceptance flow: broker posts a contract pool tied to current market simulation (high-margin routes get more contracts; low-margin routes get fewer); player accepts, contract enters in-flight state on the Player save; deadline ticks down each `update_day`; on dock at destination, if `cargo[commodity_id] >= quantity` and `current_day <= deadline_day`, contract resolves with payout (+ optional waypoint bonus if the player visited the route_waypoint_bonus_system_id during transit). Late: payout reduced by per-day late_penalty_credits; very-late (deadline + 5) cancels the contract and applies cancellation_penalty_credits debit. Faction implication table specifies the faction-standing delta for each contract source. Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

5. **Author Section 5 — Insurance premium math (~1.5 hr).** Premium formula: state `monthly_premium = ship_value × base_rate × combat_record_modifier × faction_standing_modifier` with each multiplicand defined and a numeric base_rate (recommend 0.005 to 0.015 per game-month — exact tuning deferred to SA-F5). Combat-record modifier curve: zero ship losses in last 30 game-days = 0.85; 1-2 losses = 1.0; 3+ losses = 1.5. Faction-standing modifier curve: hostile = 1.5, neutral = 1.0, friendly+ = 0.85. Three deductible tiers: low-premium-high-deductible (premium × 0.7, deductible 25% of payout), balanced (premium × 1.0, deductible 10%), high-premium-low-deductible (premium × 1.4, deductible 0%). Claim flow: ship destruction triggers `game.py` respawn path; respawn path checks active policy; if active and deductible affordable, payout = (insured_value − deductible − recent_claims_modifier) credited to the player; recent_claims_modifier reduces payout for repeat claimants. Lapse rules: 7 game-days missed premium → policy lapses; reinstatement requires inspection check (Acumen difficulty 8). Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

6. **Author Section 6 — Market manipulation event templates (~2 hr).** Decide between `GalaxyEventType.MARKET_MANIPULATION` enum + `manipulation_subtype: str` field (per the locked decision in Risks). Author 8 templates: cartel_pump, supply_line_sabotage, false_news_pump, short_squeeze, regulatory_capture, commodity_dumping, insider_leak (player-side detect), counter_manipulation (player-side action). Each template is a structured block: name, trigger condition (which `GalaxyEventManager` rules can fire it), market effect (price modifier or block), duration_days range, faction implications (which faction's standing shifts), player counter-mechanic with skill-check threshold (`futures_intel` reveal at intel-rank N; `spread_trader` enables counter-trade; the appropriate Acumen-skill check difficulty). Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

7. **Author Section 7 + Section 8 — Cross-system integration and save/load contract (~2 hr).** Section 7 enumerates the seven integration commitments with concrete code paths: Market (settlement reads `Market.get_price`, no writes); Investment (no reads, no writes — Meridian is parallel); Game-day cycle (futures + shipping + insurance hook into the existing `update_day` chain via the GalaxyEventManager pattern); Skill tree (`progression.get_bonus("speculator_premium_reduction")`); Crew (`crew_roster.get_bonus("speculator_premium_reduction")` summed with skill, capped at -0.25); Reputation (`apply_reputation_with_spillover` for shipping payouts and futures settlement deltas); Combat (`game.py` respawn integration for insurance claims); News (`news_ticker.add_template_event` for headliner contract settlement and crisis events). Section 8 gives the save/load schema for `futures_state`, `shipping_contracts`, `insurance_policies`, and the galaxy-level `manipulation_events` registry, with `data.get("...", default)` snippets that SA-F2 / F4 / F5 / F6 implementers paste. Mirror the `Market.load_supply_demand` precedent (`market.py:638`). Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

8. **Author Section 9 — Tutorial / journal / news / banter / achievement stubs (~1.5 hr).** Mirror the SA-B1 9.1-9.5 structure precisely. For each sub-system (futures, shipping, insurance, manipulation), specify: one FirstTimeTipOverlay flag and one declarative-sentence overlay copy; three journal entry templates (in captain's-notebook register, voice-checked); three news ticker headline templates (under 80 characters, with substitution placeholders); a crew-banter trigger flag list (set in code by SA-F2/F4/F5/F6, banter authored in SA-X6); stub achievement IDs (authored in SA-X7). Voice-check every sample string against `aurelia_voice_examples.md` 16-item diagnostic AND the Writing Bible scanner before commit. *Risk*: voice drift toward corporate / mentor / mythic register — keep the Meridian-tone gatekeeping (Ilse Vey) distinct from the Odom transactional register and the Stellaris ceremonial-auctioneer register. Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

9. **Author Section 10 + Section 11 + Section 12 — Deferred items, locked decisions, hand-off map (~1.5 hr).** Section 10: enumerate 8-12 deferred items, each tagged to the owning downstream sprint. Section 11: enumerate 10 locked decisions (the 10 enumerated in the Risks section above), each with rationale and source citation (file path + line number, or sister-doc decision number). Section 12: hand-off table with rows for SA-F2 / F3 / F4 / F5 / F6 / F7 + SA-X1 / X3 / X5 / X6 / X7, columns for "sections to read" and "notes." Mirror the SA-B1 section-12 table structure. Touches: `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`.

10. **Voice-check + scanner pass + validation chain + sentinel (~1 hr).** Run the Writing Bible scanner (`pytest tests/test_writing_bible_compliance.py` if applicable, plus the dialogue-integrity scanner for any dialogue-style sample strings). Run `ruff check` only if the doc adds any code-block syntax that needs verification (markdown is not lint-checked, but if any python snippets in the doc were authored, sanity-check them). Run `pytest -n auto -q`; confirm pass count >= 10268 (pre-phase baseline) and skip count == 98 (unchanged). The doc is the only deliverable; no test changes expected. Append the `**Last phase report.**` block (overwriting any prior phase report) per the agent convention; append a final Activity log entry with the `PHASE_OK` sentinel. Commit as `ralph(plan): SA-F1 — planning complete` with a body summarizing decisions locked. Do NOT push.

#### SA-F2 — Futures Core

**Status**: blocked
**Phase**: Phase V | **Size**: XL | **Effort**: 2 weeks
**Depends on**: SA-F1 | **Blocks**: SA-F3, SA-F4, SA-F5, SA-F6, SA-F7

**Goal.** Implement futures contracts. Real market simulation work. Tests against price simulation.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/sa_financial_design.md`
- `spacegame/models/market.py`
- `spacegame/models/player.py` (game_day cycle)

**Touch zones.**
- `spacegame/models/futures.py` (NEW)
- `spacegame/models/player.py` (futures_state field)
- `spacegame/save_manager.py`
- `tests/test_models/test_futures.py` (NEW)
- `tests/test_scenarios/test_scenario_futures_loop.py` (NEW)

**Deliverables.**
- Futures contract data model.
- Pricing engine integrated with market simulation.
- Contract lifecycle (offer → accept → mature → settle).
- Settlement against actual market prices on maturity.
- Save/load support.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. Player accepts a futures contract; game-days advance; settlement resolves correctly.
2. Profitable contracts pay; losing contracts cost.
3. Pricing engine produces fair contract values across commodities.
4. Save/load preserves in-flight contracts.
5. 25+ new tests.

**Activity log.**

Includes Marcus Jin reactions to Deep Shafts visits, Malia Torres reactions to Wreckers' milestones, Cargo Broker reactions to Meridian success.

**Acceptance criteria.**

## Followups

Smaller deferred items pulled from prior sprint findings. Each is independently scopable; included here so the dispatcher has additional eligible work outside the SA arc.

### CB-1 — Crew Banter scope (scoping)

**Status**: done
**Source**: Living Universe Arc deferral; `requirements/living_universe_arc.md` Phase 5 design carries the as-imagined CB scope.
**Size**: S | **Effort**: 3-5 days
**Depends on**: none | **Blocks**: CB-2

**Goal.** Lock the scope of the CB Crew Banter sprint. CB has been deferred without architectural decisions; this sprint produces `requirements/cb_scope.md`, a design contract that tells CB-2 exactly what to build, how it relates to existing crew dialogue infrastructure, where it ends and SA-X6 begins, and what the authoring quota is. The doc is the planning artifact CB-2 plans against.

**Context to read.**
- `requirements/living_universe_arc.md` — Phase 5 (CB) section, lines ~793-940. The originally-designed CB architecture (BanterEntry, BanterTrigger, BanterEngine, Ship Log menu, sub-sprint breakdown CB-1 through CB-9). Treat as the maximalist starting point; CB-1 trims it to a shippable contract.
- `requirements/character_voices.md` — voice sheets for Elena, Marcus, Priya, Tomas. Sample entries authored in this sprint must pass these voice rules.
- `requirements/station_anchors.md` — SA-X6 description (line ~197) and the cohesion claim (line ~228, "Without crew reactions, the player navigates anchor systems alone"). Determines CB / SA-X6 boundary.
- `data/crew/ambient_dialogue.json` — 249 existing ambient lines across 24 distinct `crew_id`s (verified via `python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('data/crew/ambient_dialogue.json')); ..."` 2026-04-29). Contexts split: idle 93 / player_action 60 / inter_crew 52 / home_system 24 / faction_territory 20. Top-level key is `ambient_lines`. Note: the original sprint listed `data/crew/banter.json`, which does not exist; the existing crew-banter content lives in `ambient_dialogue.json` and is managed by `AmbientDialogueManager`. The absence of `banter.json` is itself a finding for the scope doc.
- `spacegame/models/ambient_dialogue.py` — `AmbientLine` (`@dataclass`, fields: `crew_id`, `text`, `context`, `system_id`, `faction_id`, `required_crew`, `min_loyalty`, `action_type`) and `AmbientDialogueManager` (selection: `get_line` / `get_random_idle` / `get_player_action_line`; "cooldown" is a single `_shown: set[int]` of indices, not time-based; save state via `to_dict` / `load_state`). The existing engine. Decision: extend it, or replace it.
- `spacegame/data_loader.py` — `load_ambient_dialogue()` at line 1496 and `ambient_lines` collection at line 91; CB-2's data wiring extends through here.
- `spacegame/engine/game.py` — points where ambient_dialogue fires (verified 2026-04-29): construction at lines 405-407 + 618-621; warp arrival home/faction lines at 1167-1191; player_action triggers at 4129-4138; save bind/load at 4685 and 4871-4872; idle counter at 5406-5413.
- `requirements/onboarding_design.md` — six-principle framing; banter must obey "voice-check everything" (principle 6).

**Touch zones.**
- `requirements/cb_scope.md` (NEW)

**Deliverables.**
- `requirements/cb_scope.md`, ~1000-1800 words, with the eight sections enumerated in the acceptance criteria.
- 3-5 sample banter entries (in the locked schema) embedded in the doc as voice-checked exemplars.

**Acceptance criteria.**
1. **Current Coverage section** enumerates crew banter that exists today: speaker x context tabulation derived from `data/crew/ambient_dialogue.json` (249 lines across 24 `crew_id`s and 5 contexts as of 2026-04-29), explicitly distinguishing the four primary crew (Elena, Marcus, Priya, Tomas) from the ~20 secondary specialists with 5-9 lines each. Plus a one-line description of `AmbientDialogueManager`'s selection / shown-once tracking / save-state semantics (note: existing "cooldown" is a `set[int]` of shown indices, not time-based). Include line-of-evidence pointers (file path, key class, key methods).
2. **Gap Analysis section** maps each Living Universe Arc Phase 5 trigger type — `destination`, `crew_pair`, `flag`, `combat_after`, `rival_seen`, `idle` — to existing coverage as **covered / partial / missing**, with a one-sentence justification each.
3. **Architecture Decision section** locks one of: (A) extend `AmbientDialogueManager` with new context types and trigger fields; (B) introduce `BanterEntry` / `BanterEngine` per Phase 5 design, deprecating `AmbientDialogueManager`; or (C) sister-system that shares the data file. Rationale must address save-migration cost, the 224 existing entries, scanner integration, and CB-2 implementation complexity.
4. **SA-X6 Boundary section** explicitly states whether SA-X6 is (a) a subset of CB-2 (anchor-specific trigger category), (b) a sibling sprint that runs after CB-2 ships infrastructure, or (c) merged into CB-2 with SA-X6 retired. The `data/crew/banter.json` reference in SA-X6's touch zones must be reconciled (renamed or annotated).
5. **CB-2 Authoring Quota section** specifies minimum entry counts per trigger type (e.g., destination >= 20, crew_pair >= 15, flag >= 10, combat_after >= 5, idle >= 10). Quota becomes CB-2's acceptance bar.
6. **Sample Entries section** includes 3-5 banter entries written in the locked schema, voice-checked against `requirements/character_voices.md` and the Writing Bible (no em-dashes, no banned phrases, no parallel-negation rhetoric). These exemplars prove the locked schema is workable in practice.
7. **Test Surface section** specifies which test files CB-2 will add or extend, and what each asserts (eligibility evaluation, cooldown, voice consistency, scanner coverage of new content file, speaker_id resolution, save round-trip).
8. **Recommendation section** picks one of: fold-into-SA-X6, run-parallel-with-SA-X6, defer-indefinitely. Includes a one-paragraph justification grounded in the architecture decision and the SA-X6 boundary.

**Plan.**

1. **Catalog current banter coverage.** Read `data/crew/ambient_dialogue.json` end-to-end. Tabulate by `crew_id` x `context`. The file holds 249 lines split across 5 contexts (idle 93 / player_action 60 / inter_crew 52 / home_system 24 / faction_territory 20) and 24 distinct `crew_id`s — primary crew (Elena Reeves 32, Marcus Jin 36, Priya Osei 30, Tomas Drifter 29) plus ~20 secondary specialists with 5-9 lines each. Confirm `station_chatter.json` is non-crew (NPC overheard / announcement / atmosphere) and `crew_members.json` is metadata only. Output: Current Coverage section.
   - Touches: read-only on `data/crew/*.json` and `spacegame/models/ambient_dialogue.py`. No tests at this step.
   - Risk: tabulation must match the file exactly. Use a small Python one-liner or the data_loader rather than eyeballing. The doc must surface the long-tail-crew dimension (24 ids, not just the 4 primary), so quotas and gap-mapping aren't blind to secondary specialists.

2. **Gap-map against Phase 5.** For each Living Universe Arc Phase 5 trigger type, classify existing coverage. Expected mapping: `destination` (partial, since `home_system` and `faction_territory` overlap, but per-destination weighting is missing), `crew_pair` (partial, since `inter_crew` exists but lines are single-speaker, not multi-line dialogues), `flag` (missing, no dialogue_flag conditions), `combat_after` (missing, no combat-recency trigger), `rival_seen` (missing, no rival/RC integration), `idle` (covered).
   - Touches: read-only.
   - Risk: don't over-catalog. The point is CB-2's gap surface, not a complete audit of every line.

3. **Lock architecture decision (extend vs. new vs. sister).** Recommended posture: option **A (extend)**. Rationale: zero save-migration cost; preserves 224 voice-checked lines; the existing manager already serializes shown_indices and integrates at warp/idle/player_action; new contexts are additive (`destination_system`, `crew_pair_dialogue`, `flag_triggered`, `combat_after`, `rival_zone`); the doc commits to extending `AmbientLine` with optional fields rather than introducing a parallel data model. Document trade-offs.
   - Touches: drafts the Architecture Decision section. Reads `spacegame/models/ambient_dialogue.py` and `spacegame/engine/game.py` to confirm extension feasibility.
   - Risk: option A constrains CB-2 to a single-speaker line model. If multi-line crew_pair dialogues are non-negotiable, option C (sister-system, shared data file) is the fallback. The doc must document this fallback and the trigger that would force the switch.

4. **Resolve SA-X6 boundary.** Re-read SA-X6 (line ~1520 in this file). Recommend: SA-X6 stays as a sibling sprint, but its touch zone `data/crew/banter.json` must be reconciled. Either rename to `data/crew/ambient_dialogue.json` (if option A locks) or update to whatever data file the locked architecture produces. The scoping doc surfaces this as a follow-up note for SA-X6's planner; CB-1 does NOT modify SA-X6.
   - Touches: drafts the SA-X6 Boundary section. Reads ROADMAP.md SA-X6 section read-only.
   - Risk: agent must NOT edit SA-X6. That's another sprint's section. Surface the reconciliation as a note in cb_scope.md.

5. **Set CB-2 authoring quota.** Recommended starting numbers: destination >= 20, crew_pair >= 15, flag >= 10, combat_after >= 5, idle >= 10 (total >= 60, matches Phase 5's "40-60 entries at launch"). Lock the per-category floors so CB-2 cannot ship 50 idle lines and call it done.
   - Touches: drafts the CB-2 Authoring Quota section.

6. **Write 3-5 sample banter entries.** One per crew member with at least one crew_pair dialogue. Each must pass character_voices.md (read voice sheets for tonal alignment) and the Writing Bible (no em-dashes, no "couldn't help but," no "no X, no Y," no banned NPC names). Include the entries in the doc with the locked schema's field structure visible.
   - Touches: drafts the Sample Entries section.
   - Risk: voice fidelity. Re-read each character's voice sheet sample lines before drafting. If any sample line would fail the writing bible scanner, it does not belong in the scope doc.

7. **Specify CB-2's test surface.** List the test files: `tests/test_models/test_ambient_dialogue.py` (extend with new context evaluation tests), `tests/test_writing_bible_compliance.py` (confirm the existing or new file is in the scan list), `tests/test_data/test_dialogue_integrity.py` (speaker_id and flag-id resolution tests for new entries), `tests/test_models/test_player.py` (save round-trip for any new state). Note that no NEW test file is needed if option A locks.
   - Touches: drafts the Test Surface section.

8. **Lock the recommendation.** Pick run-parallel as the default if architecture option A locks (CB-2 ships engine extensions and ~60 lines of general banter; SA-X6 reduces to ~25-40 lines of anchor-specific banter using CB-2's extended infrastructure). Defer fold-into-SA-X6 unless there's a strong cohesion argument; defer-indefinitely is rejected as it leaves the Phase 5 vision unimplemented.
   - Touches: drafts the Recommendation section.

9. **Compose `requirements/cb_scope.md` end-to-end.** ~1000-1800 words. Run a self-check against all 8 acceptance criteria before the agent moves to review. Voice-check the sample entries one more time.
   - Touches: writes `requirements/cb_scope.md` (NEW).
   - Risk: doc length sprawl. Each section should be tight. Bullets and short paragraphs over prose.

**Risks / open questions.**

The following decisions were locked during this planning phase. The implementer follows them; the reviewer can challenge them.

- ~~Should the missing `data/crew/banter.json` reference block planning?~~ **LOCKED**: no. The file's absence is the current state. The scoping doc records it as a finding and the existing content lives in `ambient_dialogue.json`. The original Context-to-read entry has been corrected.
- ~~Is the scoping doc just enumeration, or does it pre-lock architecture for CB-2?~~ **LOCKED**: pre-locks architecture. The doc IS the design contract for CB-2. Without architectural commitment, CB-2 will defer again.
- ~~Should the doc include sample entries, or only describe them?~~ **LOCKED**: sample entries required (3-5, voice-checked). Paper designs hide voice-fidelity risk; sample entries surface it.
- ~~Should CB-2 quotas be set in CB-1 or by CB-2's own planner?~~ **LOCKED**: set in CB-1, per-category. Without floor numbers, CB-2 can ship a tiny content drop and claim acceptance.
- ~~Is SA-X6 a CB-2 subset, sibling, or merge target?~~ **LOCKED**: sibling, with the scope doc surfacing the touch-zone reconciliation as a follow-up for SA-X6's planner. CB-2 ships infrastructure plus general banter; SA-X6 authors anchor-specific lines using that infrastructure.

Open question (reviewer judgment, not blocking implementation):
- Architecture preference is option A (extend `AmbientDialogueManager`). Implementer may flip to option C (sister-system, shared data file) if the multi-line crew_pair dialogue requirement turns out non-negotiable while writing the sample entries. The doc must document whichever option locks and the trade-off the implementer evaluated.
